IN THE CHAIR: ROBERTA METSOLA
President

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Pursuant to Rule 251(4) of the Rules of Procedure, I would like to inform you that there was no request to put to the vote the corrigendum announced in plenary yesterday at the opening of the sitting. The corrigendum is therefore deemed to have been approved.

脗聽

3. Presentation by the President-elect of the Commission of the College of Commissioners and its programme (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The first item on today's agenda is the presentation by the Commission President芒聙聭elect of the College of Commissioners and its programme.

Before I give the floor to the Commission脗聽President芒聙聭elect, I would like to draw attention to the commitment in the political guidelines for the next European Commission to swiftly revise the Framework Agreement between Parliament and the Commission. Discussions are ongoing. President von der Leyen and I have already agreed on the political principles on the revision of the Framework Agreement, and I look forward to completing this process swiftly, in the event of a positive outcome of our vote today.

I will now give the floor to the President芒聙聭elect, Ursula von der Leyen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ursula von der Leyen, President-elect Commission. 芒聙聯 President脗聽Metsola, honourable Members, before I present to you my new team, I would like to start by thanking the outgoing College. Five years ago, no one could have imagined what Europe would go through, and I could not have imagined a team as dedicated and tireless to go through that with. So I want to thank them from the bottom of my heart for their service to Europe, and I think I speak also in your name.

Honourable Members, when I stood here exactly five years ago, asking for your confidence, I spoke of the events of 1989, about the generation that returned freedom and democracy to the people of Europe and the courage of everyday people, like those in Bratislava, in Timi脠聶oara, who stood up for their liberty, about the leaders like Lech Wa脜聜脛聶sa and V脙隆clav Havel, who inspired entire countries and reunited an entire continent. I think back to the power in those images of 2脗聽million people holding hands across the Baltics, or those tasting freedom for the first time on the border between Hungary and Austria.

So it was with great emotion that I witnessed the moving events in Prague just ten days ago 芒聙聯 not just the people honouring and celebrating 35脗聽years since the Velvet Revolution, but also those mourners who gathered across town outside the Hl脙隆vka student dormitory, which is a place that symbolised what happened on that exact same day 85脗聽years before, the day Nazi troops murdered students, closed universities and curbed civil liberties.

I start with this because these stories incarnate Europe's eternal struggle and sacrifice for freedom and democracy. Because fighting for freedom connects us as Europeans 芒聙聯 our past and our present, our nations and our generations. For me, this is the raison d'脙陋tre of our Union and it remains a driving force more than ever today. This is the Europe I love, and this is the Europe my Commission will always be devoted to. Because I believe that our generation of Europeans must once again fight for freedom and sovereignty 芒聙聯 for the freedom that the people of Ukraine are heroically fighting for, for the freedom to shape our own future in a confrontational and unstable world.

But this freedom is not just an abstract word. It is about Europeans knowing that their families will be safe, their country protected, that they will be able to afford to buy food and heat their homes, with decent wages and fair prices, that they will be able to seize opportunities, and that they feel in control of the change 芒聙聯 and the speed of change 芒聙聯 happening in societies. Our fight for freedom may look different to generations past, but the stakes are just as high.

Honourable Members, these freedoms will not come for free. It will mean making difficult choices. It will mean massive investments in our security and our prosperity. Above all it will mean staying united and true to our values, finding ways to work with each other and overcoming fragmentation 芒聙聯 this is what I, and all the 26 women and men with me, will strive for every single day. We are ready to get to work immediately.

Honourable Members, you have scrutinised every Member of this College in the last week. You have tested their fitness for the jobs they will serve. I know this was not easy. But overcoming divisions and forging compromises is the hallmark of a vivid democracy, and my message today is we want to work with you in that spirit. We will be guided by the Political Guidelines this House voted on, and by the mission letters, in which I will address concerns that you have raised in the hearings. As I said before the vote in July, we will work with all democratic pro-European forces in this House, and as I have done in my first mandate, I will always work from the centre. Because we all want the best for Europeans, and we all want the best for Europe, and it is time to come together.

Honourable Members, this unity will be all the more important in today's contested world 芒聙聯 a world in which every weakness is weaponised, every division pounced upon, every dependency exploited. Our freedom and sovereignty depend more than ever on our economic strength. Our security depends on our ability to compete, to innovate and to produce. Our social model depends on a growing economy while facing demographic change. This is why I asked Mario Draghi to chart the way forward 芒聙聯 and his diagnosis was stark and his roadmap for action equally ambitious. Many of his proposals were taken up in our mission letters. Leaders endorsed this work in the European Council earlier this month. I have listened very carefully to the hearings. I've heard your repeated calls to deliver the substantial change he is putting forward, with speed, with ambition.

So I can announce that the first major initiative of the new Commission will be a 'Competitiveness Compass'. This Competitiveness Compass will be the frame for our work for the rest of the term. The Compass will be built on three pillars from the Draghi report. First, closing the innovation gap with the United States and China. Second, a joint plan for decarbonisation and competitiveness. Third, increasing security and reducing dependencies.

The first pillar is closing the innovation gap. Let me start with good news: Europe's global share of patent applications is on par with the United States and China. But 芒聙聯 and now comes the but 芒聙聯 only one third of these are commercially exploited. We are roughly as good as the United States at creating start-ups, but when it comes to scale-ups we are doing much worse than our competitors. So we have to close this innovation gap. So we will put research and innovation, science and technology at the heart of our economy. We will invest more, we will focus better, and we will ensure that our small companies, our start-ups and scale-ups, can thrive in Europe. We know what needs to be done. We all know that a start-up from California can expand and raise money all across the United States, but a start-up in Europe has to deal with 27 different national barriers. We need to make it easier to grow in Europe. For this and for many more reasons, the first-ever Commissioner for Start芒聙聭ups, Research and Innovation will be Ekaterina Zaharieva. Her leadership and experience will be invaluable to get more breakthrough tech from the lab to the market.

This is particularly important when it comes to digital tech. For us to be competitive, Europe must be home to the next wave of frontier technologies. I can think of no better person to lead on this as Executive Vice-President than Henna Virkkunen. You know her well. She will leave no stone unturned to ensure Europe can use digital technologies to boost its prosperity, to unleash innovation and to help keep people safer. She is the right person to ensure that Europe's tech sovereignty is built right here in Europe.

Honourable Members, the second pillar is the joint plan for decarbonisation and competitiveness. Almost five years ago we launched the European Green Deal 芒聙聯 our growth strategy and roadmap to net zero. The reasons which pushed us to be so ambitious then are even more pronounced today, so I want to be clear: we must and we will stay the course on the goals of the European Green Deal.

But if we want to be successful in this transition, we must be more agile and better accompany people and business along the way. We need to play on our traditional strengths 芒聙聯 our industries and SMEs, our innovators, our workers. This is why we will put forward the Clean Industrial Deal within the first 100脗聽days of this mandate. This will involve the entire College, and we will have a team working hand in hand to coordinate our proposal.

We will have Teresa Ribera, our first Executive Vice-President for Clean, Just and Competitive Transition. She's well equipped to ensure that we have a modern competition policy to support our ambitions. She is a true and devoted European, and together we will always work for the European interest.

We will have St脙漏phane S脙漏journ脙漏. You know him well in this Parliament. He will work tirelessly as Executive Vice-President to bring the industry and internal market dimensions together.

We will have Wopke Hoekstra. Wopke was our key coordinator, key negotiator at COP28 and COP29, and he is already leading the road to net zero and clean growth.

Together, they will ensure that we tackle the biggest challenges facing our companies, and we know that the most prominent of these is high energy prices. We have done a lot to respond to Russia's blackmail and to the high inflation that followed, but the price of energy is structurally still too high and has to go down. That is what Dan J脙赂rgensen will be working on, building on his previous experience. He will work on bringing down costs for households and companies, to invest in clean energy, and to replace Russian LNG imports. It is high time to make that happen, and I know this House will support us in this.

Honourable Members, each industry has different needs, and each sector will have its own individual path to be clean and competitive. This is why I am delighted that, to help make that happen, Apostolos Tzitzikostas will be in charge of transport and tourism. His vast experience of European regions will be invaluable to develop connectivity. To support this work, I have decided to convene a strategic dialogue on the future of the car industry in Europe. This dialogue and its follow-up will be under my leadership. We will bring together all stakeholders around the table, to listen to each other, and to design solutions together as this industry goes through a deep and very disruptive transition. Europe's car industry is a European pride. Millions of jobs depend on it, and together we have to make sure that the future of cars will continue to be made in Europe.

Honourable Members, the third and the last pillar is reinforcing our economic security. We know that overdependencies can quickly turn into vulnerabilities. This is why stable and secure supply chains are so vital. Critical raw materials are the most obvious example. The demand for critical raw minerals for the clean transition has already doubled during the last mandate, and it could triple during this mandate. So we need free and fair trade to diversify our suppliers. There is no one better suited to do this than Maro脜隆 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧. He will deliver more partnerships for trade and investments and also protect our economic security against the greatest risks to our strategic interests and sovereignty 芒聙聯 and we must go further.

President脗聽Niinist脙露's report on preparedness called for a systematic review of all our dependencies. To increase our resilience, he has laid out a whole-of-government and a whole-of-society approach. This is why I have asked Hadja Lahbib to work on crisis response and preparedness. Her work will be supported by HERA. Oliv脙漏r V脙隆rhelyi will lead our work on biotechnologies, and his first priority will be the Critical Medicines Act. Jessika Roswall will work on developing a competitive circular economy. This is crucial to give us more independence. We have paid the price for putting our future in the hands of a single supplier, and we will not make the same mistake again. European sovereignty is not for sale.

Honourable Members, war, conflict and human suffering continue unabated in our neighbourhood 芒聙聯 from Ukraine to the Middle East to parts of Africa. Europe must play a stronger role in all of these areas. We have a duty to help that destruction and loss come to an end. Strengthening our security is all the more important in a world as contested and fragile as it is today 芒聙聯 a world in which I believe Europe is needed more than ever. Just think of multilateralism and partnerships; sustainable development and humanitarian aid; security and human rights. They are more important than ever, and they will continue to be the linchpin of our external action. This will need a lot of work and tireless leadership. I know we can count on Kaja Kallas as our High Representative and Vice-President. She is the right person at the right time for Europe's diplomacy and interests in the world.

To work on sustainable development, international partnerships, and to work on investments via Global Gateway, I have chosen Jozef S脙颅kela. He will bring his business-like mentality and his experience to the table to develop the truly strategic approach that we need. We will need a special focus on the regions with which we are bound to by history and by geography, starting with our southern neighbourhood. I am delighted that Dubravka 脜聽uica will be our first ever Commissioner for the Mediterranean. We share the same shores, the same challenges and the same opportunities, and we know that we share the same destiny.

Honourable Members, these partnerships will gain importance in today's ever more dangerous world. War is raging at Europe's borders, and we must be ready for what lies ahead 芒聙聯 working hand in hand with NATO. Just one figure: Russia is spending up to 9脗聽% of its GDP for defence. Europe is spending on average 1.9脗聽%. There is something wrong in this equation. Our defence spending must increase. We need a single market for defence. We need to strengthen the defence industrial base. We need to improve our military mobility. We need common European projects on defence. This is why Andrius Kubilius will be the first European Commissioner for Defence. We have no time to waste, and we must be as ambitious as the threats are serious. So we will come forward with a white paper on the future of European defence in the first 100脗聽days of this mandate. I can assure you that Europe's security will always be this Commission's priority.

Honourable Members, this fight for freedom does not just apply to our 27 Member States. The dream of Europe extends to the Western Balkans, to Ukraine, to Moldova and beyond. We have seen the bravery of the people of Ukraine in pursuing their European journey. We have seen the resilience of the people of Moldova to stick to their European future. I have seen and we have seen the commitment of the Western Balkans to reform, to grow and to get ready to join the European Union.

At the same time, we have seen how Russia will stop at nothing to prevent them from having a European future. But, honourable Members, Europe's commitment to these countries will always be stronger. Let there be no doubt: we want Ukraine as part of our European Union. So, we will stand by Ukraine for as long as it takes. We will get ready with the reforms that we need on our side, and we will support these countries every step of the way on their merits-based process until they are ready to join our Union. Marta Kos 芒聙聯 with her vast experience 芒聙聯 is the right person for this job. The vision of a continent united by democracy, the rule of law and respect for fundamental freedoms will always drive our work. Because the people of these countries deserve a future of peace, progress and prosperity, and we will make sure that that future can be in Europe.

Honourable Members, these are the three pillars of our Competitiveness Compass: innovation, decarbonisation and security. They will all require a new approach to coordinating our policies, and they will all need a major boost of investment, of simplification and of skills.

First, on the question of financing, this will have to be an investment Commission. Of course, public investment will be crucial. We have all seen what the budget can deliver for Europeans 芒聙聯 whether for our citizens, our businesses, our researchers, our farmers, our communities and our regions. But our budget is often too complex 芒聙聯 funding the same thing in different programmes and in different ways. We must be much more focused on investing in our priorities. This is why I have put Piotr Serafin in charge of shaping our next MFF. He is a skilled negotiator. He will work with the Parliament and the Council to deliver a simpler, more focused and more responsive budget, and he will work to ensure that Europe has the means to deliver on its ambitions.

But public budgets can only go so far. We urgently need more private investment. Let me give you three figures. Business expenditure for research and development in Europe accounts for about 1.3脗聽% of GDP. That is compared to 1.9脗聽% in China and 2.4脗聽% in the United States 芒聙聯 business expenditure. So this private-capital gap is the main reason we lag behind on overall R&D spending, and thus on innovation. This is why we have proposed a European Savings and Investments Union 芒聙聯 a task I have entrusted to Maria Lu脙颅s Albuquerque. She will help ensure that European companies can find the capital they need here in Europe. With her experience, I know Maria Lu脙颅s is the right person for this job.

Honourable Members, for Europe to catch up, we will also need to make things easier for our companies. They are telling us that the regulatory burden weighs heavily on them: too much reporting, too many overlaps, too complex, too costly to comply with. We need to streamline our rules to reduce the burden on businesses, and we need to give legal certainty about what we expect from them. This is why I have asked one of our most experienced Commissioners, Valdis Dombrovskis, to take the lead on simplification and implementation. He will also be responsible for boosting Europe's economy and productivity. I can announce that one of our first steps in the new mandate will be a new omnibus legislation. We will look at different sectors and assess the European legislation. Our single market has always been the greatest engine for growth. This was the message delivered by Enrico Letta in his report. The greatest strength of our single market is that it replaces a myriad of national standards and customs with a single set of rules. So we need to get back to what the single market does best and make business easier. That's our task.

Honourable Members, the foundation of our economy is people: their hard work, their talents, their skills. Competitiveness today is less about the cost of labour 芒聙聯 that was 20脗聽years ago. But it's much more about the knowledge of workers, and this is where we can outmatch our competitors. Here in Europe, companies find incredibly experienced workers and top-notch tech graduates. But we do not have enough of them, and this is a handbrake on our ability to compete. For this reason, I have appointed as Executive Vice-President, to lead our work on skills and preparedness, Roxana M脙庐nzatu. She will ensure that we put people first, because productivity depends on good working conditions, on earning a fair wage, on a good work-life balance, on finding a nursery for your kids, and good care for your elderly parents, and on having access to decent and affordable homes.

Housing was one of the biggest issues for Europeans at the last election. It comes up time and again as the biggest concern for so many families. We need to tackle the housing crisis head-on. This is why for the first time we will have a Commissioner for Housing, and I am pleased that this Commissioner will be Dan J脙赂rgensen. This idea of putting people first has always been at the core of Europe's social market economy. We want strong social partners. The market can only function well if the social dialogue thrives too. That's our experience. That's our success. We need to make sure that we strengthen social rights in Europe. Roxana will lead on the ambitious social programme that we have laid out 芒聙聯 starting by updating the European Pillar of Social Rights. I know how important this is for you here in this House. So I want to say that I have heard your call, and I am happy to announce that social rights and quality jobs will be part of Roxana's title as Executive Vice-President.

Honourable Members, this is how we will uphold the unique quality of life of Europeans across Europe. Whether in a coastal or fishing community, in a rural area or farming community, on an island or in an outermost region, in a big city or in one of Europe's unique and diverse regions, we must work to tackle the issues that regions face 芒聙聯 from changing demographics, to climate change, to the need for modern infrastructure. This goes to the heart of the freedom I am talking about today. Because for many people, freedom is about choice 芒聙聯 choosing where to live, work and study. Choosing where to bring up their families 芒聙聯 in other parts of Europe or perhaps where they grew up. This is what Letta called 'the right to stay'. I want regions and communities to be in control of their own destiny and to help shape our policies. This is the task of cohesion and reforms that I have entrusted, as Executive Vice-President, to Raffaele Fitto. This is a choice I have made, also because I know how vital it is to give regions the political importance they deserve.

This is notably the case in our rural and coastal communities. Farmers and fishers give us the highest-quality food in the world. They bear the brunt of climate change more than anybody, and they deserve a level playing field and their hard work to be rewarded. We must ensure that our farmers and fishers have a fair and sufficient income. That they have the right incentives to help protect our nature. This is why I have chosen Christophe Hansen as Commissioner for Agriculture and Food. He will bring the same skills to find compromises as he always did in this House, and he will lead the follow-up of the Strategic Dialogue for the Future of Agriculture in Europe. I chose, as Commissioner for Fisheries and Oceans, Costas Kadis. His academic and professional expertise will help to deliver the right solutions. Both Christophe and Costas will ensure that our farming and fishing communities can thrive, just as they have for generations gone by. Europe will always be at their side, and so will be this Commission.

Honourable Members, since the start of my first mandate, I promised an approach to migration that is both fair and firm, that guarantees sovereignty and solidarity, with stricter rules but also guarantees for individual rights 芒聙聯 and we will work on opening up legal pathways. This approach is now at the core of the Pact for Migration and Asylum. Magnus Brunner is the right person to preserve this balance, and he will work every single day to secure our external border and bolster our internal security.

Honourable Members, the rights and freedoms we enjoy were gained slowly, generation after generation. We owe them to our freedom fighters who stood on a barricade, and trade unionists on a picket line; to women who raised their voice, and reformers who patiently lobbied for change; to mass mobilisations; and to lonely campaigners with the courage to challenge the status quo. It has taken decades to earn the freedoms we hold today. But we all know it can take a moment to lose them. This is why we must always be vigilant. I have tasked Michael McGrath to be a steadfast guardian of our values and our principles, and I have listened to our European Parliament and added consumer protection to his title. He will consolidate the tools for the rule of law we already have, and he will work to build new ones, including proposals on smart conditionality. He will make sure that the rule of law and justice for all remains at the heart of the European project, and he will strengthen equal application of all rule-of-law instruments. Our values are our foundation and our common bond, and they will always be our future.

Honourable Members, this College is the right team for this moment in Europe's history. I want to stress the word 芒聙聵team'. We will be a team with European citizens 芒聙聯 listening and learning more from the daily realities people face. We will also be a team with you and with the Member States. We know that your confidence will need to be continuously earned. Not just for today's vote but for the whole five years. This is why I have asked my Commissioners to be more present in this Plenary and in each of your committees, to engage more and be more transparent, and we will work to keep strengthening our partnership with this House 芒聙聯 starting with an ambitious reform of our Framework Agreement. You can count on me, and you can count on Maro脜隆 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧 and all Commissioners to deliver on that from day one. Together, we can work on an ambitious reform agenda in general that Europe needs. I believe we need Treaty change, where it can improve our Union, and I want to work on that with this House.

This team that you are voting on today is truly European. It represents different nations and generations. We have former prime ministers and ministers, mayors and local officials. Some have been CEOs and others have worked for non-profits. Some have been journalists and business owners; others are biologists and physicists by training. Some are from rural areas and farming stock, and others from the bright lights of our big cities. Some have lived through war; others through the transition to democracy. I am proud that this team is so diverse in so many ways. But we all know there is more work to do. As I promised you in July, I fought tooth and nail just to ensure we got to 11脗聽women in this College 芒聙聯 up from 5 when the first set of nominations came in 芒聙聯 and yet it is still not even half, and it is certainly not enough.

We have achieved so much in the last mandate. We have tackled the gender pay gap. We have taken European action on violence against women. We did what people said was impossible by unblocking women on boards. I want to thank all of you here 芒聙聯 women and men 芒聙聯 in this House for the cross-party support. We will never stop fighting for equality. This is why I am so glad to have Hadja Lahbib as our Commissioner for Equality. She has broken so many glass ceilings herself. She will strengthen women's rights and ensure equality for all. Together, we will work every day to bring different parts of our society together. I have chosen last, but certainly not least, Glenn Micallef 芒聙聯 born in the year when the Berlin Wall fell 芒聙聯 to be the bridge between generations. He will support our young people and ensure solidarity between people of all ages.

To conclude, honourable Members: freedom and democracy need to be nourished and fought for every single day, so we can pass them on to the next generation, just as we have inherited them from those before us.

Last week, Strasbourg marked a momentous anniversary: 80脗聽years since the day Strasbourg was liberated 芒聙聯 23脗聽November 1944. After five years of Nazi annexation, Strasbourg was finally free. It was a fresh start for the city and it was hope for Europe. But what followed wasn't easy. Europeans had fought each other for so long. It took a generation of leaders with the courage to mend the wounds, to imagine a different future.

This is the spirit that has led our continent forward ever since. Time and again, we have had a choice: on the one hand, the course of division and decline; on the other hand, the very narrow path towards a more united Europe.脗聽Europe has always chosen strength in unity.

So it is with honour and deep emotion that I stand here in Strasbourg 80 years on 芒聙聯 in this House of European democracy, at the heart of a free Europe, asking you for your trust in this team, asking you to choose a stronger and a more united Europe, asking you to choose a future of freedom for Europe. That path has never been the easiest one. But together, we know we can do it. So let's get to work.

Long live Europe. Vive l'Europe. Es lebe Europa.

(Loud and sustained applause)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Manfred Weber, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 President of the European Parliament, the European Commission, future Commissioners, today is an important day. The European Parliament has to vote on the new Commission 芒聙聯 I would call it our European government.

Parliament has a strong mandate. 180 million people, Europeans, participated in June's elections. We, all 720 Members, represent the political reality and, more importantly, the political will of the people of Europe, and today we have to vote for our Commission.

I am, since my first day in this Chamber, a proud Member of the European Parliament, and I thank the mothers and fathers of the Lisbon Treaty for giving us, as Parliament, such a strong role. In none of our Member States, the parliament is doing a similar hearing process as we do. Elected governments propose the candidates. The European Parliament finally votes for the next Commission. This means full democratic legitimacy for the new team. Let us be proud about this.

The new Commission also builds on the successes of the previous one, which delivered great results, as Ursula von der Leyen already said: remember Brexit, COVID, the war in Ukraine, the finalisation of the migration pact, the Green Deal. Sometimes we forget to speak about our positive results. That is why I also want to thank, on behalf of the European People's Party, all outgoing Commissioners and our Commission President for their work. Thank you so much. You made Europe stronger in the last five years.

Now we look forward, dear Ursula. You proposed a very balanced Commission 芒聙聯 geographically, politically 芒聙聯 and also focused on the main priorities. Especially, let me underline, that our 14 EPP Commissioners in the new team are also showing the best part of Europe; they are coming from all parts of Europe, different generations, teachers, mayors, governors, as Ursula von der Leyen already mentioned. So that's really representing Europe at its best.

The Commission enters office at a time of war in Europe. Global competition is intensifying. Rivals like China are challenging our industries and our economic model, the social market economy. We need actions now from this new team from day one.脗聽V脙隆clav Havel said, and I want to quote him, 'It is not enough to stare up the steps, we must step up the stairs'. Let's do what Havel told us. Let's do the first step now.

With the priorities on competitiveness, last week, the EPP Group invited the car industry 芒聙聯 seven CEOs from big European car manufacturers were there 芒聙聯 and the message, dear colleagues, is dramatic: thousands of jobs in Europe are, in the near future, at risk. 13 million in total 芒聙聯 7脗聽% of the GDP. A lot is at stake. That is one reality of today's Europe.

The second reality of Europe is the floods in Valencia, the floods in Hungary, in Austria, the fire in Portugal. That is why we have to implement the Green Deal. We have to do our ambitious approach, as it was agreed in the last period.

Dear colleagues, the historic responsibility of the next five years is to bring jobs, economic growth 芒聙聯 the social responsibility and the environmental responsibility together. That is the first step we have to do.

The second is we must keep Europeans safe. We will support Ukraine as long as it takes, no doubt about this. The next Commission must realise De Gasperi's dream of building up a European defence union, and we need safe borders.

And the third pillar is we have to protect the European way of life. What Europe does to make us to feel at home. And let's be honest, rising inflation, polarisation, fractures inside of our societies are threatening our democracies. We must keep our societies together. We must define quality of life, not only with economic figures, and we must make sure that nobody is left behind.

Dear colleagues, that is a challenge in front of us. The socialists, liberals and the European People's Party are the founding parties of today's Europe. Later, also, the Greens contributed a lot to the future of Europe, and even politicians like Thatcher from the conservative side strengthened Europe with inventing the single market. They fought for this idea of the single market. We all have a special responsibility.

And let me be on one point for the EPP crystal clear. The German AfD, for example, is asking to leave the European Union and the euro. The German AfD, Le Pen in France and Orb脙隆n in Hungary 芒聙聯 they are not just democratic competitors but political enemies for us as the EPP. I will not allow them to destroy my Europe I love so much. P脙漏ter Magyar against Orb脙隆n, Donald Tusk against Kaczynski 芒聙聯 the EPP is in the first row of defending what we believe in: pro-Europe, pro-Ukraine and pro-rule of law.

And I thank the socialists and the liberals that we agreed to put into the platform agreement, especially this formulation of the red line towards the extremists, as I said it, in this moment of time.

I want to thank my colleagues, Iratxe and Valerie, we have worked together in the last month already very intensively. I want to thank the Greens, Terry and Bas, for the constructive approach, but also Nicola, because with your support we achieved the two thirds majority.

We have different ideas, dear colleagues. We fight for our convictions but we all want to find compromises and serve all Europeans. Let us keep this spirit. Europe's best years are in front of us, dear colleagues. Let us vote now for the Commission and then let us start to work.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Iratxe Garc脙颅a P脙漏rez, en nombre del Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora presidenta von der Leyen, la nueva Comisi脙鲁n empezar脙隆 su mandato en un momento decisivo para el futuro del proyecto europeo, en un momento en el que est脙隆 en juego el progreso de nuestros ciudadanos, la supervivencia del planeta, la competitividad de nuestras empresas, nuestras capacidades de defensa y el liderazgo que queremos en el mundo.

Retos de enorme magnitud que tendremos que afrontar en un tiempo en el que la extrema derecha quiere desguazar el proyecto europeo, Trump amenaza con romper el v脙颅nculo transatl脙隆ntico, China ha hecho a脙卤icos las reglas comerciales y el mundo se debate entre la ley de los fuertes o las reglas del Derecho internacional, como consecuencia de la devastadora guerra de Putin contra Ucrania y de la matanza de Netanyahu en Gaza y en el L脙颅bano.

Y, en este contexto de riesgo, hay quienes han socavado el prestigio de la instituci脙鲁n y han expuesto a la Uni脙鲁n a una crisis de gobernabilidad. El s脙颅 de la familia socialdem脙鲁crata es un s脙颅 para seguir avanzando. Es un s脙颅 al nuevo Colegio de Comisarios responsable, un s脙颅 constructivo, un s脙颅 por la estabilidad europea, pero tengan claro que no es un cheque en blanco.

Se脙卤ora von der Leyen, se脙卤or Weber, las fuerzas europe脙颅stas que hemos construido la Uni脙鲁n desde su fundaci脙鲁n tenemos un acuerdo. Y los acuerdos se cumplen. Sean fieles a la tradici脙鲁n europe脙颅sta y reafirmen su compromiso con la mayor脙颅a proeuropea.

Insisto, no aceptaremos un doble juego. Porque no se puede construir Europa con quienes quieren destruirla. Porque es inmoral construir Europa con quienes impulsan una agenda de supresi脙鲁n de los derechos conquistados, con quienes niegan el cambio clim脙隆tico y dan marcha atr脙隆s en las pol脙颅ticas de igualdad, de medio ambiente o de justicia social. Porque es imposible construir Europa con quienes rechazan el proceso de integraci脙鲁n y quieren reducirla a un simple mercado interior, sin las pol脙颅ticas comunes y sin avances en pol脙颅ticas de defensa y exteriores.

Frente al relato del miedo y el instinto nacionalista, las fuerzas europe脙颅stas tenemos la obligaci脙鲁n de defender los principios sobre los que se construy脙鲁 la Uni脙鲁n Europea.

Recordemos a los ciudadanos por qu脙漏 somos europe脙颅stas.

Somos europe脙颅stas porque compartimos los valores de la democracia, el Estado de Derecho, la igualdad de g脙漏nero y la protecci脙鲁n de las minor脙颅as.

Somos europe脙颅stas porque queremos que nuestros Estados hagan valer su soberan脙颅a para defender nuestros intereses comunes, frente a retos como el cambio clim脙隆tico, la transformaci脙鲁n digital o la evasi脙鲁n fiscal.

Somos europe脙颅stas porque creemos que nuestra econom脙颅a social de mercado es la mejor fuente de progreso, un modelo social 脙潞nico en el mundo que garantiza a todas las personas educaci脙鲁n, sanidad o pensiones; un modelo social que todav脙颅a est脙隆 por construir. Y no hay mejor manera de vencer al populismo de la extrema derecha que garantizar salarios dignos y viviendas asequibles.

Somos europe脙颅stas porque nuestra larga historia de odio, del miedo al otro y de las guerras crueles, nos ha ense脙卤ado que los conflictos deben resolverse pac脙颅ficamente siempre que sea posible.

Y somos europe脙颅stas porque la historia nos demuestra que, despu脙漏s de cada crisis, nuestra uni脙鲁n sale m脙隆s integrada y unida.

S脙颅, los socialdem脙鲁cratas somos europe脙颅stas. Y con el liderazgo de Teresa Ribera en la transici脙鲁n verde, con el empe脙卤o de Roxana M脙庐nzatu por los empleos de calidad, con el compromiso de Dan J脙赂rgensen en la lucha contra la pobreza energ脙漏tica y por el acceso a la vivienda, y con la implicaci脙鲁n de Glenn Micallef por el futuro de los j脙鲁venes, lo seremos m脙隆s en el futuro.

La historia de Europa tiene sello socialdem脙鲁crata y que nadie piense que nos vamos a resignar. Seguiremos firmes, con la cabeza alta y con nuestros valores intactos para afrontar los nuevos desaf脙颅os.

Se脙卤or脙颅as, la Uni脙鲁n Europea es el proceso de integraci脙鲁n m脙隆s exitoso en la historia del planeta. Juntos, hemos logrado la estabilidad y el progreso para un territorio en el que conviven 27脗聽Estados con 450脗聽millones de personas. Y hoy, m脙隆s all脙隆 de nuestras diferencias ideol脙鲁gicas, debemos seguir avanzando por un objetivo com脙潞n: construir una Europa unida, una Europa pr脙鲁spera, una Europa en paz.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jordan Bardella, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame la pr脙漏sidente de la Commission europ脙漏enne, apr脙篓s un mandat jalonn脙漏 par de nombreux 脙漏checs et par de nombreux scandales, apr脙篓s des 脙漏lections marqu脙漏es par une contestation sans pr脙漏c脙漏dent du fonctionnement de l'Union europ脙漏enne, vous nous pr脙漏sentez aujourd'hui la nouvelle 脙漏quipe qui formera la Commission europ脙漏enne.

Ces commissaires europ脙漏ens sont anonymes, inconnus du grand public. Ils n'ont 脙漏t脙漏 脙漏lus par personne et pourtant ils dictent le quotidien de 450脗聽millions de citoyens. Et pourtant ce sont eux qui ont droit de vie ou de mort sur des pans entiers de l'脙漏conomie europ脙漏enne. Ce sont eux, au d脙漏tour d'un couloir 脙聽 Bruxelles, qui peuvent d脙漏cider d'interdire la vente de v脙漏hicules thermiques 脙聽 l'horizon脗聽2035, de plonger notre industrie dans la d脙漏tresse, de soumettre les agriculteurs 脙聽 des normes draconiennes, sans jamais songer aux cons脙漏quences ravageuses de ces d脙漏cisions pour ceux qui les subissent.

Malgr脙漏 les protestations populaires, malgr脙漏 les mobilisations, comme celle de nos paysans, vous restez camp脙漏e sur vos positions, sur votre id脙漏ologie; vous r脙漏affirmiez, il y a quelques instants, les ambitions d脙漏mesur脙漏es du pacte vert, qui est l'autre nom plus bucolique de votre projet funeste de d脙漏croissance, qui va remettre la souverainet脙漏 des nations d'Europe et notre destin entre les mains des autres.

La Commission refuse de se r脙漏former parce qu'elle refuse de reconna脙庐tre publiquement plus d'une d脙漏cennie d'erreurs et d'errances qui ont men脙漏 les nations d'Europe l脙聽 o脙鹿 elles sont aujourd'hui. En d脙漏crochage face aux 脙聣tats-Unis, menac脙漏e par l'avanc脙漏e rapide de la Chine, contest脙漏e par l'脙漏mergence de nouveaux g脙漏ants comme l'Inde, vid脙漏e de ses talents qui s'expatrient chaque jour davantage, l'Europe est devenue l'homme malade des 脙漏conomies d脙漏velopp脙漏es.

Cette r脙漏alit脙漏 devrait vous appeler au sursaut, mais elle vous conduit ind脙漏niablement 脙聽 la continuit脙漏: la m脙陋me inaction face 脙聽 la concurrence d脙漏loyale, les m脙陋mes r脙篓gles europ脙漏ennes du march脙漏 de l'脙漏lectricit脙漏, la m脙陋me 脙漏cologie punitive, le m脙陋me d脙漏ferlement migratoire qui change l'identit脙漏 de notre continent, la m脙陋me faiblesse 脙聽 l'脙漏gard de l'islamisme et de ses relais. Vous 脙陋tes, Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, 脙聽 l'image de beaucoup d'脙漏lus ici dans ce Parlement, une femme qui se disait de droite, mais qui r脙陋vait de se faire aduler par la gauche et par ses lubies.

Les m脙陋mes causes entra脙庐nant les m脙陋mes effets, ce chemin m脙篓nera l'Europe tout droit vers le d脙漏classement. Ne comptez donc pas sur nous pour vous y accompagner. Le groupe des Patriotes pour l'Europe votera 脙聽 l'unanimit脙漏 contre cette nouvelle Commission europ脙漏enne.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicola Procaccini, a nome del gruppo ECR. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, l'ECR ha una sua identit脙聽 politica. Siamo un gruppo che si batte per la conservazione dei valori che hanno fondato l'Europa. Siamo uomini e donne fermi nelle proprie convinzioni ma anche misurati nel modo di esporle.

Manteniamo un certo stile britannico, se me lo consentite, malgrado l'Inghilterra abbia lasciato l'Unione europea e con essa i Tories che fondarono l'ECR.

Infatti, abbiamo assistito con un certo distaccato divertimento allo psicodramma delle sinistre, in preda alle convulsioni per una vicepresidenza assegnata ad un conservatore italiano. La collega Aubry ieri ha detto: "I gruppi della sinistra hanno ceduto su tutta la linea".

Io credo invece, pi脙鹿 semplicemente, che i cittadini europei abbiano espresso il loro punto di vista alle elezioni europee e nazionali e che questo vada tenuto in considerazione, soprattutto dalla Presidente della Commissione, a cui devo riconoscere di aver resistito con dignit脙聽 e coraggio alle pressioni delle sinistre.

Ogni volta, e dovunque si 脙篓 votato, la sinistra ha perso le elezioni e la composizione di quest'Aula ce lo spiega pi脙鹿 di 1脗聽000 pensose analisi politiche.

Ciononostante, non esiste una maggioranza di centrodestra, perch脙漏 non esiste un vincolo di maggioranza: non c'脙篓 mai stato, neanche nei cinque anni passati, quando, grazie a numeri ben diversi dagli attuali, i socialisti bullizzavano i popolari, Timmermans bullizzava von der Leyen e tutti insieme bullizzavano i cittadini europei con un feroce radicalismo verde.

Fortunatamente le maggioranze qui si formano sui contenuti e possono cambiare ad ogni votazione. Noi resteremo fedeli a noi stessi. Abbiamo sostenuto i commissari indicati dai governi nazionali senza risparmiare loro le nostre critiche durante le audizioni, ma rispettando la prerogativa degli Stati membri di nominare il proprio commissario.

E daremo libert脙聽 di voto oggi, perch脙漏 ogni nostra delegazione ha il diritto di scegliere, di decidere in base al proprio interesse nazionale. Questo non significa essere euroscettici o antieuropeisti: questo vuol dire amare l'idea originale e meravigliosa di un'Europa dei popoli, "unita nella diversit脙聽".

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Val脙漏rie Hayer, au nom du groupe Renew. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente de la Commission, chers coll脙篓gues, 脙搂a n'aura pas 脙漏t脙漏 une mince affaire, 脙搂a n'aura pas 脙漏t脙漏 facile tous les jours, mais on y est arriv脙漏s. On sort d'un moment intense de conviction, de n脙漏gociation, de responsabilit脙漏.

D'abord, je voudrais saluer tous les candidats commissaires. Si votre coll脙篓ge est approuv脙漏 aujourd'hui, on travaillera ensemble, main dans la main, pour l'avenir de l'Europe. Je voudrais aussi avoir un mot particulier pour nos cinq candidats Renew: St脙漏phane, Kaja, Marta, Hadja, Michael. Vous avez fait un sans faute! Et dans votre 脙漏quipe, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Renew Europe, deuxi脙篓me force politique du coll脙篓ge, sera moteur de l'Europe. Mais il y en a qui ne seront pas moteur de l'Europe, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, et vous le savez tr脙篓s bien. Parmi eux, il y a le Fidesz.

Je rappelle qu'en脗聽2020 Viktor Orban pr脙麓nait les vaccins russes et qu'il intimide tous les jours les femmes qui veulent avorter. Alors nos coll脙篓gues en commission parlementaire ont 脙漏t脙漏 intransigeants. Merci, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, de les avoir 脙漏cout脙漏s, en retirant du portefeuille du commissaire Fidesz les droits sexuels et reproductifs et l'ensemble de la direction g脙漏n脙漏rale脗聽HERA. Voil脙聽 le travail du Parlement europ脙漏en. Et ce travail paie.

Le s脙漏rieux paie, parce que, disons-le clairement, je crois que l'on fait tous la m脙陋me analyse sur la mani脙篓re dont les auditions se sont pass脙漏es. Franchement, est-ce que c'est 脙搂a l'image qu'on veut renvoyer du Parlement europ脙漏en? Une guerre commerciale nous pend au nez et, pendant ce temps, on passe des jours 脙聽 se parler d'ordre de passage. C'脙漏tait cens脙漏 脙陋tre un sujet technique et non un combat politique.

Vladimir脗聽Poutine m脙篓ne une guerre barbare 脙聽 nos portes, multiplie les ing脙漏rences ici, en Europe, sur notre sol. Pendant ce temps, parce qu'un populiste peut fragiliser son premier ministre en Slov脙漏nie, nous perdons des jours dans le processus d'audition, ici, au Parlement europ脙漏en. Et je ne cite pas, 脙漏videmment, un autre d脙漏bat national, que l'on a tous en t脙陋te, et qui a vampiris脙漏 le processus de ces auditions.

Chers coll脙篓gues, 脙搂a ne marche pas, parce que, dans ces auditions, la vraie question, en r脙漏alit脙漏, est: est-ce que le commissaire 脙聽 l'脙漏nergie va nous proposer un mix qui garantisse notre ind脙漏pendance 脙漏nerg脙漏tique? La vraie question est: est-ce que le commissaire 脙聽 la d脙漏fense va soutenir, oui ou non, l'industrie europ脙漏enne? La vraie question est: est-ce que chacun de vos commissaires, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, sera en mesure d'assurer la vraie simplification qu'attendent nos PME, nos agriculteurs et nos citoyens?

C'est cela le c脜聯ur des auditions; c'est pour cela qu'on a cr脙漏脙漏 ce processus; c'est pour cela qu'elles sont importantes. 脙聙 la lumi脙篓re des derni脙篓res semaines, chers coll脙篓gues, nous devons clairement r脙漏viser la fa脙搂on dont cela se passe: depuis les nominations jusqu'aux 脙漏valuations, sur la parit脙漏, sur la rapidit脙漏 de la proc脙漏dure 芒聙聯脗聽cela fait bient脙麓t un an que l'on n'a plus un seul texte sur la table du Parlement europ脙漏en脗聽芒聙聯 et sur la mani脙篓re dont on s'assure que les jeux nationaux et les jeux des groupes ne parasitent plus les auditions.

Chers coll脙篓gues, dans une heure, nous allons voter sur le coll脙篓ge. La question est maintenant: comment travailler ensemble pour les cinq prochaines ann脙漏es? Car nous le voyons aujourd'hui, il y a deux majorit脙漏s possibles dans ce Parlement europ脙漏en.

Nous avons, d'un c脙麓t脙漏, la majorit脙漏 脗芦Venezuela脗禄. Celle-l脙聽, il faut le dire clairement, elle va faire beaucoup de mal aux d脙漏mocrates qui la font exister, parce qu'elle est contre l'Europe et surtout parce qu'elle est contre-nature. Elle est tellement contre-nature que les textes, 脙聽 la fin, notamment sur le budget脗聽芒聙聯 on l'a vu脗聽芒聙聯 tombent quand m脙陋me. En face, nous avons la seule majorit脙漏 qui vaille, la seule possible: c'est celle qui a fait le plan de relance, celle qui a fait le pacte vert, c'est aussi celle qui a fait Schengen et qui a fait l'euro par le pass脙漏. C'est la majorit脙漏 centrale, celle des pro-europ脙漏ens.

Cher Manfred, ch脙篓re Iratxe, c'est cette majorit脙漏 centrale que nous avons formalis脙漏e la semaine derni脙篓re dans l'accord que nous avons pass脙漏. Merci beaucoup pour le travail commun. Et c'est sur elle, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, que vous devrez vous appuyer, m脙陋me si脗聽芒聙聯 vous le savez parfaitement脗聽芒聙聯, sur la question des vice-pr脙漏sidents ex脙漏cutifs, nous ne sommes pas d'accord avec tous vos choix. Parce que cette coalition, c'est la seule qui soutient l'Ukraine, qui soutient l'脙漏tat de droit; la seule qui soutient le pouvoir d'achat, l'environnement, la libert脙漏; la seule qui porte un programme positif pour l'Europe.

Je voudrais avoir un mot pour Bas et Terry: je sais votre engagement et nous aurons l'occasion de fortifier ensemble le chemin pro-europ脙漏en de ce Parlement europ脙漏en. Car cette majorit脙漏, 脙漏videmment, est ouverte 脙聽 tous ceux qui veulent la rejoindre sur cette base.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Terry Reintke, im Namen der Verts/ALE-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, Frau Kommissionspr脙陇sidentin! Ich glaube, uns allen ist klar, und wenn wir einen Schritt zur脙录ckgehen, dann sehen wir es noch mehr: Wir stehen vor riesigen geopolitischen Herausforderungen. Gerade ist ein Demagoge und Antieurop脙陇er wieder ins Wei脙聼e Haus gew脙陇hlt worden. In der Ukraine finden gerade die blutigsten Auseinandersetzungen seit Anbeginn dieses Krieges statt, und wir sehen immer st脙陇rker, wie brutal der Klimawandel uns schon jetzt trifft脗聽芒聙聯 nicht in der Zukunft, sondern heute. So gro脙聼 diese Aufgaben sind, so gro脙聼 ist auch unsere Verantwortung. Wir brauchen gerade jetzt eine handlungsf脙陇hige Europ脙陇ische Union, und dazu geh脙露rt ein handlungsf脙陇higes Europ脙陇isches Parlament. Aber das Europ脙陇ische Parlament hat sich ehrlich gesagt in den letzten Wochen selbst verzwergt mit einem unw脙录rdigen Prozess der Anh脙露rungen, bei dem es nicht um Qualifikationen, sondern um Machtpoker ging脗聽芒聙聯 mit einem brutalen Ende letzte Woche, bei dem auch die Kandidaten der extremen Rechten durch Aussch脙录sse gewunken worden sind 芒聙聯 leider auch mit Stimmen der Konservativen, der Sozialdemokratinnen und 芒聙聭demokraten und der Liberalen. Wir Gr脙录ne haben dagegen gestimmt, und das aus voller 脙聹berzeugung, weil wir uns sicher sind, dass nur mit proeurop脙陇ischen gemeinsamen Mehrheiten der Mitte eine Europ脙陇ische Union funktionieren kann.

Diese zentrale Chance, genau diese Mehrheiten zu bauen, die ist verpasst worden. Denn mit proeurop脙陇ischen Mehrheiten geeint h脙陇tten wir eine Strategie vereinbaren k脙露nnen, die das Europ脙陇ische Parlament gest脙陇rkt h脙陇tte und klare Mehrheitsfindungen auch in Zukunft sichert. Die EVP zeigt ehrlich gesagt leider immer offener, dass sie sich Zusammenarbeit mit der extremen Rechten offenhalten will, zur Gefahr f脙录r das gesamte europ脙陇ische Projekt. Aber wir Gr脙录ne sagen sehr klar: Gerade weil uns dieses europ脙陇ische Projekt so am Herzen liegt, haben wir bis zum letzten Moment verhandelt. Wir haben das nicht getan aus Eitelkeit, wir haben das nicht getan, weil wir es uns einfach machen wollen, wir haben das getan, weil wir die Europ脙陇ische Union st脙录tzen wollen. In der Gesamtabw脙陇gung haben wir von der Pr脙陇sidentin Zusagen bekommen zum Green Deal, zur Rechtsstaatlichkeit und auch zu proeurop脙陇ischen Mehrheiten.

Ich kann Ihnen sagen, die Debatten bei uns in der Fraktion waren nicht einfach. F脙录r uns ist diese Entscheidung extrem schwierig, und nicht alle Abgeordneten der Gr脙录nen sind zum gleichen Ergebnis gekommen. Ich habe gro脙聼en Respekt vor den unterschiedlichen Entscheidungen meiner Kolleginnen und Kollegen. Am Ende wird eine knappe Mehrheit unserer Fraktion f脙录r diese Kommission stimmen. Wir werden in diesem Parlament immer beides sein 芒聙聯 kritisch und konstruktiv. Wir wollen proeurop脙陇ische Mehrheiten gegen Rechtsextreme bauen, weil wir nur so dieses europ脙陇ische Projekt sch脙录tzen k脙露nnen.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Manon Aubry, au nom du groupe The Left. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, votre nouvelle Commission sera le premier gouvernement d'union de la droite et de l'extr脙陋me droite de l'histoire de l'Union europ脙漏enne.

La pr脙漏tendue coalition des groupes centraux n'existe plus et toutes les lignes rouges des soi-disant progressistes ont 脙漏t脙漏 pi脙漏tin脙漏es. On nous disait qu'il n'y aurait pas d'extr脙陋me droite 脙聽 des postes de responsabilit脙漏: vous avez choisi comme vice-pr脙漏sident Raffaele脗聽Fitto, le post-fasciste aussi connu pour ses accusations de corruption.

Vous nous avez dit qu'il n'y aurait pas de n脙漏gociation avec l'extr脙陋me droite pour la r脙漏partition des postes de pouvoir: au final, l'extr脙陋me droite a men脙漏 les n脙漏gociations.

Vous nous avez dit qu'il n'y aurait pas de coalitions entre la droite et l'extr脙陋me droite: depuis le d脙漏but de ce mandat, elles sont syst脙漏matiques, comme on l'a vu par exemple pour faire tomber un texte contre la d脙漏forestation. Et il suffisait de voir, 脙聽 l'instant, la jubilation de Manfred脗聽Weber, c脙漏l脙漏brant sa nouvelle coalition avec le groupe ECR d'extr脙陋me droite pour en 脙陋tre s脙禄r.

Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, la Commission que vous nous proposez, c'est en r脙漏alit脙漏 la Commission de la fin du cordon sanitaire. C'est la Commission de la honte, qui d脙漏roule le tapis rouge 脙聽 l'extr脙陋me droite en c脙漏l脙漏brant des dirigeants autoritaires, comme en Hongrie, en Italie, qui s'attaquent aux droits des femmes, aux LGBT et propagent leur haine raciste et x脙漏nophobe. Alors, 脙聽 mes coll脙篓gues 脙漏cologistes et socialistes, je demande: comment pouvez-vous cautionner cela?

D'autant plus que tout le processus de d脙漏signation de cette Commission a 脙漏t脙漏 un pur scandale d脙漏mocratique. Les conflits d'int脙漏r脙陋ts n'ont jamais 脙漏t脙漏 s脙漏rieusement 脙漏tudi脙漏s. Un seul exemple suffit pour le comprendre: le commissaire Wopke脗聽Hoekstra sera en charge du climat et de la fiscalit脙漏, alors qu'il a travaill脙漏 pour l'entreprise p脙漏troli脙篓re Shell et a eu une soci脙漏t脙漏 脙漏cran dans un paradis fiscal. Les auditions n'ont 脙漏t脙漏 qu'un cirque risible dont le d脙漏nouement 脙漏tait d脙漏j脙聽 connu; des magouilles opaques ridiculisant un peu plus, voire totalement, la cr脙漏dibilit脙漏 de notre Parlement europ脙漏en.

Je vous le dis avec fiert脙漏, Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen: notre groupe de la gauche est le seul 脙聽 avoir refus脙漏 cette mascarade depuis le d脙漏but. Car, sur le fond, votre projet politique va 脙漏galement ajouter partout du malheur social.

Quelle r脙漏ponse apportez-vous 脙聽 la multiplication des plans sociaux 脙聽 travers l'Europe et 脙聽 l'augmentation de la pauvret脙漏? Un ch脙篓que en blanc aux multinationales qui peuvent toucher de l'argent public tout en licenciant, et une nouvelle saign脙漏e sociale avec votre cure d'aust脙漏rit脙漏 XXL.

Quelle r脙漏ponse apportez-vous 脙聽 la col脙篓re agricole et 脙聽 l'urgence climatique? La signature, 脙聽 tour de bras, d'accords de libre-脙漏change, dont celui avec le Mercosur sera probablement un de vos premiers actes.

Quelle r脙漏ponse apportez-vous, enfin, aux massacres en cours dans la bande de Gaza? Une complicit脙漏 coupable, Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, en refusant de prendre les sanctions qui s'imposent contre le g脙漏nocidaire Benjamin脗聽Netanyahou malgr脙漏 le mandat d'arr脙陋t de la Cour p脙漏nale internationale. Alors comment osez-vous rester silencieuse apr脙篓s une telle d脙漏cision, alors que plus de 45脗聽000 civils sont d脙漏j脙聽 morts sous les bombes?

Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, vous l'aurez compris, notre groupe de la gauche votera unanimement contre votre Commission de malheur et constituera la seule opposition cr脙漏dible de cet H脙漏micycle.

Avec votre b脙漏n脙漏diction, le r脙篓gne de l'extr脙陋me droite commence aujourd'hui. Je pense donc, pour finir, aux mots de la grande r脙漏sistante fran脙搂aise Lucie Aubrac: 脗芦Le verbe r脙漏sister doit toujours se conjuguer au pr脙漏sent.脗禄 Alors, depuis cet H脙漏micycle, je lance ce grand, ce bel, ce puissant appel 脙聽 la r脙漏sistance avant qu'il ne soit trop tard.

脗聽脗聽Sarah Knafo, au nom du groupe ESN. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, vous n'ignorez pas que les peuples europ脙漏ens ne s'int脙漏ressent absolument pas 脙聽 cette 脙漏lection des commissaires. Et je veux vous dire en face, Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, que c'est de votre faute.

脙聙 force d'arrangements 脙聽 voix basse, de petits calculs partisans et d'hypocrisie technocratique, vous avez r脙漏ussi 脙聽 d脙漏go脙禄ter les citoyens des institutions europ脙漏ennes. Ils ne veulent m脙陋me plus savoir qui va 脙陋tre d脙漏sign脙漏 commissaire aujourd'hui, ni pourquoi. Ils savent d'avance que ces commissaires seront 脙聽 votre image, 脙聽 l'image de ce que vous avez fait de l'Europe depuis cinq ans: une Europe 脙漏touffante, dangereuse, scl脙漏ros脙漏e.

Je veux dire 脙聽 ces millions d'脙漏lecteurs que, pour les cinq ans qui viennent, je ne vous laisserai pas faire. Vous ne ferez pas de notre continent un espace sans int脙漏r脙陋t et sans 脙垄me, o脙鹿 vos caprices technocratiques font la loi et dictent la morale. Avec tous ceux de cette Assembl脙漏e qui sont attach脙漏s 脙聽 la v脙漏rit脙漏 et 脙聽 la libert脙漏, nous briserons le silence qui r脙篓gne dans votre institution.

Vous gagnerez sans doute cette 脙漏lection, mais nous vous feront perdre tout le reste. Dans les cinq ans qui viennent, nous ne vous accorderons aucun r脙漏pit. Nous ne vous laisserons plus op脙漏rer en catimini. Nous serons le projecteur braqu脙漏 sur vos agissements. Les Europ脙漏ens doivent savoir la v脙漏rit脙漏. Ils en ont envie, ils en ont besoin et ils y ont droit.

脗聽脗聽Andrzej Halicki (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo! Bezpiecze脜聞stwo, bezpiecze脜聞stwo, bezpiecze脜聞stwo, bezpiecze脜聞stwo 芒聙聯 m脙鲁g脜聜bym tak jeszcze kilka razy wymienia脛聡 to s脜聜owo, bo obywatele Europy czekaj脛聟, by poczucie bezpiecze脜聞stwa wzmacnia脛聡 i to nasze wsp脙鲁lne zadanie.

Bezpiecze脜聞stwo militarne 芒聙聯 mamy wojn脛聶 na naszym kontynencie, u naszych bram. W tym miejscu chcia脜聜bym zaapelowa脛聡 do Parlamentu, do wszystkich grup politycznych: czas sko脜聞czy脛聡 debat脛聶 i dyskusj脛聶 o tym, jak i w jaki spos脙鲁b powo脜聜a脛聡 nowe komisje, kt脙鲁re powinny wspomaga脛聡 prace Komisji Europejskiej. Komisja 芒聙聯 sta脜聜a, du脜录a 芒聙聯 do spraw bezpiecze脜聞stwa powinna dzia脜聜a脛聡 natychmiast, najdalej od pocz脛聟tku przysz脜聜ego roku. Tak, obie komisje powinni脜聸my powo脜聜a脛聡 jak najszybciej, bo bezpiecze脜聞stwo nie mo脜录e czeka脛聡. Na to czekaj脛聟 te脜录 nasi obywatele.

脗聽脗聽Nicola Zingaretti (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, noi guardiamo alle cittadine e ai cittadini europei che hanno paura del futuro e stanno perdendo la speranza e chiedono protezione. E i singoli Paesi da soli non potranno garantire questo futuro e solo l'Europa pu脙虏 essere una soluzione, ma deve cambiare: non l'Europa intergovernativa delle nazioni ma un'Europa che nel disordine mondiale riprende la strada dell'integrazione federale.

Dobbiamo unirci, investire per politiche comuni pi脙鹿 forti sul lavoro, la competitivit脙聽, l'innovazione dell'industria, la ricerca tecnologica, formare nuove risorse umane adeguate, affrontare la transizione ecologica e digitale, pensare a nuovi modelli di welfare e salute per proteggere le persone.

Dobbiamo unirci intorno a coraggiose riforme che ci rendano un attore globale per una politica estera e di sicurezza comune e difendere la pace, rilanciare le cooperazioni rafforzate, ridiscutere il criterio dell'unanimit脙聽, pensare a riforme fiscali comuni che portino giustizia.

Presidente von der Leyen, noi a luglio abbiamo votato il suo programma per questi motivi. Ora, se si 脙篓 cambiato idea e si cercano altre strade, sarebbe corretto dirlo senza furbizie, perch脙漏 ora quel programma va attuato con i fatti.

E attenzione: i nazionalisti non hanno un'idea diversa di Europa; non la vogliono l'Europa unita e si alleano nel mondo con i nemici dell'Europa. Noi voteremo "s脙卢" perch脙漏 si deve partire. Non faremo regali a Donald Trump e a Putin ma da ora nessuno sconto.

Vigileremo per il rispetto del programma e combatteremo in ogni spazio parlamentare per far avanzare il progetto di un nuovo europeismo, sapendo, per dirla con Altiero Spinelli, "che la via da percorrere non 脙篓 facile, non 脙篓 sicura ma deve essere percorsa e lo sar脙聽". Per questo voteremo "s脙卢" con questa chiarezza verso una nuova Europa, pi脙鹿 forte e pi脙鹿 umana.

脗聽脗聽Harald Vilimsky (PfE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, meine sehr geehrten Damen und Herren! In Richtung der Linken, der Gr脙录nen, der Marxisten, der Leninisten, was immer da herumkrebst bei Ihnen: Ich lasse es mir nicht l脙陇nger gefallen, dass von Ihnen meine politische Gruppierung hier in die N脙陇he des Extremismus ger脙录ckt wird. Sie waren es, die Bella Ciao get脙露nt haben. Sie sind es, die in Ihren Reihen eine Gewaltt脙陇terin haben, die mit einem Hammer auf Menschen losgegangen ist. Meine sehr geehrten Damen und Herren, wir haben nicht fraternisiert mit der Frau von der Leyen und ihrer Kommission. Wir lehnen diese Kommission ab. Nicht ein einfaches Nein脗聽芒聙聯 ein dreifaches, ein f脙录nffaches Nein, denn diese Kommission steht f脙录r Massenmigration, sie steht f脙录r Freiheitsentzug, sie steht f脙录r Kriegstreiberei, sie steht f脙录r Deindustrialisierung und den Niedergang Europas. V脙露llig unn脙露tig in einer Zeit, wo wir wirtschaftliche Probleme haben. Sie h脙陇tten Geld sparen k脙露nnen. Sie h脙陇tten deregulieren k脙露nnen. Was Sie machen, ist der falsche Weg.

脗聽脗聽Adam Bielan (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca, dzisiaj w Pani przem脙鲁wieniu najcz脛聶脜聸ciej wymienianym s脜聜owem by脜聜o s脜聜owo 芒聙聻konkurencyjno脜聸脛聡芒聙聺. I bardzo s脜聜usznie, poniewa脜录 Unia Europejska w ostatnich latach, mi脛聶dzy innymi pod Pani rz脛聟dami, dramatycznie t脛聶 konkurencyjno脜聸脛聡 na rzecz naszych rywali na 脜聸wiecie utraci脜聜a.

Jak czytamy w raporcie Mario Draghiego, kt脙鲁ry r脙鲁wnie脜录 Pani prezentowa脜聜a, jednym z g脜聜脙鲁wnych powod脙鲁w utraty tej konkurencyjno脜聸ci jest fakt, 脜录e 芒聙聯 cytuj脛聶 芒聙聯 芒聙聻ceny detaliczne energii elektrycznej 芒聙聯 zw脜聜aszcza dla sektor脙鲁w przemys脜聜owych 芒聙聯 s脛聟 obecnie dwa do trzech razy wy脜录sze ni脜录 w USA i w Chinach芒聙聺, a wi脛聶c jedne z najwy脜录szych na 脜聸wiecie.

G脜聜脙鲁wnym powodem tak wysokich cen energii jest szale脜聞czy 芒聙聯 nie waham si脛聶 tego s脜聜owa u脜录y脛聡 芒聙聯 szale脜聞czy plan Zielonego 脜聛adu. A jakie wnioski Pani z tego wyci脛聟ga? Pani nam dzisiaj powiedzia脜聜a, 脜录e nie tylko nie zawr脙鲁ci Pani z tej drogi, kt脙鲁ra prowadzi gospodark脛聶 Unii Europejskiej ku katastrofie, ale ze wzgl脛聶du na to, 脜录e potrzebuje Pani g脜聜os脙鲁w frakcji Zielonych, w ci脛聟gu najbli脜录szych lat b脛聶dziemy mie脛聡 de facto wi脛聶cej tego samego.

Tymczasem w ostatnich eurowyborach wyborcy jasno opowiedzieli si脛聶 za zmian脛聟. Niestety, w dzisiejszym wyst脛聟pieniu udowodni脜聜a Pani, 脜录e jest Pani g脜聜ucha na g脜聜os obywateli. Nikt z nas nie kwestionuje tego, 脜录e dzia脜聜ania na rzecz ochrony 脜聸rodowiska s脛聟 potrzebne. Ale jedyny efekt obecnej polityki klimatycznej, jakiego do脜聸wiadczaj脛聟 obywatele Unii Europejskiej, to dro脜录sza energia, dro脜录szy samoch脙鲁d, dro脜录sze ciep脜聜o i dro脜录sza 脜录ywno脜聸脛聡.

Nie us脜聜yszeli脜聸my dzisiaj nic o wycofaniu si脛聶 z gro脜潞nego, szkodliwego paktu migracyjnego. S脜聜yszymy za to od miesi脛聶cy o pr脙鲁bie wprowadzenia umowy handlowej z Mercosurem, kt脙鲁ra b脛聶dzie brutalnym ciosem w europejskie rolnictwo.

I cho脛聡 w obecnym sk脜聜adzie Komisji Europejskiej znajduje si脛聶 wielu kompetentnych kandydat脙鲁w, takich jak pan Fito, pani Kallas czy pan Kubilius, to nic nie wskazuje na to, aby ta b脜聜脛聶dna i szkodliwa polityka Komisji Europejskiej jako ca脜聜o脜聸ci mia脜聜a ulec jakiejkolwiek zmianie. Nie mo脜录e by脛聡 na to naszej zgody. Dlatego jako delegacja Prawa i Sprawiedliwo脜聸ci w Parlamencie Europejskim zag脜聜osujemy dzisiaj przeciwko tej Komisji.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Billy Kelleher (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, I want to wish the individual Commissioners success personally, and I hope that the Commission collectively will have success into the future. We have significant challenges geopolitically, economically and in the areas of competitiveness.

President, you referenced the issue of increasing our spending in innovation and research, decarbonising our economy and supporting the Green Deal and ensuring economic security. These are critical issues to be dealt with immediately. You speak about investment and public and private funding, simplification of the single market, decent wages, quality of life, education, childcare, healthcare and housing. And in our regions and our rural communities, you speak about ensuring that farmers and fishers have a future.

But most importantly, President, we have a duty in this House to ensure that we hold you to account. You spoke about the fact that you were going to work from the centre out. Well, I honestly believe that the centre starts there and finishes there (points to the left and the right of the Chamber). And if you are to work from the centre out, if you continue, and if others continue, to listen to the far right, well, then the centre will not hold.

And our duty is to ensure that the centre does hold, because on the far right they want to destroy the very values that you spoke about. So we cannot give comfort or succour to those on the far right. Let us be very clear. Individual freedoms and individual rights never, ever come from the far right, and our history and lessons should teach us that.

So, Commission President, I wish you well, and I hope that you will ensure that the coalition of the centre will work, will function and will be supported, and people in this House have a duty to ensure that the coalition works as well. So we do not want to be in truck to those on the right.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Diana Riba i Giner (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤oras presidentas, se脙卤oras comisarias, 脗驴era posible tener una Comisi脙鲁n sin la presencia de la extrema derecha? Ciertamente, probablemente, no lo era. Si hay Estados gobernados por la extrema derecha, sabemos que habr脙隆 comisarios de la extrema derecha. Pero lo que s脙颅 estaba en sus manos, presidenta von der Leyen, era decidir qu脙漏 papel iban a jugar estos comisarios. Y, ante esta elecci脙鲁n, usted ha decidido dar una vicepresidencia 芒聙聰con grandes responsabilidades芒聙聰 al enviado de Meloni, ni m脙隆s ni menos que las pol脙颅ticas de cohesi脙鲁n que, a trav脙漏s de sus fondos, gestionan un tercio del presupuesto europeo. Y por ah脙颅 no podemos pasar. No podemos dar apoyo a lo que, a todas luces, implica la normalizaci脙鲁n de la extrema derecha.

Sabemos que, en su Comisi脙鲁n, hay personas que est脙隆n tan inc脙鲁modas como nosotros con esta decisi脙鲁n. A ellas les decimos que haremos una oposici脙鲁n constructiva y que siempre, siempre, nos encontrar脙隆n dispuestos a trabajar por una Europa de derechos y libertades. No son tiempos f脙隆ciles para la democracia, pero tampoco son tiempos para bajar los brazos.

(La oradora se niega a que Stefano Cavedagna le formule una pregunta con arreglo al procedimiento de la 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄)

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Botenga (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, nous ne vous soutiendrons jamais. Jamais, parce que dans votre Europe, travailler ne suffit plus pour vivre: plus d'un tiers des actifs europ脙漏ens, aujourd'hui, disent que leurs revenus ne suffisent plus pour payer les factures. Plus d'un tiers des Europ脙漏ens a d脙禄 renoncer 脙聽 des soins de sant脙漏, pr脙篓s d'un tiers, renoncer m脙陋me 脙聽 des repas, et plus de deux parents sur trois, aujourd'hui, renoncent aux sorties en famille.

Dans ce contexte, vous pr脙麓nez l'aust脙漏rit脙漏 pour les travailleurs, vous qui gagnez 30脗聽000脗聽euros par mois. C'est facile, 脙漏videmment! Pendant que vous refusez de taxer les millionnaires, vous refusez de tacler les superprofits des grandes multinationales. Mais quelle honte!

Et parlons de l'international, o脙鹿 personne n'a oubli脙漏 votre voyage 脙聽 Tel-Aviv, pour aller encourager le criminel de guerre Netanyahou. Je me demande si, un jour, ce soutien, votre voyage peut-脙陋tre, vous vaudra aussi un billet pour La Haye.

Madame von der Leyen, pour sauver votre place, vous avez pactis脙漏 avec l'extr脙陋me droite, vous avez achet脙漏 les 脙漏cologistes, humili脙漏 les socialistes, mais nous, nous ne c脙漏derons rien. Nous serons l'opposition sociale pendant les cinq ans 脙聽 venir. Comptez sur nous pour nous opposer 脙聽 vous.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Christine Anderson (ESN). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Donald Trump stellt sein neues Kabinett vor: hochkompetente Patrioten, entschlossen to make America great again, entschlossen, wieder Politik f脙录r das Volk zu machen. Und was kriegen wir? Sie, Frau von der Leyen 芒聙聯 herzlichen Dank! Die Abrissbirne, die gnadenlos alles niederrei脙聼t, was die europ脙陇ischen V脙露lker stark gemacht hat. Ich m脙露chte ein Europa, das stark, souver脙陇n und erfolgreich ist; ein Europa des Erfolgs, ein Europa der Freiheit, der Demokratie und der Rechtsstaatlichkeit. Mit Ihnen werden wir all das nicht bekommen.

Frau von der Leyen, Sie alleine w脙陇ren ja schon schlimm genug. Aber die angehenden Kommissare, die Sie um sich versammelt haben, das ist die reinste Tr脙录mmertruppe, was Freiheit und Demokratie und Rechtsstaatlichkeit angeht. Legen Sie, Frau von der Leyen, doch erst mal Rechenschaft 脙录ber Ihre uns脙陇glichen mRNA芒聙聭Impfstoffliefervertr脙陇ge ab. Ansonsten k脙露nnen Sie sich Ihren Vorschlag der Kommissare an den Hut stecken. Meine Fraktion wird das nicht unterst脙录tzen. Nun, bedauerlicherweise darf ich Sie nicht korrupt nennen, sonst stellt man mir hier wieder das Mikrofon ab, aber seis drum: Frau von der Leyen, Sie sind korrupt bis in die letzte Faser Ihres Daseins!

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dolors Montserrat (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤oras presidentas, esta nueva Comisi脙鲁n tendr脙隆 que liderar la defensa de nuestros valores, nuestra seguridad y nuestro Estado de Derecho, impulsar la competitividad y la innovaci脙鲁n, dar oportunidades a los j脙鲁venes, cumplir el pacto migratorio y proteger a agricultores, ganaderos, pescadores y aut脙鲁nomos, para que ninguna familia se quede atr脙隆s.

El Partido Popular Europeo hemos sido el motor decisivo de los grandes avances de Europa y, como miembros leales de la familia popular europea, apoyamos firmemente a esta Comisi脙鲁n liderada por Ursula von der Leyen.

Somos profundamente europe脙颅stas porque somos espa脙卤oles. Ustedes saben que nos duele Espa脙卤a. No callaremos cuando el peor Gobierno de Espa脙卤a de nuestra democracia est脙隆 sumido en una deriva de corrupci脙鲁n, populismo y vulneraci脙鲁n del Estado de Derecho que tambi脙漏n nos afecta como europeos.

Teresa Ribera ha desaparecido de Espa脙卤a en lo peor de una tragedia que se llev脙鲁 la vida de 220脗聽europeos. Teresa Ribera ha sido la vicepresidenta de un Gobierno que se脙卤ala a jueces, intenta silenciar a la prensa cr脙颅tica, utiliza al fiscal general, imputado por el Tribunal Supremo por maniobrar pol脙颅ticamente contra una presidenta regional, y est脙隆 envuelto en un esc脙隆ndalo de corrupci脙鲁n que puede afectar a once脗聽ministerios, entre ellos el de Teresa Ribera. Ribera llega a Bruselas con demasiadas cuentas pendientes en Espa脙卤a y muy poco cr脙漏dito en Europa.

As脙颅 que nosotros 芒聙聰la delegaci脙鲁n espa脙卤ola del Partido Popular芒聙聰 nos mantenemos firmes, decimos s脙颅 a la Comisi脙鲁n y no a Teresa Ribera, por principios, lealtad y justicia.

(La oradora se niega a que Petras Gra脜戮ulis y Cristophe Clergeau le formulen preguntas con arreglo al procedimiento de la 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Javier Moreno S脙隆nchez (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora presidenta von der Leyen, la delegaci脙鲁n socialista espa脙卤ola votar脙隆 a favor del Colegio de Comisarios y Comisarias tras el acuerdo alcanzado entre las fuerzas europe脙颅stas de esta C脙隆mara.

Me alegra que el Partido Popular Europeo haya vuelto al redil europe脙颅sta con la declaraci脙鲁n pol脙颅tica conjunta y espero que no vuelva a rendirse ante los cantos de sirena de la extrema derecha de esta C脙隆mara.

Me alegra tambi脙漏n que el Partido Popular Europeo haya apoyado a su vicepresidenta primera, a Teresa Ribera, y haya abandonado en la m脙隆s fr脙颅a soledad al Partido Popular espa脙卤ol con su ruido, sus bulos y su fracaso. Se脙卤or Weber, va por buen camino.

Se脙卤or脙颅as, tiene que arrancar ya la nueva Comisi脙鲁n Europea. En un contexto geopol脙颅tico convulso, frente a una extrema derecha en alza, tenemos que cumplir nuestros compromisos con la ciudadan脙颅a europea, para garantizarle la paz, la democracia y el bienestar social y tambi脙漏n para convertir a la Uni脙鲁n Europea en un l脙颅der mundial competitivo que proteja el planeta. En esta labor, se脙卤ora presidenta, cuenta usted con un referente mundial: su vicepresidenta primera, Teresa Ribera.

脗聽脗聽Ant脙鲁nio T脙垄nger Corr脙陋a (PfE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhora Presidente da Comiss脙拢o, Caros Colegas, hoje quero expressar aqui a minha preocupa脙搂脙拢o com o estado do processo de nomea脙搂脙拢o dos membros da Comiss脙拢o Europeia que compromete a credibilidade da nossa Uni脙拢o.

O que deveria ser um exemplo de transpar脙陋ncia e meritocracia tornou-se um palco para jogos pol脙颅ticos. Esta politiza脙搂脙拢o mina a confian脙搂a dos cidad脙拢os nas nossas institui脙搂脙碌es.

Por um lado, as pol脙颅ticas prosseguidas pela anterior e pela atual Comiss脙拢o fazem-nos ficar extremamente preocupados. Um脗聽Green Deal que cada vez mais se transforma num Black Deal, com inaceit脙隆veis consequ脙陋ncias. Por outro lado, a liberdade concedida 脙聽 imigra脙搂脙拢o, sem qualquer controlo, preocupa todos os estados europeus, os cidad脙拢os da Europa e todas as sociedades que est脙拢o a ser por essa imigra脙搂脙拢o destru脙颅das.

Finalmente, gostaria de dizer uma coisa, Senhora Presidente, 脙漏 que n脙鲁s somos a favor da liberdade e contra o totalitarismo. E 脙漏 isso que nos faz mexer e 脙漏 isso que nos faz lutar.

脗聽脗聽Charlie Weimers (ECR). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! N脙陇r v脙陇ljarna nu v脙陇nder sig bort fr脙楼n Europas socialdemokrater kan vi se sorgens fem faser utspela sig: F脙露rnekelse, n脙陇r vi gjorde upp om att st脙露tta oppositionen i Venezuela s脙楼 l脙陇mnade socialdemokraterna f脙露rhandlingen. Ilska, den gruppen var inte glad n脙陇r vi inf脙露rde realism i klimatpolitiken. Och nu ser vi f脙露rhandling, nu vill socialdemokraterna g脙楼 i avtal med de borgerliga f脙露r att slippa k脙陇nna av konsekvenserna av v脙陇ljarnas dom.

Min fr脙楼ga till er borgerliga 脙陇r: Varf脙露r agerar ni terapeuter 脙楼t socialdemokraterna? Varf脙露r inte g脙露ra gemensam sak med oss p脙楼 h脙露gersidan f脙露r att minska byr脙楼kratin och 脙露ka konkurrenskraften, inf脙露ra en realistisk klimatpolitik d脙陇r k脙陇rnkraften byggs ut, s脙陇kra gr脙陇nserna, 脙楼terv脙陇nda Illegala migranter? Anv脙陇nd h脙露germajoriteten.

I脗聽st脙陇llet pr脙陇glas nu de politiska direktiven till kommissionen av mer av samma. Det 脙陇r inte proeuropeiskt, det 脙陇r prostagnation eller, som fru von der Leyen sj脙陇lv s脙陇ger,脗聽managing the decline.

脗聽脗聽Dan Barna (Renew). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, este o dovad脛聝 de responsabilitate 脠聶i maturitate fa脠聸脛聝 de cet脛聝脠聸enii europeni c脛聝 negocierile politice au ajuns la rezultatul 脙庐n care putem spune ast脛聝zi c脛聝 avem o comisie 脠聶i c脛聝 aceasta 脙庐脠聶i va 脙庐ncepe mandatul pe 1 decembrie. Arhitectura electoral脛聝 dup脛聝 9 iunie nu a fost deloc 脠聶i nu este una simpl脛聝, a脠聶a c脛聝 v脛聝 felicit pe to脠聸i negociatorii pentru anduran脠聸a de a ob脠聸ine un rezultat 脙庐n acest moment. Acum 脙庐ns脛聝, doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝 脠聶i doamnelor 脠聶i domnilor comisari, a sosit momentul s脛聝 脙庐ncepem s脛聝 脙庐ndeplinim a脠聶tept脛聝ri 脠聶i s脛聝 furniz脛聝m rezultate. Acum, nu peste 2 ani, nu peste 5 ani.

Cer de la acest pupitru al Parlamentului o preocupare adev脛聝rat脛聝 pentru statul de drept. Cer o aplicare strict脛聝 脠聶i clar脛聝 a legisla脠聸iei privind re脠聸elele sociale. Cer mecanisme mai dure de combatere a dezinform脛聝rii 脠聶i m脛聝suri mai eficiente de r脛聝spuns la r脛聝zboiul hibrid, care, iat脛聝, vine din ce 脙庐n ce mai mult peste noi. Altfel, alegerile democratice, a脠聶a cum le 脠聶tim 脠聶i le dorim 脙庐n Europa, s-ar putea s脛聝 devin脛聝 istorie. Suntem o uniune a oamenilor, nu a algoritmilor, a脠聶a c脛聝 cer acestei Comisii ca o opera脠聸iune precum cea de la alegerile de duminica trecut脛聝 din Rom脙垄nia s脛聝 nu mai poat脛聝 fi posibil脛聝. Trebuie s脛聝 ac脠聸ion脛聝m. Acum.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Toussaint (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, en choisissant de nommer Raffaele脗聽Fitto 脙聽 un poste de vice-pr脙漏sident, vous entamez votre second mandat 脙聽 la t脙陋te de la Commission europ脙漏enne de la plus triste des mani脙篓res.

Votre choix, Madame, n'est pas celui du compromis, mais de la compromission avec l'extr脙陋me droite. Et c'est une faute, car la situation politique n'exige ni complaisance ni confusion, mais au contraire de la vigilance et de la clart脙漏. Mes amis italiens, qui sont l脙聽 et qui affrontent au quotidien l'extr脙陋me droite modernis脙漏e de Giorgia脗聽Meloni, vivent la nomination de Monsieur脗聽Fitto comme une insulte. Relisez Reconna脙庐tre le fascisme du grand Umberto脗聽Eco et vous comprendrez que, m脙陋me recouvert d'un masque de respectabilit脙漏, le fascisme demeure le fascisme.

Je veux ainsi relayer ici la voix des millions d'Europ脙漏ennes et d'Europ脙漏ens qui refusent le chemin du d脙漏shonneur. Aucun calcul politicien ne peut nous exon脙漏rer de notre devoir de vigilance. L'Europe doit rester un espace de lutte contre le repli nationaliste, qui porte l'id脙漏e de la confrontation permanente des peuples. Face 脙聽 l'extr脙陋me droite, 脙聽 cette extr脙陋me droite x脙漏nophobe, complotiste et climatosceptique, nous devons r脙漏sister, r脙漏sister et r脙漏sister encore. C'est ce que feront les 脙漏cologistes.

Mais vous, Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, apr脙篓s avoir install脙漏 l'extr脙陋me droite 脙聽 la t脙陋te de la Commission europ脙漏enne, que ferez芒聙聯vous?

(L'oratrice refuse une question carton bleu de Francesco Torselli)

脗聽脗聽Irene Montero (The Left). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, la Comisi脙鲁n Europea que hoy se vota es una herida posiblemente mortal al proyecto europeo. Sus prioridades pol脙颅ticas son la guerra, la austeridad y el racismo criminal en las fronteras. Se van a gastar ustedes en armas el dinero que la gente necesita para escuelas, para hospitales, para viviendas, para ciencia o para hacer frente a la emergencia clim脙隆tica.

Adem脙隆s, el pacto que hace posible esta Comisi脙鲁n convierte a la extrema derecha de Meloni y de Orb脙隆n en socios estables, no solo de la derecha, sino tambi脙漏n de la socialdemocracia. Los socialistas, se脙卤ora Ribera, hoy votan a favor de la extrema derecha y gobernar脙隆n juntos en Europa. Es inmoral, dice la se脙卤ora Garc脙颅a, pero ustedes lo van a hacer.

Se脙卤ora von der Leyen, 脗驴qu脙漏 va a hacer para que Europa cumpla con la orden de detenci脙鲁n de Netanyahu? Alemania, Italia y Hungr脙颅a ya han dicho que van a proteger a los genocidas. 脗驴Va a mirar usted para otro lado mientras Europa pisotea el Derecho internacional? La realidad es que Israel no podr脙颅a llevar un a脙卤o de genocidio sin el apoyo de Europa. Su complicidad con los genocidas es criminal, se脙卤ora von der Leyen, y nosotras nos oponemos a este proyecto de guerra, austeridad y genocidio. Vamos a trabajar para que, igual que hace quince脗聽a脙卤os, frente al austericidio, los pueblos de Europa, por su propia supervivencia y en defensa de su dignidad y de sus derechos, tambi脙漏n de la democracia y de la paz, les dijeron 脗芦Basta脗禄, se lo vuelvan a decir ahora.

脗聽脗聽Ewa Zaj脛聟czkowska-Hernik (ESN). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Chcecie pozna脛聡 najbardziej niedemokratyczn脛聟 i niepraworz脛聟dn脛聟 instytucj脛聶 w Unii Europejskiej? Europa, kt脙鲁r脛聟 Pani tworzy, to kontynent hipokryzji i udr脛聶ki dla zwyk脜聜ego cz脜聜owieka. Olbrzymie ceny energii, zakaz wjazdu samochod脙鲁w do miast, kobiety i dzieci b脛聶d脛聟ce ofiarami imigrant脙鲁w, kt脙鲁rym op脜聜aca Pani hotele i zasi脜聜ki z naszych pieni脛聶dzy. Na komisarza do spraw imigracji wybra脜聜a Pani kogo脜聸, kto kompletnie nie ma poj脛聶cia na temat funkcji, jak脛聟 b脛聶dzie sprawowa脜聜. Patrz脛聶 na sk脜聜ad Komisji i widz脛聶 eurokomunizm w najgorszym wydaniu. Zielone szale脜聞stwo, zero konkurencyjno脜聸ci, do脜录ynanie rolnictwa umow脛聟 z Mercosur, ograbienie nas z to脜录samo脜聸ci narodowej. To sko脜聞czy si脛聶 katastrof脛聟. Zamiast zerowych emisji b脛聶dziemy mieli zerowy wzrost gospodarczy. Papierowe s脜聜omki i nakr脛聶tki przymocowane do butelek 芒聙聯 to jest Pani plan na Europ脛聶.
