脗聽脗聽Thomas Geisel (NI). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Ich bef脙录rchte, mit der Kommission, die Frau von der Leyen zusammengestellt hat, wird die Bedeutung Europas weiter zur脙录ckgehen. Sorgen bereitet mir vor allem, wie sich die Kommission au脙聼en芒聙聭 und sicherheitspolitisch aufstellt. Verantwortlich hierf脙录r sind Frau Kallas und Herr Kubilius. Sie vertreten eine Au脙聼enpolitik, die sich klar gegen Russland richtet. Das mag mit Blick auf ihre und die Geschichte ihrer Herkunftsl脙陇nder nachvollziehbar sein, f脙录r Europa ist es verh脙陇ngnisvoll.

Russlands Pr脙陇sident hat ein Nachbarland unter Bruch des V脙露lkerrechts angegriffen. Das ist verwerflich, darf aber nicht zum Anlass genommen werden, alle Br脙录cken zwischen der Europ脙陇ischen Union und Russland, immerhin dem gr脙露脙聼ten europ脙陇ischen Land, auf Dauer abzubrechen und die Gr脙陇ben immer tiefer werden zu lassen. Statt den Krieg in der Ukraine mit immer mehr Waffen weiter zu befeuern und Europa immer mehr aufzur脙录sten, bedarf es einer neuen Ostpolitik der Europ脙陇ischen Union, die die Spaltung Europas 脙录berwindet und so die Voraussetzungen schafft, dass Europa im Konzert der Weltm脙陇chte 脙录berhaupt noch eine Rolle spielt. Europa muss f脙录r Frieden in Europa sorgen. Wir d脙录rfen das nicht den Vereinigten Staaten von Amerika 脙录berlassen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Siegfried Mure脜聼an (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, President von der Leyen, welcome back to the European Parliament. We have 26 members of the European Commission who have successfully passed the hearings in the European Parliament with large majorities. Today we are giving 芒聙聯 with a large pro-European majority of democratic forces 芒聙聯 the support and the vote to this European Commission. We are going to work together as the European Parliament with the new members of the European Commission.

We will bring Europe forward. We will deliver. We will construct. We heard what the people have expressed at the European elections. We heard people on the 9 June saying they want Europe to protect them; they want a Europe that protects, a Europe that concentrates on security, on defence, on protecting our borders.

We will build a Europe which will be more competitive with a stronger economy, because competitiveness means safe jobs and this is essential to safeguard our high social standards in Europe.

We will accelerate the transition to the digital economy. We will continue the transition to the green economy but with the industry not against, with the farmers not against.

And we will strengthen our neighbours, our neighbouring countries, because we can only be safe in Europe if our neighbouring countries, many of them EU candidate countries, can be safe.

And one of the best instruments to deliver will be the budget of the European Union. We will have a budget which will be clearly aligned in the next years with the priorities of the Union: safety, security, competitiveness, agriculture and supporting our neighbouring countries. A budget that will be easier, simpler, less bureaucratic, and a budget which will be in line with our values. Whoever wants to receive European funds has to respect European values. The rule of law will matter. This is what we will do: a budget as an essential tool to help citizens in good times, but also in times of crisis.

To achieve all this, we are going to give 芒聙聯 with a large pro-European majority 芒聙聯 the vote to this European Commission now and we will start the work.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ren脙漏 Repasi (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Frau Kommissionspr脙陇sidentin! In der von Ihnen vorgeschlagenen Kommission darf erneut jede Regierung einen Kommissar stellen. Daraus aber zu schlie脙聼en, dass Sie in diesem Spektrum frei die Mehrheiten wechseln k脙露nnen, ist ein Trugschluss. Das degradiert dieses Haus zu einem Notar der Kommission. Eine Selbstentleibung, die wir bei den Anh脙露rungen bereits erleben mussten. Wer lieber knappe Mehrheiten mit den Rechtsextremen bildet, anstatt mit uns stabile proeurop脙陇ische L脙露sung zu erarbeiten, der darf sich nicht wundern, wenn wir unsere Unterst脙录tzung irgendwann verweigern.

Vor dem Sommer hat die SPD Ihnen einen Vertrauensvorschuss gegeben, damit Sie eine Kommission entlang der sie tragenden politischen Kr脙陇fte aufbauen. Mit der Bef脙露rderung von Herrn Fitto zum Vizepr脙陇sidenten und der seitdem von Ihrer Parteienfamilie, der EVP, praktizierten Mehrheitsbildung mit Rechtsextremen gegen die Parteien Ihrer urspr脙录nglichen proeurop脙陇ischen Mehrheit, ist f脙录r uns ein Punkt gekommen, an dem wir Ihnen sagen m脙录ssen: Das geht zu weit, nehmen Sie das ernst.

Tak脜戮e budeme m脙颅t p脜聶脙颅le脜戮itost skute脛聧n脛聸 opravit n脛聸kter脙漏 v脛聸ci tak, abychom neztr脙隆celi konkurenceschopnost a ve sv脛聸t脛聸 obst脙隆li.

脗聽脗聽Marie-Pierre Vedrenne (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, aujourd'hui marque l'aboutissement d'un processus d脙漏mocratique unique en Europe: auditionner des candidats et les 脙漏lire afin de donner 脙聽 la Commission europ脙漏enne la l脙漏gitimit脙漏 d'agir, en concertation avec nous. Pas pour h脙漏siter, mais pour avancer, vite et fort.

Au nom de la d脙漏l脙漏gation L'Europe Ensemble, que je pr脙漏side, ch脙篓re Ursula, j'avais 脙漏voqu脙漏 avec vous, lors de notre 脙漏change, nos trois priorit脙漏s. La souverainet脙漏: face aux menaces de Moscou et vu le retour de Donald脗聽Trump, l'Europe doit assumer sa propre s脙漏curit脙漏 et avancer sur sa d脙漏fense commune. La prosp脙漏rit脙漏: alors que des plans sociaux se multiplient, nous devons soutenir massivement nos entreprises et investir pour des emplois de qualit脙漏 et d'avenir. La comp脙漏titivit脙漏: face aux 脙聣tats-Unis et 脙聽 la Chine, l'Europe, qui n'est certes plus na脙炉ve, doit d脙漏fendre ses industries avec force et sans complexe.

Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, nous voterons pour ce coll脙篓ge de commissaires. Constructifs, nous vous assurons notre soutien. Nous serons des partenaires, mais des partenaires exigeants, parce que l'Europe reste l'une des plus grandes r脙漏alisations politiques et historiques. Mais l'Europe doit inspirer, elle doit surprendre, elle doit s'imposer. Alors, ensemble, travaillons pour cela.

脗聽脗聽Damian Boeselager (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, even a failed German Government started with a happy photo at the beginning. But when I look around here, I see that the coalition is already fuming. And that is because, Mr Weber, you chose to strong-arm the social democrats and the liberals into a coalition with Meloni, and because you decided on reforestation and budget votes with the German AfD. What a great start.

Why are you so afraid to accept the Greens into a formal coalition? Why are you so afraid? I can tell you, I sit with them for five years now: they don't bite. Mr Weber, stop choosing the far right over the Greens and Volt.

Dear Commission President, looking at the US, Ukraine, Germany and France now, and even Romania, we really need a strong European government. We at Volt will therefore stay pragmatic, and we have asked for the group to stay pragmatic, because we need this government to be strong.

But please, don't accept the Union as it is. Advance it, reform it, so that European citizens and this House can nominate and vote for the European government.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kathleen Funchion (The Left). 芒聙聯 A Uachtar脙隆in, after all the drama and the delays, the deal is done and we will have five more years of Ursula von der Leyen. President von der Leyen, you are unacceptable to us on the left, and you are unacceptable to the Irish people, as an enabler of an ongoing genocide against the Palestinian people.

What's more, your Commission is hell bent on passing the Mercosur Agreement 芒聙聯 an agreement which is bad for farmers and for our environment. Your vision of the EU is one where workers rights and solidarity are just footnotes to your main agenda of militarisation and centralisation of powers in Brussels' hands.

I want to speak directly to the Irish MEPs of Fine Gael, Fianna F脙隆il and the Labour Party. We see you and the Irish people are watching you very closely. It is not too late to do the right thing and vote against this Commission. The people of Ireland do not want this agenda 芒聙聯 shame on any Irish MEP who supports it.

A demokr脙隆cia addig van itt m脜卤k脙露d脙漏sben, ameddig a szavaz脙隆s az 脙聳n脙露k 脙隆ltal elv脙隆rt eredm脙漏nyt hozza, m脙隆sk脙录l脙露nben egyszer脜卤en elhalasztj脙隆k azt. Csak azok lehetnek a Bizotts脙隆g tagjai, akiket a f脙露der脙隆lis globalista k脙露r脙露k j脙鲁v脙隆hagynak. Ellenv脙漏lem脙漏ny kiz脙隆rva. A Bizotts脙隆g eln脙露k脙漏nek tiszts脙漏g脙漏re is csak egy s脙潞lyos korrupci脙鲁s v脙隆dakkal terhelt jel脙露ltb脜聭l lehetett v脙隆lasztani. A fej脙漏t脜聭l b脜卤zlik a hal. Most m脙隆r nem csak az vil脙隆gos, hogy Eur脙鲁pa legkorruptabb szervezete a Bizotts脙隆g, hanem az is, hogy egyben a legantidemokratikusabb.

脗聽脗聽Tomas Tob脙漏 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Mer 脙陇n fem m脙楼nader efter Europavalet s脙楼 beh脙露ver en ny kommission komma p脙楼 plats. Europa beh脙露ver ledas. Nu 脙陇r inte tid f脙露r plakatpolitik, utan det 脙陇r faktiskt dags att b脙露rja jobba. Men det vill uppenbarligen inte ytterkanterna till h脙露ger eller till v脙陇nster i det h脙陇r huset. 脗聽脗聽Rapha脙芦l Glucksmann (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, il y avait une coalition pro-europ脙漏enne pour vous soutenir, en juillet. Pourquoi, d脙篓s lors, int脙漏grer Fratelli d'Italia au groupe des vice-pr脙漏sidents qui pilotent, politiquement, la Commission? Rien ne vous y obligeait et nous ne l'acceptons pas. Et pourquoi le PPE s'allie-t-il 脙聽 l'extr脙陋me droite la plus radicale pour d脙漏manteler la directive sur la d脙漏forestation?

La politique d脙漏mocratique, vous l'avez dit, est un art du compromis. Mais ce n'est pas un art de la compromission. Alors, pour soutenir massivement la r脙漏sistance ukrainienne, pour construire l'Europe de la d脙漏fense, pour d脙漏fendre le pacte vert europ脙漏en, pour d脙漏fendre la souverainet脙漏 脙漏conomique de l'Europe, d脙漏velopper une politique industrielle, renforcer les solidarit脙漏s, avancer sur le chemin de l'脙漏galit脙漏, nous serons 脙聽 vos c脙麓t脙漏s.

Mais 脙聽 chaque fois que se reformera cette coalition contre-nature, m脙陋lant les h脙漏ritiers suppos脙漏s d'Adenauer avec les forces les plus anti-europ脙漏ennes de ce Parlement, eh bien, nous serons l脙聽 pour lui tenir t脙陋te et pour r脙漏sister.

脗聽脗聽Paolo Borchia (PfE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, meno male che questo dibattito ha riscaldato un po' l'atmosfera in Aula, perch脙漏 questa mattina ho visto un'atmosfera freddina, sfiduciata, dove ogni gruppo applaudiva i propri commissari. Diciamo un segnale di debolezza. Una maggioranza sfilacciata non 脙篓 un buon modo per iniziare questo percorso.

Ecco, tranne poche lodevoli eccezioni 芒聙聯 faccio un in bocca al lupo ai commissari Fitto, V脙隆rhelyi, Zaharieva 芒聙聯 mi spiace dire che ci sono troppi commissari che sono stati nominati non per merito ma per compiacere la voglia di comandare di partiti, partitini e gruppi politici.脗聽Ecco, tutti preoccupati di apparire vincitori piuttosto che di trovare i profili migliori per il futuro di questa Europa.

Pensando agli imprenditori, agli agricoltori, ai giovani, con onest脙聽 e con tanta coerenza, la Lega ribadisce il suo no al bis di Ursula von der Leyen.

脗聽脗聽Gheorghe Piperea (ECR). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, chiar 脙庐n acest Parlament, doamna von der Leyen a f脛聝cut o declara脠聸ie 脙庐n iulie, pronun脠聸脙垄nd un cli脠聶eu: 芒聙聻nu vom l脛聝sa pe nimeni 脙庐n urm脛聝芒聙聺. Primordial, asta ar 脙庐nsemna c脛聝 Uniunea European脛聝 ar trebui s脛聝 aib脛聝 grij脛聝 脙庐n primul r脙垄nd, primordial, de cet脛聝脠聸enii Uniunii Europene care tr脛聝iesc la limita s脛聝r脛聝ciei. 脙聨n 2019, s脛聝racii europeni erau 脙庐n num脛聝r de 100脗聽de脗聽milioane, iar, 脙庐n 2023, de 102脗聽milioane. Dintre ace脠聶ti oameni care au fost l脛聝sa脠聸i 脙庐n urm脛聝, 6,5脗聽milioane sunt cet脛聝脠聸eni rom脙垄ni. Au contat mai mult mirajul schimb脛聝rii climatice, r脛聝zboiul, afacerile companiilor farmaceutice, bancare 脠聶i tehnologice, inclusiv vaccinurile inutile, cu emblematicul Pfizergate 脙庐n frunte 芒聙聯 exist脛聝 un dosar penal cu acest nume.

脙聨n noua sa formul脛聝, Comisia European脛聝 se 脙庐ndep脛聝rteaz脛聝 tot mai mult de proiectul originar al Uniunii Europene. Noul colegiu al comisarilor a rezervat un tren special pentru elita neofeudal脛聝 a Uniunii Europene. Cet脛聝脠聸enii obi脠聶nui脠聸i vor fi l脛聝sa脠聸i pe peronul g脛聝rii. De aceea, votul meu este contra 脙庐nvestirii acestei Comisii.

脗聽脗聽Martin Hojs脙颅k (Renew). 芒聙聯 Dear Madam President, dear College-designates, the new Commission steps in in a time of great challenge. We need the green transition to ensure that our planet remains inhabitable. We need socially just solutions which lead to prosperity while building a resilient and competitive Union on the global stage. These are no small talks, and the eyes of Europeans are on you to deliver.

At the same time, we must ensure that the Union remains safe and secure, standing strong against those who threaten our values or seek to divide us. We all want peace. Peace 芒聙聯 not to surrender to the aggressor, not to surrender our freedoms and liberties.

And finally, and above all, you have a responsibility to protect liberal democracy. To protect and enforce the rule of law, protect strong civil society, protect independent media, not to appease or compromise with those who threaten them, because we can lose our democracy in a moment.

Dear College, the work ahead is massive. We are ready to work with you, but also control you and hold you accountable for the delivery of your commitments.

脗聽脗聽Gordan Bosanac (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjednice, po脜隆tovana predsjednice Europske komisije, u srpnju ove godine dobili ste podr脜隆ku ovog Doma ve脛聡inom zastupnika iz redova proeuropskih grupacija.

Dobit 脛聡ete glasove onih koji ne po脜隆tuju vladavinu prava. Moj glas protiv, kao i dobrog dijela kolega iz moje grupe, poruka je otpora ovom ozbiljnom presedanu.

Iako smatram da u sastavu nove Komisije ima kvalitetnih povjerenika, kao 脜隆to u najavama programa prepoznajem neke va脜戮ne poteze, hvatanje uko脜隆tac s krizom stanovanja, nastavak rada na borbi protiv klimatskih promjena, ja nisam uvjeren da, upravo zbog koketiranja s antidemokratskim snagama, prijedlozi politika koji 脛聡e dolaziti od Vas 脛聡e biti u smjeru boljeg 脜戮ivota za sve u Europskoj uniji.

Paradoksalno, moj glas protiv je ujedno i glas za. Za sve one koji su spremni boriti se za o脛聧uvanje europskih vrijednosti na kojima smo do sada gradili ovu Europsku uniju. Nema predaje!

脗聽脗聽Romana Tomc (PPE). 芒聙聯 Gospa predsednica. Danes odlo脛聧amo o ekipi, ki bo v naslednjih letih skrbela za dobrobit Evropske unije. 脛聦esa se pri tem bojim?

Kaj od Komisije pri脛聧akujem? Da boste za脜隆脛聧itili evropski na脛聧in 脜戮ivljenja. Da boste za脜隆脛聧itili na脜隆e evropske meje. 脗聽脗聽Jorge Buxad脙漏 Villalba (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidente, se脙卤ora von der Leyen, esta Comisi脙鲁n nace herida de muerte. 脗聽脗聽Bert-Jan Ruissen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, collega's, de hele gang van zaken rond de benoeming van de nieuwe Commissie verdient voorwaar geen schoonheidsprijs. Het hele proces werd gedomineerd door partijpolitieke overwegingen, terwijl we de nieuwe commissarissen toch primair op hun bekwaamheden moeten beoordelen.

We zagen dat heel duidelijk in de hoorzitting met de heer V脙隆rhelyi. Hij liet zien dat hij zijn dossiers uitstekend kende, maar omdat hij zich niet expliciet uitsprak voor abortus, heeft het Parlement nu een deel van zijn portefeuille afgepakt. Triest, dieptriest: een ander woord heb ik daar niet voor. Als je laat doorschemeren dat je ruimte wilt laten voor de lidstaten om het kwetsbare ongeboren leven in de moederschoot te beschermen, dan word je door dit parlement 芒聙聯 en mevrouw von脗聽der脗聽Leyen accepteert de hele gang van zaken 芒聙聯 in de verdachtenbank gezet. Dergelijke dubieuze deals die de bescherming van het ongeboren leven ondermijnen, kan ik onmogelijk steunen.

Voor mij is dat een van de redenen waarom ik niet met de benoeming van deze Commissie kan instemmen.

脗聽脗聽Daniel Caspary (PPE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Selbst die italienischen Sozialdemokraten werden heute f脙录r die Kommission stimmen, w脙陇hrend Sie mal wieder es besser wissen und sich damit in der eigenen Fraktion isolieren. Ich kann deshalb nur alle aufrufen: Wir brauchen dringend Handlungsf脙陇higkeit in Europa. Wir brauchen die starke Kommission, die Ursula von der Leyen vorgestellt hat. 脗聽脗聽Marta Temido (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, a vota脙搂脙拢o neste Col脙漏gio de Comiss脙隆rios 脙漏, para muitos de n脙鲁s, uma dura prova.

N脙拢o fomos n脙鲁s que escolhemos ignorar o princ脙颅pio da paridade na sua composi脙搂脙拢o. N脙拢o escolhemos atribuir uma vice-presid脙陋ncia 脙聽 direita radical. N脙拢o escolhemos entregar pastas centrais para os europeus e que atingem os direitos das mulheres e raparigas europeias a quem por elas nutre apenas indiferen脙搂a.脗聽Mas sabemos entender a excecionalidade dos tempos que vivemos.

Compreendemos as amea脙搂as que a Uni脙拢o Europeia enfrenta externamente, como compreendemos as amea脙搂as que a minam por dentro, do crescimento dos nacionalismos ao cont脙隆gio de uma recess脙拢o econ脙鲁mica. Vamos hoje votar um Col脙漏gio de Comiss脙隆rios, que em muitos casos n脙拢o seria o nosso, mas que deve trabalhar com todos e para todos. Num contexto em que das institui脙搂脙碌es se espera que sejam capazes de resolver problemas e transmitir confian脙搂a aos cidad脙拢os.

Recordo que as expectativas dos europeus s脙拢o altas e que no passado mostr脙隆mos que somos capazes. Habita脙搂脙拢o, emprego, ambiente, energia, competitividade, defesa, Estado de direito, ordem internacional baseada em regras.脗聽Se n脙拢o estivermos 脙聽 altura destas expectativas, ser脙隆 o projeto europeu que fragilizamos. Isso implica implementar, sem hesita脙搂脙碌es nem recuo, o acordo de coopera脙搂脙拢o assumido entre os europe脙颅stas.

Senhora Presidente, o nosso escrut脙颅nio do trabalho do Col脙漏gio come脙搂a hoje, ap脙鲁s a vota脙搂脙拢o. Mais do que nunca, desejamos-lhe boa sorte.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marieke Ehlers (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, the hearings of Commissioners芒聙聭designate have veered into the troubling territory of politicisation. Instead of concentrating on qualifications and vision, they are increasingly influenced by partisan rivalries and secret political deals. This not only undermines the integrity of the process, but also the trust of our citizens and the very principles of democracy we seek to uphold.

Member States have the exclusive right to nominate Commissioners. The European Parliament's oversight role is vital, but cannot negate the role of the Member States. To allow bias against certain national governments to influence our decisions is to betray the trust of the European people.

We must also address the glaring lack of transparency. Postponing evaluation meetings, holding closed芒聙聭door sessions and excluding key stakeholders from the process flies in the face of our commitment to accountability and transparency.

We must commit to a Europe where the sovereignty of Member States is respected, and where transparency and accountability are non芒聙聭negotiable. The future of Europe demands nothing less.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Paulo Cunha (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, o or脙搂amento 脙漏 a base do futuro da Uni脙拢o Europeia. Sem um or脙搂amento ambicioso, n脙拢o temos como responder aos desafios que enfrentamos, nem como garantir o crescimento sustent脙隆vel e equilibrado entre todos os Estados-Membros.

Precisamos de um or脙搂amento forte e focado na competitividade. Um or脙搂amento que preserve pol脙颅ticas como a Pol脙颅tica de Coes脙拢o e a Pol脙颅tica Agr脙颅cola Comum, fundamentais para fortalecer a converg脙陋ncia regional e a competitividade europeia.

A competitividade da Europa n脙拢o pode ficar ref脙漏m de or脙搂amentos limitados. Devemos explorar novos recursos pr脙鲁prios e o investimento privado deve ser incentivado.脗聽E mais, a competitividade requer um compromisso claro com a inova脙搂脙拢o, os avan脙搂os tecnol脙鲁gicos e a transforma脙搂脙拢o digital e verde. Mas tamb脙漏m precisamos de combater a burocracia que sufoca as nossas pequenas e m脙漏dias empresas.

O or脙搂amento 脙漏 mais do que n脙潞meros. 脙聣 o motor da competitividade europeia. Sem ambi脙搂脙拢o, n脙拢o h脙隆 coes脙拢o nem lideran脙搂a global. Mas, com vis脙拢o, h脙隆 modelo social e um futuro para cada cidad脙拢o.

脗聽脗聽Hel脙漏ne Fritzon (S&D). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! De kommande fem 脙楼ren h脙陇r i Europaparlamentet kommer sannerligen att bli en utmaning, det visar sig redan nu inf脙露r dagens val av kommission.脗聽Det 脙陇r en provokation att ordf脙露rande von der Leyen f脙露resl脙楼r en h脙露gerextrem kandidat fr脙楼n Italien att vara hennes vice ordf脙露rande. F脙露r oss svenska socialdemokrater 脙陇r det avg脙露rande att de moderata och konservativa krafterna st脙楼r f脙露r sina l脙露ften till oss n脙陇r vi nu v脙陇ljer att ta ansvar och st脙露dja kommissionen, f脙露r vi v脙陇ljer ju tillsammans ett brett samarbete i den breda mitten med Europav脙陇nliga partier. Vi g脙露r det f脙露r demokrati och s脙陇kerhet, f脙露r klimatet och milj脙露n. Vi g脙露r det f脙露r jobben och j脙陇mst脙陇lldheten. Vi beh脙露ver ett starkt EU som forts脙陇tter att ta ledarskap i en tid av krig och kriser, och vi g脙露r det f脙露r v脙楼ra barns och barnbarns framtid. 脗聽脗聽Tom Vandendriessche (PfE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, het signaal van de kiezer was overduidelijk. Onze mensen willen meer koopkracht en minder migratie. Maar die politieke elite, die doet gewoon verder alsof er helemaal niets gebeurd is. Deze Commissie芒聙聭Von脗聽der脗聽Leyen is dan ook gewoon meer van hetzelfde. Meer migratie en meer klimaatwaanzin. Deze Commissie is niet alleen volledig losgekomen van de bevolking, maar ook van de realiteit. De rest van de wereld gaat immers helemaal niet mee in die Green脗聽Deal, die opengrenzenpolitiek of die krankzinnige oorlogsretoriek van Ursula von der Leyen.

Nog voor ze gestart is, behoort deze Commissie daarom nu al tot het verleden. Want overal kiest het volk voor een andere weg: die van vrede, die van welvaart en die van het gezond verstand. En dat is de weg van de nationalisten. En daarom zijn wij de eersten van morgen en is deze Commissie芒聙聭Von脗聽der脗聽Leyen de laatste van gisteren.

脗聽脗聽Karlo Ressler (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjednice, po脜隆tovana predsjednice Komisije, kolegice i kolege, Europa nema vremena za lamentacije, Europa nema vremena za gubljenje. Za osigurati mir, prosperitet i napredak, morat 脛聡e biti sna脜戮nija i dinami脛聧nija nego danas.

Hvala vam i sretno na tom putu!

脗聽脗聽Alex Agius Saliba (S&D). 芒聙聯 Ninsab kburi li l-kulle脛隆脛隆 li jmiss se jkollu Kummissarju Malti b芒聙聶portafoll li jirrefletti l-prijoritajiet progressivi tal-politika tag脛搂na, rwol uniku li se j脛隆ib ideat u perspettivi 脛隆odda madwar il-mejda.

L-a脛搂脛搂ar i脜录da mhux l-inqas, se jqieg脛搂ed il-kultura, l-isport, is-sa脛搂脛搂a mentali u s-cyberbullying fi脛聥-脛聥entru tal-portafoll tieg脛搂u, u b芒聙聶hekk ji脜录gura li dawn l-aspetti importanti tas-so脛聥jet脙聽 tag脛搂na jing脛搂ataw l-attenzjoni u r-ri脜录orsi li jixirqilhom. 脗聽脗聽Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Frau Kommissionspr脙陇sidentin! Es ist gut, dass Sie am 1.脗聽Dezember mit Ihrer Kommission starten k脙露nnen, und es ist auch notwendig, dass Sie den Schwerpunkt auf Wettbewerb und Wirtschaft legen. Das war die Erfolgsgeschichte der Europ脙陇ischen Union, und das muss die Erfolgsgeschichte bleiben. Nur mit starken wirtschaftlichen Leistungen k脙露nnen wir auch unser europ脙陇isches Lebensmodell f脙录r unsere B脙录rgerinnen und B脙录rger finanzieren. Weniger B脙录rokratie, mehr Innovation und Forschung, auch ein umfassender Kapitalmarkt sind hier unbedingt notwendig. Diese Kapitalmarktunion ist hier eine Grundvoraussetzung.

Gleichzeitig m脙录ssen wir aber auch unsere Anstrengungen verst脙陇rken, um unsere Sicherheit zu gew脙陇hrleisten. Von Putin芒聙聭Russland geht hier eine gro脙聼e Gefahr aus. Wir brauchen hier mehr f脙录r unsere Verteidigung. Wir brauchen aber keine Freundschaftsvertr脙陇ge, wie sie die Freiheitliche Partei 脙聳sterreichs mit der Partei von Putin abgeschlossen hat. Und wir brauchen nachhaltige europ脙陇ische L脙露sungen bei der illegalen Migration. Unser EU芒聙聭Kommissar脗聽芒聙聯 aus 脙聳sterreich kommend, Magnus Brunner脗聽芒聙聯 braucht ja auch die Unterst脙录tzung der 27脗聽Nationalstaaten.

Ein letzter Punkt: Die Europ脙陇ische Union wird auch dann gest脙陇rkt脗聽芒聙聯 wirtschaftlich und sicherheitspolitisch脗聽芒聙聯, wenn der Westbalkan hier bei uns seine Heimat findet 芒聙聯 in der Europ脙陇ischen Union.

脗聽脗聽Christel Schaldemose (S&D). 芒聙聯 Fru formand! Fru Kommissionsformand! Jeg stemmer for den nye Europa-Kommission i dag. Er Kommissionen og dens politik perfekt? Nej! Flere sp脙赂rgsm脙楼l om vores klima og vores klode, for eksempel, mangler stadig svar. Men vi er lykkedes med at presse Kommissionen til at give vigtige politiske indr脙赂mmelser. Jeg gik til valg p脙楼 at sikre tryghed for b脙赂rn og unge online, for problemerne der er alvorlige. Det har den nye Kommission lovet at g脙赂re noget ved. Vi vil ogs脙楼 gerne have forbedrede krav til offentlige indk脙赂b. Den kamp bliver ikke nem, men Kommissionen har lovet at kigge p脙楼 det, s脙楼 vi kan f脙楼 bedre regler for l脙赂nmodtagere og for milj脙赂et. Nu skal vi i gang med arbejdet. Et samlet Europa er vores st脙娄rkeste forsvar mod trusler udefra og vores allerbedste v脙楼ben mod klimaforandringerne. En ny Europa-Kommission er kun begyndelsen p脙楼 de n脙娄ste fem 脙楼rs h脙楼rde arbejde og slid for at sikre et tryggere, et gr脙赂nnere og et mere retf脙娄rdigt Europa. Lad os nu komme i gang!

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lud脛聸k Niedermayer (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, we are almost at the end of the debate, so let me just say two remarks. The first: in the debate, I guess we learned how people interpret the democracy, how they interpret the elections and how they think about the future of our common Europe. And this was very sad. It shows how much the responsibility is lacking on parts of the politics in the 21st century.

And the second comment, to you, Madam President: I guess we do not need to tell you that the times will be very difficult. You have experience with that, and we should learn how we managed the severe crises in the past few years. We must keep focus. It is not time to do, nice to have. We must really focus on things that deliver, do them well and do them quickly. You can count on that, on my support and the support of my colleagues from my delegation. So I wish you good luck, to all of us.

脗聽脗聽Angelika Niebler (PPE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Frau Kommissionspr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen, liebe Kollegen! Wir brauchen eine handlungsf脙陇hige Kommission. Die Herausforderungen, vor denen wir hier in Europa stehen, sind gewaltig, sind heute schon vielfach angesprochen worden: Seit 脙录ber 1脗聽000脗聽Tagen Krieg in der Ukraine, die Trump芒聙聭Administration wird am 20.脗聽Januar ins Amt gehen, viele Regionen schotten sich ab, erheben Z脙露lle, unserer Wirtschaft geht es schlecht, Unternehmen bauen in ganz Europa Tausende von Stellen ab. Deshalb kann ich nur alle Kolleginnen und Kollegen noch einmal auffordern, heute die Kommission zu unterst脙录tzen, heute die Kommission zu best脙陇tigen und sich damit auch klar zu einem starken gemeinsamen Europa zu bekennen.

Liebe Kolleginnen, liebe Kollegen! Wir k脙露nnen im Nachgang zu der Best脙陇tigung heute der Kommission jeden einzelnen Kommissar und jede einzelne Kommissarin kritisch unter die Lupe nehmen, wenn ich das so sagen darf. Es ist ja noch nicht vorbei mit der Best脙陇tigung, aber heute bitte ich Sie, wirklich noch einmal in sich zu gehen, all diejenigen, die noch Zweifel haben, mit Blick auf das, was in der Welt vor uns liegt und wie wir in der Welt wahrgenommen werden. Bitte geben Sie heute der Kommission mit Ihrem Votum die Best脙陇tigung, die wir brauchen. Danach werden wir weiterhin unsere Aufgabe machen, kritisch hinterfragen, anmahnen. Wenn die politischen Schwerpunkte, die wir uns in der neuen Kommission w脙录nschen, nicht kommen, dann werden wir sicher unsere Arbeit leisten. Aber heute muss ein Signal der Geschlossenheit und der starken Unterst脙录tzung f脙录r eine starke Kommission und ein starkes Europa von diesem Parlament hier ausgehen und darum bitte ich alle Kolleginnen und Kollegen.

脗聽脗聽Micha脜聜 Szczerba (PPE). 芒聙聯 Szanowna Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca Komisji! To bardzo wa脜录ny moment dla bezpiecze脜聞stwa Europy. 脗聽脗聽Nils U脜隆akovs (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, the previous mandate of the European Parliament and the Commission began with Brexit, and municipalities located at the western border of the European Union were particularly affected by the departure of the United Kingdom As a result, a special financial instrument was established, providing up to EUR脗聽5 billion in support for these municipalities.

This mandate starts with the continued war in Ukraine and municipalities at the eastern border of the European Union, like Latgale, in Latvia and now literally living by the wall, bearing significant economic and social costs. When you are forced to construct anti-tank infrastructure in your region, private investment obviously tend to avoid those areas. Eastern border regions currently receive no support comparable to what the western border regions have been granted. This glaring inequality must be addressed by the House and by the new Commission that will be presumably elected today.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Silvia Sardone (PfE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, con orgoglio diciamo un deciso "no" a Ursula von der Leyen. "No" a questa maggioranza, "no" a questa Commissione europea.

Lo diciamo perch脙漏 il mondo sta cambiando direzione ma l'Unione europea, inspiegabilmente, insiste su politiche sbagliate che la stanno indebolendo. Negli Stati Uniti, con la vittoria di Trump, si punta a fermare i conflitti, a porre fine alle follie politiche climatiche, a snellire l'opprimente macchina burocratica e a ridurre il peso fiscale.

A Bruxelles, al contrario, si spinge per alimentare le guerre, per moltiplicare politiche green disastrose e per imporre una regolamentazione ossessiva che soffoca imprese e famiglie. Si sta portando avanti la rovina di interi settori, come l'industria europea dell'auto, che 脙篓 gi脙聽 in ginocchio.

Abbiamo gi脙聽 vissuto l'esperienza von der Leyen e si 脙篓 rivelata un fallimento, come confermato dagli stessi elettori. L'Europa merita di meglio. L'Europa deve cambiare strada. L'Europa deve cambiare ora.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, Euroopalle on nyt tarjolla uusi komissio, joka t脙陇t脙陇 maanosaa l脙陇htisi luotsaamaan vaikeassa tilanteessa. Kysymys on nyt siit脙陇, ett脙陇 luotammeko me luotsitaitoihin. Olen tehnyt arvion ja minun vastaukseni on, ett脙陇 en luota.

Mit脙陇 Eurooppa nyt tarvitsisi kaikkein kipeimmin? Ukrainan paljon voimakkaampaa tukemista, siirtolaisvy脙露ryn torppaamista ja rajojen suojaamista, Eurooppa-my脙露nteist脙陇 ilmastopolitiikkaa sek脙陇 s脙陇脙陇ntelyn purkutalkoot. Mutta tulemmeko n脙陇kem脙陇脙陇n kaikkea t脙陇t脙陇? Kun katson kulunutta viitt脙陇 vuotta, niin mist脙陇脙陇n n脙陇ist脙陇 en anna komissiolle puhtaita papereita. Joissain on jopa katastrofaalisesti ep脙陇onnistuttu.

Mit脙陇s nyt sitten? Puikoissa jatkaa sama johtaja eik脙陇 suuria tarvittavia linjanmuutoksia todistettu kuulemisissa. Vaikuttaa silt脙陇, ett脙陇 olemme saamassa jatko-osan. Toivon, ett脙陇 jatko-osa ei ole edelt脙陇j脙陇脙陇ns脙陇 huonompi, kuten monesti on, koska silloin olisimme todella pulassa.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hilde Vautmans (Renew). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, voor u staat een overtuigde liberale Europeaan die vandaag toch heel gelukkig is, mevrouw von脗聽der脗聽Leyen.

U heeft twee klemtonen gelegd waar wij al heel lang naar vragen. E脙漏n: u heeft de focus gelegd op jobs en economie, en er de nadruk op gelegd om de jobs in Europa te houden. Twee: de focus op veiligheid. Eindelijk een Europese commissaris voor Defensie en een ongelooflijk sterke vrouw als onze hoge vertegenwoordiger van de Unie voor buitenlandse zaken en veiligheidsbeleid. Daar zijn wij als liberalen heel erg blij mee.

Alleen moet ik u eerlijk zeggen: u heeft u hier getoond 芒聙聯 en daar ben ik ook blij mee 芒聙聯 als een echte Europese leider, een leidster. U zocht naar een breed draagvlak. U zorgt dat Europa verenigd is. En dat gaan we moeten zijn. Als we kijken naar de dreigingen die op ons afkomen, is er misschien 脙漏脙漏n klein minpuntje: u heeft niet gesproken over een Europees leger. U weet, ik zal dat hier blijven herhalen tot we daar naartoe kunnen werken. Ik denk wel dat u zich bewust bent van de urgentie. Zorg nu ook dat die woorden daden worden. U heeft onze steun.

脗聽脗聽Leoluca Orlando (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, il 19 luglio Lei ha avuto una larga maggioranza con i voti contrari dell'estrema destra.

Adesso una incomprensibile svolta in coalizione con l'estrema destra, con gruppi sovranisti, con pulsioni liberticide e avverse a processi di pace, diritti, giustizia ambientale e sociale, in aperto contrasto con le ragioni fondative dell'Europa democratica, antifascista e inclusiva.

Voter脙虏 "no", come tanti, per evitare che venga legittimata un'estrema destra, inaccettabile e pericolosa per l'Unione e per i singoli Stati membri.

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left). 芒聙聯 Senhora von der Leyen, o voto contra esta Comiss脙拢o Europeia significa a rejei脙搂脙拢o do seu programa pol脙颅tico.

Os povos precisam de paz, de coopera脙搂脙拢o. Precisam de coes脙拢o social, de melhores sal脙隆rios e pens脙碌es, de acesso 脙聽 habita脙搂脙拢o, 脙聽 sa脙潞de, 脙聽 educa脙搂脙拢o, 脙聽 prote脙搂脙拢o social, de resposta aos problemas ambientais.

A Comiss脙拢o insiste em pol脙颅ticas de militarismo, guerra e confronta脙搂脙拢o; em pol脙颅ticas que negam direitos sociais e laborais e que transformam em neg脙鲁cio todas as condi脙搂脙碌es de que depende uma vida justa e digna para quem trabalha ou trabalhou uma vida inteira.

Os povos precisam de economias orientadas para a satisfa脙搂脙拢o das suas necessidades, do aproveitamento dos recursos produtivos e o desenvolvimento nacional. A Comiss脙拢o insiste em pol脙颅ticas da designada competitividade, com mais liberaliza脙搂脙拢o de mercados, mais privatiza脙搂脙碌es e ainda maior favorecimento dos grupos econ脙鲁micos, das multinacionais e dos bancos.

Os povos precisam de mais democracia, progresso social e desenvolvimento. Afirmando essa alternativa que serve os povos, rejeitamos a Comiss脙拢o e as suas pol脙颅ticas.

脗聽脗聽Diego Solier (NI). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, voy a dejar unos comentarios y unas preguntas.

Estamos viendo la situaci脙鲁n de nuestro tejido industrial, muy mala, a la que nos han llevado las pol脙颅ticas realizadas en los 脙潞ltimos cinco脗聽a脙卤os. 脗驴Vamos a seguir por ese mismo camino?

脗聽脗聽Maria Grapini (S&D). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝 von der Leyen, ave脠聸i o echip脛聝 nou脛聝. Vreau s脛聝 le urez succes tuturor. 脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anna Maria Cisint (PfE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, questa Commissione 脙篓 un grave tradimento della volont脙聽 manifestata dagli elettori, che hanno chiesto invece una svolta delle politiche europee che hanno emarginato il nostro continente nei mercati globali, messo in ginocchio la nostra agricoltura e le nostre produzioni tipiche, alterato i valori identitari e occidentali con politiche permissive e la tolleranza verso tutti gli integralismi delle culture islamiche.

Mentre si tenta di emarginare il terzo gruppo per rappresentanza politica, quella dei Patrioti, si ricostruisce l'ambiguit脙聽 di una Commissione che sin d'ora mostra i limiti della precariet脙聽 e della volont脙聽 di continuare sotto la pressione dei socialisti e delle sinistre.

I programmi di quel Green Deal, peraltro con un commissario, la Ribera, addirittura pi脙鹿 integralista del precedente, e l'azione di snaturamento delle basi di convivenza civile, ignorando le istanze per un'Europa pi脙鹿 vicina ai cittadini.

Per questo la stima verso il commissario italiano Fitto, che ha ben condotto in Italia la difficile partita del PNRR, e il commissario ungherese V脙隆rhelyi non possono essere l'alibi per avallare questa operazione, che va contro gli interessi dei cittadini a tutela dell'ideologia.

(La Presidente toglie la parola all'oratrice)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Carlo Fidanza (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, non so se davvero questa sar脙聽 la Commissione pi脙鹿 a destra della storia, come sentiamo dire da una sinistra nervosa, dai verdi nervosissimi.

Me lo auguro, francamente, ma lo vedremo, lo vedremo alla prova dei fatti; lo vedremo se sar脙聽 capace di riportare pragmatismo nel Green Deal, nonostante la signora Ribera, se sapr脙聽 sostenere le nostre imprese, se sapr脙聽 controllare le nostre frontiere e aumentare i rimpatri.

Su questi temi vedremo anche quali maggioranze si creeranno in questa Aula, al di l脙聽 dei comunicati e della forma 芒聙聯 quello che si voter脙聽 qui e chi lo voter脙聽 芒聙聯 e ne vedremo delle belle, ne sono convinto.

Quello che 脙篓 certo 脙篓 che il governo italiano guidato da Giorgia Meloni ha offerto a questa Commissione europea una persona capace e competente, permettetemi, la pi脙鹿 capace e la pi脙鹿 competente per gestire un portafoglio importantissimo come quello della coesione: Raffaele Fitto, che 脙篓 un conservatore. E lo ha fatto grazie a un mandato democratico dato dai cittadini italiani con milioni di voti al governo guidato da Giorgia Meloni e dal centrodestra.

Questa 脙篓 la democrazia, vi piaccia o no.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Estrella Gal脙隆n (The Left). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, hoy Europa no tiene nada que celebrar. Hoy se aprueba aqu脙颅 una Comisi脙鲁n que abraza a la extrema derecha, se脙卤ora von der Leyen. Y, adem脙隆s, le otorga usted una vicepresidencia, que no es cualquier cosa. 脗驴Tanto les deben para darles un cargo tan importante?

Nosotras vamos a votar 脗芦No脗禄. Y ser脙隆 un 脗芦No脗禄 rotundo y claro, porque no vamos a ser c脙鲁mplices de una Comisi脙鲁n que est脙隆 blanqueando a los herederos del fascismo y de la historia m脙隆s negra de nuestra Europa.

Hoy van a aprobar una Comisi脙鲁n cuyas prioridades se alejan mucho de las necesidades de la mayor脙颅a social.脗聽Votamos 脗芦No脗禄 a una Comisi脙鲁n que blanquea a quienes niegan la crisis clim脙隆tica, a quienes consienten el genocidio en Gaza mientras suenan tambores de guerra en nuestras fronteras, a quienes no defienden los servicios p脙潞blicos, a quienes fomentan el odio, el racismo y la xenofobia, a quienes criminalizan al colectivo LGTBI y, adem脙隆s, niegan la diversidad y la igualdad entre mujeres y hombres.

Hoy, los trabajadores y las trabajadoras de Europa, los ciudadanos y las ciudadanas que so脙卤amos con una Europa m脙隆s justa y verde no tenemos nada que celebrar.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta Metsola, se脙卤ora presidenta von der Leyen, en su discurso de investidura del mes de julio, al aludir a migraciones y asilo 芒聙聰de lejos, el asunto m脙隆s sensible y divisivo de la agenda europea芒聙聰 subray脙鲁 usted las palabras 脗芦firmeza脗禄 y 脗芦seguridad脗禄 y aludi脙鲁 a soluciones imaginativas, como un aparente subterfugio para la externalizaci脙鲁n de las fronteras, adem脙隆s de las migraciones y de las solicitudes de asilo.

Sin embargo, hoy, en su discurso, ha introducido usted 脗芦responsabilidad compartida脗禄 y 脗芦solidaridad脗禄, adem脙隆s del compromiso con los derechos fundamentales, que es exactamente el objeto del Pacto sobre Migraci脙鲁n y Asilo, por el que tanto trabajamos en la legislatura anterior.

Quienes nos disponemos a votar favorablemente al Colegio de Comisarios vamos a exigir de la Comisi脙鲁n entera un compromiso activo con su papel de guardiana de los Tratados y del Derecho europeo, para que demande de los Estados miembros 芒聙聰de los veintisiete芒聙聰 un compromiso eficaz y riguroso de cumplimiento de las obligaciones contra脙颅das con la legislaci脙鲁n que hemos puesto en vigor 芒聙聰el Pacto sobre Migraci脙鲁n y Asilo芒聙聰 y, por supuesto, con perfecto respeto de los derechos fundamentales de los migrantes y de los solicitantes de asilo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Luke Ming Flanagan (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, there are so many reasons to vote against this new Commission.

We start with the President, von der Leyen. You enabled genocide in Palestine by giving unconditional support to Israel.

Christophe Hansen, you were very clear in your support for Mercosur. You also have no ambition to increase the CAP budget, no public money for the Nature Restoration Law.

Michael McGrath, from Ireland, you are responsible for the rule of law, yet when it comes to EU market surveillance laws, your government has done everything but protect this rule of law. Then there's the two self-proclaimed fascists.

It didn't take Manfred Weber long to get back into bed with Viktor Orb脙隆n. All your words about disgust, about him, and still you put one of his friends in as vice-president.

I'll finish by quoting the leader of the S&D from her speech today, 'you cannot build Europe with those who destroy it脗聽...'

(The President cut off the speaker)

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, sehr geehrte Frau Kommissionspr脙陇sidentin in spe von der Leyen! Als wir das letzte Mal miteinander gesprochen haben, habe ich Ihnen gesagt, dass ich aufgrund einer basisdemokratischen Entscheidung meiner Partei nicht f脙录r Sie gestimmt habe, und 脙陇u脙聼erte die Hoffnung, dass Sie uns eines Besseren belehren w脙录rden. Ich w脙录rde Ihnen f脙录r diese Kommission eine 6 von 10 geben. Nicht schlecht, aber mit Luft nach oben, insbesondere im Hinblick auf einzelne Kommissare. Unbeschadet dessen freue ich mich nun, den k脙录nftigen Kommissarinnen und Kommissaren in die Augen zu blicken, und w脙录nsche ihnen das Allerbeste f脙录r ihre Arbeit und werde auch f脙录r diese Kommission stimmen.

Ich m脙露chte Sie aber zuletzt an eine Sache erinnern: Sie sind mittelbar, aber nicht unmittelbar demokratisch gew脙陇hlt. Die europ脙陇ische Bev脙露lkerung hat nicht direkt f脙录r Sie gestimmt. Wie ein Kollege aus Frankreich, der sonst den eher intellektuell-kreativen politischen Kr脙陇ften angeh脙露rt, es gesagt hat: Sie kennt sie auch gr脙露脙聼tenteils nicht. Es ist nun Ihre Aufgabe, durch eine transparente Arbeit, starkes Engagement in den klassischen und sozialen Medien und umsichtiges Regierungshandeln den Menschen in Europa zu zeigen, dass Sie Ihren Posten auch verdienen.

T脙陇htis samm on ka esimese Euroopa kaitsevoliniku ametisse nimetamine, mis kinnitab, et Euroopa m脙碌istab praeguste ohtude t脙碌sidust ja tegutseb vastavalt.

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Dear colleagues, we are now going to have, after a very long debate, a round of political speakers in reverse order. Everybody will speak from their seat.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ren脙漏 Aust, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Europa kann mehr. Europa kann mehr, als diese EU-Kommission vorhat. Europa hat das Potenzial f脙录r eine f脙录hrende Rolle in der Welt 芒聙聯 politisch, wirtschaftlich und kulturell. Doch um dieses Potenzial f脙录r die Zukunft abrufen zu k脙露nnen, braucht es eine klare Ausrichtung auf die zentralen Aufgaben der europ脙陇ischen Ebene: die Sicherung der Au脙聼engrenzen, die Zusammenarbeit der europ脙陇ischen Staaten im Au脙聼enhandel, in der Verteidigung und bei der Forschung, den Binnenmarkt und die Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit st脙陇rken und so Millionen Arbeitspl脙陇tze erhalten.

Europa wird nur stark, wenn es sich auf die Kernaufgaben konzentriert. Stattdessen verschwendet die EU ihre Kraft f脙录r ideologische Projekte. Sie k脙录mmert sich um Nebens脙陇chlichkeiten, w脙陇hrend wichtige Themen liegen bleiben. Deshalb ist es notwendig, die Europ脙陇ische Union zur脙录ckzubauen脗聽芒聙聯 weniger Kompetenzen, weniger Aufgaben, weniger Ausgaben. Doch diese Kommission steht f脙录r das Gegenteil. Ihr Programm ist: Weiter so! Mehr Auflagen f脙录r unsere Industrien, mehr Ausgaben f脙录r Gender- und Klimapolitik, mehr Zensur, mehr Zentralismus.

Mit anderen Worten: Diese EU-Kommission will mehr von alledem, was wir ablehnen. Und darum lehnen wir diese EU-Kommission ab. Europa funktioniert, wenn die Nationalstaaten ihre Souver脙陇nit脙陇t in den Bereichen Kultur-, Sozial-, Einwanderungs- und Haushaltspolitik bewahren und gleichzeitig in der Sicherheitspolitik, beim Schutz der Au脙聼engrenzen und beim Binnenmarkt zusammenarbeiten. Unser Europa ist das Europa der Freiheit und der Zusammenarbeit; ein Europa, das in den Herzen unserer B脙录rger, in den Vaterl脙陇ndern lebt, nicht in den Fluren dieser B脙录rokraten. Deshalb sagen wir Nein. Nein zu dieser EU-Kommission, Nein zur Politik von Frau von der Leyen und Ja zur Souver脙陇nit脙陇t unserer Nationen in Europa!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Manon Aubry, au nom du groupe The Left. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame von der Leyen, ce qui m'a frapp脙漏, au cours de ce d脙漏bat, c'est votre niveau de d脙漏connexion totale avec la r脙漏alit脙漏 que vivent des millions de citoyens europ脙漏ens: des citoyens qui peinent 脙聽 boucler leurs fins de mois, 脙聽 payer leurs factures d'脙漏lectricit脙漏, qui perdent leurs emplois, parce que quelques actionnaires avides de profits pr脙漏f脙篓rent d脙漏localiser. De ces gens-l脙聽, aujourd'hui, il n'en a pas 脙漏t脙漏 question, Madame von der Leyen, peut-脙陋tre parce que vous avez un salaire bien confortable, du haut de vos 30 000 euros par mois. De m脙陋me, mettre 脙聽 contribution les milliardaires, les superprofits, plut脙麓t que de faire payer les travailleurs de leurs poches, il n'en a jamais non plus 脙漏t脙漏 question dans ce d脙漏bat.

脙聙 la place, vous avez transform脙漏 votre discours en 脗芦L'Europe a un incroyable talent脗禄, en listant un 脙聽 un vos commissaires comme des rockstars. J'aurais aussi pu le faire. Vous pr脙漏sentez Monsieur Dombrovskis, en charge du racket de nos services publics et de nos syst脙篓mes de protection sociale, responsable d'orchestrer l'aust脙漏rit脙漏; Monsieur 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧, en charge du grand d脙漏m脙漏nagement du monde, en signant 脙聽 tour de bras des accords de libre-脙漏change; Monsieur S脙漏journ脙漏, en charge de la d脙漏sindustrialisation, des d脙漏localisations et des plans de licenciements; et, cerise sur le g脙垄teau, Monsieur Fitto, en charge de la nouvelle alliance avec l'extr脙陋me droite 芒聙聯 quoique j'aurais pu mentionner aussi Madame Kallas, en charge de la complicit脙漏 avec le g脙漏nocide 脙聽 Gaza.

La r脙漏alit脙漏, c'est que les seuls gagnants de ce concours, Madame von der Leyen, sont 脙聽 l'extr脙陋me droite. Et on a vu 脙聽 quel point le groupe de Monsieur Fitto, le groupe d'extr脙陋me droite men脙漏 par les n脙漏ofascistes en Italie, sortait gagnant avec Monsieur Weber. Avec Monsieur Weber. Car oui, pour sauver votre coalition, Madame von der Leyen, vous avez pactis脙漏 avec l'extr脙陋me droite. Pas 脙漏tonnant qu'aucun talent n'ait 脙漏t脙漏 propos脙漏, par exemple, pour sauvegarder les droits des femmes. C'est surtout cela que l'on retiendra aujourd'hui, quitte 脙聽 humilier certains groupes ici.

Alors, mon groupe The Left 芒聙聯 de la gauche 芒聙聯, Madame von der Leyen, n'est pas 脙聽 vendre. Comptez sur nous pour nous opposer fermement, mais avec espoir et radicalit脙漏, 脙聽 votre Commission de malheur.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bas Eickhout, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, it was a very difficult decision for our group, and you know very well we are still of the opinion making Fitto Executive Vice-President of this Commission is a mistake.脗聽People that say, 'He is not far right,' tell that to the mothers in Italy whose rights to have children together have been taken away just because they want to live together. Tell that to the judges who are criticised by the government for just doing their job. And tell that to the journalists who feel under pressure more and more just to do their job independently.

We as a Green group will always fight for an open and an inclusive society where everyone feels welcome and can love whoever they want. But we see as well that much work lies ahead of us: the climate crisis is accelerating; wars to our east and south threaten our democracy and credibility; our industry is under pressure of China and US; and people fight for the cost of their living.

We see a Commission that wants to work with us, and we want too, to work on climate adaptation, to work on a clean industrial act, to work on the energy transition, to work on a just transition for everyone, to work on a stronger rule of law. But also, we will make sure that the simplification agenda will not be a deregulation agenda.

It was a difficult decision for us also because of the parliamentary process. And I am just going to say that there is a big responsibility for the biggest group: being harder on a respected minister from the Social Democrats than you were on a Fidesz candidate that called us idiots. How could you do that? So let us at least learn one thing. Let never any more national politics get back into this arena.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Val脙漏rie Hayer, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, thank you. Madam President, you were elected to lead the Commission on the basis of a work programme. It largely includes our Renew proposals and this is very good news. We are at a turning point. We are facing existential challenges.脗聽So you know my family's ambitions 芒聙聯 three things.

First, freedom. No rule of law, no EU funds. Madam President, we appreciate your support for smart conditionality. Thank you for endorsing it for the first time. And we also call for the Commission to move to the next step of Article 7.

Second, prosperity. Let's answer Mario Draghi's wake-up call. We have to scrap red tape, to simplify, we have to speed up the savings and investments Union. You have announced a Clean Industrial Deal. It must be our tool to decarbonise the economy. It must serve the Green Deal. EU businesses are already on track for a clean future. They need our support and we won't accept any backtracking when it comes to protecting the planet.

And third, security. 80脗聽% of our military equipment comes from abroad. We are the target of more and more threats on our democracies here in Europe. If we don't reinforce our security, our defence market and our support to Ukraine, Europe will be at major risk. We need a true defence and security Union. There is no plan B.

We are counting on your entire College to follow to the letter the direction that you, yourself, set out in this Chamber. It is a huge, huge task, but we will be by your side.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Assita Kanko, namens de ECR-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter,脗聽芒聙聹Wat we vooral willen, is vrijheid, en niet gebukt gaan onder oneindige belastingen芒聙聺, schrijft Guillaume op Facebook. 芒聙聹Hoe kan mijn bedrijf overleven als Europa zo doorgaat?芒聙聺, zucht een CEO. 芒聙聹Ik wil gehoord worden芒聙聺, fluistert een vrouw die verkracht werd en nooit gerechtigheid kreeg. 芒聙聹Waarom word ik anders behandeld?芒聙聺 Wij Europeanen moeten voor onze waarden en normen durven kiezen, geen shariapraktijken toestaan en geen geld geven aan organisaties die ons kapot willen maken.

Ook ik heb mijn twijfels bij een aantal van de nieuwe commissarissen, maar het is genoeg geweest met de koehandel en het moddergevecht. Er moet nu voor de burger worden gewerkt. En we weten allemaal dat dit zonder nieuwe Commissie niet kan. Anders kunnen we niet aan de slag en betaalt de burger voor het tijdverlies. Hij krijgt hier genoeg van. Beste collega芒聙聶s, laten we eerlijk zijn en stoppen met geheime deals en geheime relaties. We kunnen niet van anderen verwachten dat ze transparant zijn, als wij ons op die manier gedragen. Want er is maar 脙漏脙漏n persoon van wie wij het vertrouwen moeten winnen, en dat is de kiezer.

Ze willen dat we ons migratiebeleid op orde brengen en dat we werken aan goede terugkeerwetten die worden aangenomen en ge脙炉mplementeerd. Ze willen dat we antisemitisme bestrijden; dat Joodse kinderen niet naar scholen gaan die beveiligd moeten worden, maar naar scholen gaan die veilig zijn. Ze willen dat we onze rechtsstaat durven te beschermen; dat we de politi脙芦le en justiti脙芦le samenwerking verbeteren; dat we de georganiseerde misdaad aanpakken en dat we onze havens beschermen tegen drugscriminaliteit. Ze willen dat we ervoor zorgen dat geen enkel seksueel misdrijf onbestraft blijft; dat de economie gerepareerd wordt; dat onze industrie niet vernietigd wordt door de gekke plannen van de groenen en van de communisten. Ze willen dat we eindelijk een EU zijn waarop zij kunnen rekenen. Door u vandaag mijn stem te geven, doe ik iets dat ik heel serieus neem, mevrouw von脗聽der脗聽Leyen. Ik vertrouw de vrouw in de cockpit en de capaciteit van dit parlement om zich te herpakken. We laten de kiezer niet in de steek. We moeten aan de slag.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kinga G脙隆l, a PfE k脙漏pvisel脜聭csoport nev脙漏ben. 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k Asszony! J脙潞niusban a v脙隆laszt脙鲁k t脙露bbs脙漏ge a v脙隆ltoz脙隆sra szavazott. Erre minden okuk megvolt. Ennek els脜聭 jele volt, hogy a biztosi meghallgat脙隆sokat politikai boh脙鲁zatt脙隆 sil脙隆ny脙颅tott脙隆k. El脜聭re meghat脙隆rozt脙隆k, hogy kiket t脙隆mogatnak 脙漏s kiket nem, f脙录ggetlen脙录l a biztosjel脙露ltek teljes脙颅tm脙漏ny脙漏t脜聭l. Politikai alapon blokkoltak felk脙漏sz脙录lt jel脙露lteket, csak mert konzervat脙颅v jobboldaliak, 脙漏s engedtek 脙隆t olyanokat, akik az alapvet脜聭 k脙漏rd脙漏sekre sem adtak megfelel脜聭 v脙隆laszokat, vagy probl脙漏m脙隆s a meg脙颅t脙漏l脙漏s脙录k. Majd, hogy 脙隆tmenjen ez a von der Leyen bizotts脙隆g, a baloldal a liber脙隆lisokkal 脙漏s a N脙漏pp脙隆rttal koal脙颅ci脙鲁s meg脙隆llapod脙隆st k脙露t脙露tt. Ez a meg脙隆llapod脙隆s a v脙隆ltozatlans脙隆g bizony脙颅t脙漏ka, az eddigi hib脙隆k tov脙隆bbg脙露rget脙漏se.

Migr脙隆ci脙鲁s paktumot akarnak, a h脙隆bor脙潞 tov脙隆bbi t脙隆mogat脙隆s脙隆t, a b脙录rokr脙隆cia er脜聭s脙颅t脙漏s脙漏t, m脙漏g t脙露bb ideol脙鲁gi脙隆t. Meger脜聭s脙颅t脙漏st nyert az is, hogy tov脙隆bbra is elv脙隆rj脙隆k a Bizotts脙隆g eln脙露k脙漏t脜聭l, hogy zsarol脙隆sra haszn脙隆lja az uni脙鲁s forr脙隆sokat. Lehet r脙露vidt脙隆v脙潞 paktumokat k脙露tni, de az emberek akarat脙隆t nem lehet les脙露p脙露rni. Eln脙露k asszony, mi patri脙鲁t脙隆k nem felejtj脙录k el, ahogy a magyar soros eln脙露ks脙漏gr脜聭l sz脙鲁l脙鲁 vit脙隆ban p脙漏ld脙隆tlan m脙鲁don t脙隆madott r脙隆 Magyarorsz脙隆gra, a szerep脙漏vel vissza脙漏lve politikai kamp脙隆nybesz脙漏det mondott egy tag脙隆llami korm脙隆ny ellen. 脗聽

脗聽脗聽Iratxe Garc脙颅a P脙漏rez, en nombre del Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora presidenta von der Leyen, estoy segura 芒聙聰no tengo ninguna duda芒聙聰 de que hoy usted ha le脙颅do perfectamente el mensaje de esta casa. Ha le脙颅do perfectamente el mensaje que aquellos que queremos construir Europa con usted y con su Colegio de Comisarios queremos trasladarle.

Evidentemente, el Parlamento Europeo representa la soberan脙颅a ciudadana, una Europa diversa, una Europa con diferencias, pero una Europa que es consciente de que tiene que trabajar unida.

Evidentemente, no ha sido f脙隆cil la negociaci脙鲁n. Evidentemente, usted no tendr脙隆 el apoyo del 100脗聽% de esta C脙隆mara, ni siquiera de mi grupo. Pero en esta casa hay una mayor脙颅a que puede avanzar en construir la Europa que necesitamos: la Europa de la igualdad, la Europa de la solidaridad, la Europa de la defensa de los derechos humanos, la Europa verde, la Europa feminista, la Europa que quiere responder a los problemas de la ciudadan脙颅a.

Hay quien ha puesto muchos problemas en esta negociaci脙鲁n, quien ha intentado desestabilizar a la Uni脙鲁n Europea por poner sus intereses partidistas por encima de los intereses de Europa. Pero tenga clara una cosa: el mensaje es evidente, aqu脙颅 hay una mayor脙颅a entre populares, liberales, socialdem脙鲁cratas y verdes que quieren trabajar conjuntamente. Posiblemente hoy no sean todos los votos positivos, pero s脙颅 hay un compromiso para que ma脙卤ana empecemos a trabajar por el bien com脙潞n. Hoy no termina nada, hoy empieza todo y tiene con usted y con todo el Colegio de Comisarios a un Grupo Socialdem脙鲁crata que va a ser responsable, que va a marcar la agenda, que va a defender los valores de la Uni脙鲁n y que va a seguir trabajando por el bien de toda la ciudadan脙颅a europea.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jeroen Lenaers, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, before going on to the vote of the new European Commission, let me also thank the outgoing Commissioners for their dedication and their work in the past years. Regardless of nationality or political colour, you have put yourself at the service of the European citizens and we thank you for that.

As for the new Commission, almost 200脗聽million voters went to the ballot box and they put their trust in us to deliver on their priorities and to address their concerns. They gave us the responsibility for making sure that Europe is heading in the right direction. We take an important step today in that responsibility, by making sure Europe is governed and by making sure the new European Commission can take office without delay.

I would like to say to President脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen and the new team 芒聙聯脗聽and this might not be a shock脗聽芒聙聯 but you can rely on the support of the EPP Group. At the same time, I will say though, our support will come with expectations. Expectations that you will deliver on that message from the voters.

Like Robert Schuman said, Europe will not be built at once, but through concrete achievements, and this European Commission must be the Commission of concrete achievements on European competitiveness and reducing red tape, on migration, on strategic autonomy, on agriculture, on security and defence. On all these issues Europe must deliver.

We have no time to lose. War still rages on the European continent. Dictators at our eastern borders continue to try and destabilise our Union and the world is in turmoil. We need European leadership. You were right in July when you said that we cannot control demagogues and dictators in the world, but we can choose to protect our own democracy and we can choose to build our own European future.

The big decisions of the next five years will define for a long time the future and our place in the world. So, we must get these decisions right and we must get them right together. With a strong European Commission and with a strong pro-European, democratic, pro芒聙聭rule of law majority in this House, we can build a future European Union that prospers, that protects us and that prepares us for whatever the future will bring. So let's get to work.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you. The item is closed.

Motions for resolutions on the presentation by the Commission President-elect of the College of Commissioners and its programme.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 I have received two motions for resolutions on the presentation of the College of Commissioners, and according to Rule 129 we will start with these.

(see minutes, item 5.1)

Election of the Commission.

I have received a request for adjournment of the vote on the election of the Commission. Pursuant to Rule 206(4), a political group or Members reaching at least the low threshold may present a motion calling for a vote to be adjourned. Since the request is not submitted by Members reaching the low threshold, it is not admissible.

We now move to the vote on the election of the Commission. I remind you that Parliament shall elect or reject the Commission by a majority of the votes cast by roll call.

I would like to take the opportunity to thank the outgoing Commission for their commitment to Europe and, to the incoming Commission, should you be elected today, we hope to see you back here often.

After the vote, I will announce the result. If the Commission is elected by a majority of the votes cast, I will sign a transmission letter on the decision of the European Parliament to elect the Commission. I will hand it to the President of the Commission and I will inform the Council.

(see minutes, item 5.1)

I will now sign the transition letter and I will transmit it to the President of the European Commission.

脗聽脗聽Giuseppe Lupo, relatore. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, stiamo purtroppo assistendo al verificarsi di fenomeni climatici drammatici in tutta Europa.

In questo quadro, il Fondo di solidariet脙聽 interviene sostenendo gli Stati membri colpiti da calamit脙聽, dimostrando la concreta vicinanza dell'Unione europea.

Oggi votiamo la proposta di decisione, gi脙聽 approvata in commissione Bilanci da tutti i gruppi politici, per la mobilitazione del fondo a favore della Germania e dell'Italia, per un ammontare di circa 116 milioni di euro in relazione alle alluvioni verificatesi a giugno 2024.

Penso sia necessario adeguare il Fondo di solidariet脙聽 per fronteggiare le emergenze future. Ringrazio tutte le istituzioni che hanno collaborato per rendere pi脙鹿 veloce l'adozione della decisione di mobilitazione.

脗聽脗聽J脙隆nos B脙鲁ka, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members, ladies and gentlemen, I congratulate you for concluding the vote on the budget of the European Union for the financial year 2025.

I note with satisfaction that the European Parliament confirmed the agreement reached at the Conciliation Committee meeting on 16脗聽November脗聽2024. This allows for an orderly start of 2025.

The budget is a reasonable compromise. It responds to our priorities, fulfils our legal obligations and provides support to actions that are important to our citizens.

I take this opportunity to thank once again the Chair of the Committee on Budgets, Johan脗聽Van脗聽Overtveldt, and the rapporteurs, Victor脗聽Negrescu and Niclas脗聽Herbst, as well as other Members of the European Parliament who participated in the budgetary procedure for their constructive approach.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 So the joint text agreed by the Conciliation Committee on the 16 November 2024 has been approved by both Parliament and the Council. The budgetary procedure for the financial year 2025 can thus be deemed to have been completed.

The Union's general budget for the financial year 2025 is definitively adopted.

I will now sign the budget.

Besz脙漏des, hogy a vil脙隆g 20 legnagyobb v脙隆llalata k脙露z脙露tt nincs eur脙鲁pai. A GDP 3 sz脙隆zal脙漏k脙隆t mostant脙鲁l kutat脙隆sra, fejleszt脙漏sre 脙漏s innov脙隆ci脙鲁ra ford脙颅tani k脙露teless脙漏g lesz. Eur脙鲁pa nyitott gazdas脙隆g, l脙漏t脙漏rdeke a konnektivit脙隆s, hogy min脙漏l t脙露bb ir脙隆nyba, min脙漏l szabadabban kereskedhessen. A meg脙隆llapod脙隆soknak nem leck脙漏ztetni kell a partnereket, hanem k脙露lcs脙露n脙露s el脜聭ny脙露ket biztos脙颅tani, mert p脙隆rbesz脙漏ddel sokkal t脙露bbet 脙漏rhet脙录nk el.

Vissza kell adnunk a gazd脙隆k becs脙录let脙漏t. Az agr脙隆rpolitik脙隆t nem szabad 脙潞jabb, k脙露lts脙漏ges szab脙隆lyokkal terhelni. Kamp脙隆nyban sokan sokat 脙颅g脙漏rtek, de amit az脙鲁ta l脙隆ttunk, ezzel ellent脙漏tes. Mi, patri脙鲁t脙隆k mindent meg fogunk tenni az eur脙鲁pai mez脜聭gazdas脙隆g meger脜聭s脙颅t脙漏s脙漏脙漏rt.

脙職j iparstrat脙漏gi脙隆ra van sz脙录ks脙漏g, megfizethet脜聭 energi脙隆ra. Ehhez azonnali reformokat v脙隆runk a Bizotts脙隆gt脙鲁l. Ideje munk脙隆hoz l脙隆tni, Tisztelt K脙漏pvisel脜聭t脙隆rsaim! Kevesebb ideol脙鲁giai sz脙鲁cs脙漏pl脙漏s, t脙露bb munka 脙漏s 脙露sszefog脙隆s. Az 脙隆llam- 脙漏s korm脙隆nyf脜聭knek siker脙录lt. Nek脙录nk is meg kell tudnunk ezt tenn脙录nk.

S脙录rgetem a Bizotts脙隆got, hogy az els脜聭 100 napban 脙隆lljon el脜聭 pragmatikus, v脙隆llalatbar脙隆t megold脙隆sokat tartalmaz脙鲁 akci脙鲁tervvel, az Eur脙鲁pai Tan脙隆cs pedig ne engedje ki a kez脙漏b脜聭l az ir脙隆ny脙颅t脙隆st, k脙漏rjen mindent sz脙隆mon ezen a Parlamenten 脙漏s a Bizotts脙隆gon is! Tegy脙录k naggy脙隆 脙潞jra Eur脙鲁p脙隆t!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽J脙隆nos B脙鲁ka, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, Commissioner, I am delighted to address the plenary and report to you on the outcome of the informal meeting of the heads of state and government that took place in Budapest on 8脗聽November.

Competitiveness is the overarching priority of the Hungarian Presidency that orders, informs and reinforces the presidency's other priorities, such as strengthening Europe's defence policy, shaping future agriculture and cohesion policy, and addressing demographic challenges. In Budapest, leaders had the opportunity to have an exchange of views with Mario Draghi on his report on the future of European competitiveness, as well as with Christine Lagarde, President of the European Central Bank. Leaders adopted the Budapest declaration on the new European competitiveness deal, a document that is a clear and loud call for change.

Europe needs to change. We have seen this in the Draghi report, we have seen this in the results of the last EP elections, we are seeing this in the results of recent national elections. The declaration stresses the need for decisive action to meet common challenges with collective efforts. We need a simplification revolution to reduce administrative burden and overregulation. In particular, we expect initiatives to reduce reporting obligations under EU law by 25脗聽% in the first half of 2025. We also need secure and affordable energy supply, and to this effect, urgent European action needs to be taken. As long as our companies pay two to three times more for electricity and four to five times more for natural gas than their US competitors, competitiveness is just a phrase.

The declaration is a statement of intent about making the European Union more competitive, productive, innovative and sustainable. In the field of industry and manufacturing, the declaration stresses the need to ensure our industrial renewal, hand in hand with decarbonisation. In the field of agriculture, the declaration insists on the need to deliver a competitive, sustainable and resilient agricultural sector. This will provide a stable and predictable framework for farmers, strengthening their position in the food supply chain and ensuring fair competition globally and in the internal market. It's a strategic issue. It's about secure food supply, affordable and safe foodstuffs, fair international competition based on a playing field with equal standards and a decent living for our European farmers. We must show them that they matter, and they matter not only in the last few weeks before the EP elections.

To this end, we are looking forward to the Commission's vision for agriculture and food in March 2025, and we expect this vision to take into account the strategic discussion we have conducted in Council about the future of the common agricultural policy.

In total, the declaration identifies no less than 12 drivers of competitiveness, including the need for making urgent progress on the capital markets union and to accelerate the digital transformation across industries. In the process, we need to harness Europe's talent and invest in people's skills so as to foster high-quality jobs right throughout our 27 Member States. Honourable Members, this is the Budapest declaration on the new European competitiveness deal, and by facilitating its delivery, I truly believe that the Hungarian Presidency has done a service to the cause of competitiveness in the European Union.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kadri Simson, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, thank you for inviting the Commission to discuss the future of our manufacturing and farming industry in light of the recently adopted Budapest declaration. I am here replacing Executive脗聽Vice芒聙聭President脗聽Vestager, under whose remit this topic belongs.

During this legislative cycle, we have already taken several key initiatives to lay the ground for a stronger European industry and farming sector. We know this is a tough situation. Europe is faced with a complex problem: making our industry and farming sector more competitive, while at the same time pursuing our climate ambition, preserving the single market and catering for our security.

Let me start by stressing how important a solid industrial base is to the competitiveness of Europe. Manufacturing amounts to 20脗聽% of EU GDP and 25脗聽% of jobs in Europe, and establishing a robust industrial base is also a matter of economic security. This industrial bedrock gives Europe a strong position in global markets. The EU remains one of the largest global traders of goods, representing 16.5脗聽% of global exports, compared to 21脗聽% for China and 12.5脗聽% for the US. And when it comes to export of services, the EU is in the lead with 33脗聽% of global exports, compared to 8脗聽% for China and 20脗聽% for the US. Despite challenges, sectors such as the automotive industry, chemicals, agri芒聙聭food and pharma remain prime examples of European excellence. In all these sectors, the EU is the second largest global producer and the top world exporter.

At the same time, the European Union has managed to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by almost a third since 1990, compared to just about 2脗聽% in the United States, for example. The EU is also a leader in the deployment of renewable energy so that for the first time in 2023, the European Union produced more electricity from wind power than from gas. All this shows Europe's resilience and stamina.脗聽Of course, it depends a lot on our single market 芒聙聯 one of the world's largest integrated market areas. The single market has given Europe the weight and the wingspan to thrive and we will need it more than ever. So, we have strong assets to rely on.

I am not trying to present a rosy picture. It is about putting things into perspective and boosting Europe's self芒聙聭confidence in a context where some of the biggest global players are getting more assertive. Long芒聙聭term competitiveness is at the core of the Commission's agenda for the next five years, and let me underline that decarbonisation does not mean deindustrialisation. We need to find the right modus operandi to provide more certainty in an ever芒聙聭changing world, to catalyse European industry into action, to keep our jobs and our added value without recreating dependencies.

This is the direction of travel announced by President脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen in her political guidelines in July. Again, prosperity and competitiveness will be the priority of the new Commission. The Strategic Technologies for Europe Platform is already boosting investment in the development and manufacturing of clean, digital and deep tech and biotechnologies in the EU.

The clean industrial deal will strengthen the business case for investments in competitiveness and decarbonisation. This means concrete actions to simplify permits and improve access to infrastructures, access to energy and raw materials, access to public and private funding, access to skills and technologies and access to markets. A key element of the clean industrial deal is also to support European lead markets for the clean tech industry and will speed up planning, tendering and permitting processes, in particular for energy intensive sectors.

Likewise, decarbonising or greening the farming sector does not mean defarming. From the economic side, the Commission has worked to improve farmers' income and strengthen their position in the food value chain, in particular through the common agricultural policy that provides direct payments and support for certain sectors to increase resilience of farms and support farmers' income; second, crisis measures; and third, regulatory measures aiming at ensuring fairer remuneration. Moreover, the common agricultural policy strategic plans allocate EUR脗聽8.5脗聽billion for young farmers aiming to support generational renewal in agriculture.

When preparing the vision on agriculture and food, we will aim at strengthening the competitiveness, sustainability and resilience of the EU food system to ensure food safety, availability and affordability, whilst at the same time protecting and restoring the natural environment and its ecosystem, ensuring decent living conditions to farmers and enabling consumers to make healthy choices.脗聽This work will build on the report and recommendations that resulted from the Strategic Dialogue on the future of EU agriculture.

Now, I am looking forward to hearing your views.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Carmen Crespo D脙颅az, en nombre del Grupo PPE. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora comisaria,脗聽gracias, en primer lugar, por apoyar a nuestro pa脙颅s en esta DANA tan devastadora en Valencia, en Andaluc脙颅a y en otras regiones de Espa脙卤a; pero, sobre todo, en la reconstrucci脙鲁n, porque creo que esto es importante para la competitividad. En este momento ya tenemos fondos en marcha: con la Medida 23 a trav脙漏s del Feader, con la reserva de crisis, pero tambi脙漏n con RESTORE, con el FEDER y con el Fondo Social Europeo.

Pero quiero dedicar un momento a NextGenerationEU, que creo que es la esperanza blanca: el 24脗聽% se ha gastado en mi pa脙颅s y pr脙隆cticamente nada en obras hidr脙隆ulicas de prevenci脙鲁n. Y, por supuesto, nada en agricultura: ni en simplificaci脙鲁n, ni en tecnolog脙颅a 5.0, ni en bajar la huella h脙颅drica de los sectores agrarios, que es fundamental para la competitividad.

Pero hoy aqu脙颅 se ha hablado de un concepto de importancia may脙潞scula que es la econom脙颅a circular. Es el momento de tener ingresos para luchar contra el cambio clim脙隆tico y para los sectores agrarios: en este momento, los residuos, los subproductos, pueden ser una oportunidad en la que tenemos que trabajar para dotar a esos sectores de tecnolog脙颅a y hacerlos realmente competitivos: en este caso, las aguas regeneradas (para equilibrar el sector en cuanto al consumo de agua) y, por supuesto, el biog脙隆s, los purines o cuestiones similares.

Creo que el mercado es fundamental en estos casos. Tenemos que trabajar en cl脙隆usulas de reciprocidad que permitan a los sectores no tener competencia desleal y, desde luego, que no sean ellos quienes脗聽paguen los aranceles de otros pa脙颅ses. No solamente debemos hacerlo con Estados Unidos, sino tambi脙漏n, en este caso, con China, y por ello tenemos que tener un gabinete jur脙颅dico europeo potente que sea especialista en estos casos.

Y, por supuesto, hacer una PAC para el futuro que tambi脙漏n trabaje por los agricultores profesionales, que son los que est脙隆n afianzando la poblaci脙鲁n en el territorio rural y van a dar el acceso que queremos a una agricultura potente donde entren los j脙鲁venes 芒聙聰hombres y mujeres芒聙聰.

Europa tiene que decidir qu脙漏 quiere ser de mayor: si un museo precioso o un museo precioso y competitivo. Y ah脙颅, la agricultura tiene mucho que decir.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Antonio Decaro, a nome del gruppo S&D. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, le sfide economiche, sociali e politiche che la nuova Commissione e il Parlamento dovranno affrontare sono enormi. La necessit脙聽 di una risposta unitaria non 脙篓 mai stata cos脙卢 impellente: lo dice la dichiarazione di Budapest. Occorre che gli interventi siano strutturati e integrati.

Non possiamo parlare di neutralit脙聽 climatica nel 2050 se non programmiamo subito investimenti sul rinnovamento industriale, sulla decarbonizzazione, sulle tecnologie strategiche, prestando particolare attenzione alle industrie tradizionali in transizione per garantire competitivit脙聽 ma anche posti di lavoro di qualit脙聽.

脙聢 urgente colmare il divario di innovazione e produttivit脙聽, sia con i nostri concorrenti globali, sia tra i Paesi membri, investendo in ricerca e in economia circolare, in particolare per le materie prime critiche.

Walter Veltroni, che 脙篓 stato il segretario del Partito Democratico, il primo segretario nel mio Paese, qualche giorno fa ha scritto su un quotidiano nazionale che Roosevelt ai tempi delle grandi dittature oltreoceano e con la Grande depressione in casa invent脙虏 il New Deal. Noi non dobbiamo inventare niente: dobbiamo solo imparare dalla storia.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Filip Turek, on behalf of the PfE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, ladies and gentlemen, let me share some important numbers that everyone should hear. Only 3.5脗聽% of all CO2 on planet comes from human activity. From this small amount, Europe produces just 8脗聽%, and from Europe's share, only 20脗聽% comes from transport, and an even smaller part comes from making new cars. So think about this: when we put heavy costs on our citizens, when we stop making cars here, when we force our factories to buy expensive permits to operate, what do we actually achieve? For every factory we close in Europe, two new ones open in Asia, usually ones that pollute much more than ours did.

While Donald Trump is protecting the USA, its industry and workers, also through dismissing the Paris Agreement, Ursula von der Leyen and her kind keep destroying our competitiveness with each new regulation and environmental fantasy.

There are 1.5脗聽billion cars in the world. If we stop making traditional cars in Europe, it will change nothing for the global picture. We cannot achieve zero emissions. It's impossible. We cannot have production without any emissions, transport without any emissions or life without any emissions. And even if all of Europe had zero emissions, it would not change the world's weather, or climate, if you want.

I hope these next five years will be the last where we follow the Soviet-style central planning that is destroying our industry. The result of your policies are already devastating, forcing our companies out of global markets and making European products uncompetitive. History will judge this Commission's actions just as it judged those who weakened Europe before. We must return to protecting our production, our workers and our future.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Veronika Vrecionov脙隆, za skupinu ECR. 芒聙聯 Pane p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, na脜隆e konkurenceschopnost je zab脙颅jena byrokraci脙颅 a regulacemi. Na脜隆i farm脙隆脜聶i maj脙颅 zaji脜隆脜楼ovat kvalitn脙颅 potraviny a za to maj脙颅 b脙陆t n脙隆le脜戮it脛聸 odm脛聸脜聢ov脙隆ni. Nemaj脙颅 sed脛聸t u stoh脜炉 pap脙颅r脜炉 nebo tr脙隆vit 脛聧as se stovkami kontrol. Jsem r脙隆da, 脜戮e kone脛聧n脛聸 mluv脙颅me o pot脜聶eb脛聸 zm脛聸ny, ale hlavn脛聸 mus脙颅me za脛聧脙颅t jednat. 脜陆脙隆dn脙漏 dal脜隆脙颅 zpr脙隆vy a strategie ke konkurenceschopnosti n脙隆m nepomohou. Mus脙颅me ud脛聸lat revizi pr脙隆vn脙颅ch p脜聶edpis脜炉, mus脙颅me zajistit pr脙隆vn脙颅 jistotu a p脜聶edv脙颅datelnost prost脜聶ed脙颅. Nen脙颅 mo脜戮n脙漏 p脜聶ij脙颅mat legislativu bez kvalitn脙颅ch dopadov脙陆ch studi脙颅, kter脙漏 budeme dost脙隆vat v脛聧as. A poj脛聫me na tom pracovat hned.
