脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Brigitte van den Berg (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues 芒聙聯 'intensifying our efforts', 'decisive steps', 'business as usual is no longer an option' 芒聙聯 I compliment the Member States for their beautiful words included in the Budapest declaration.

However, I worry that when things become difficult, when Member States have to let go of red lines or bring home a tough message, these words will be much less impressive. Because when push comes to shove, it is this House that has to keep reminding Europe that inaction is not an option. And this means that we need a real European industrial strategy that contains stronger EU policies for energy, stronger EU policy for the capital markets and an ambitious EU budget.

So I call upon my colleagues here. Let us agree to be ambitious. Let us agree to take the Draghi report seriously and implement its recommendations. And let us push the other institutions to follow through on their beautiful declarations.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fran脙搂ois-Xavier Bellamy (PPE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, nous parlons de comp脙漏titivit脙漏 et, derri脙篓re ce mot difficile, il y a une r脙漏alit脙漏 concr脙篓te.

Cet 脙漏t脙漏, lorsque des touristes, am脙漏ricains notamment, sont venus aux Jeux olympiques 脙聽 Paris, ils ont 脙漏t脙漏 frapp脙漏s de l'importance de leur pouvoir d'achat quand ils arrivaient dans la capitale fran脙搂aise, et pour une raison tr脙篓s simple: en脗聽2008, le PIB am脙漏ricain 脙漏tait identique au PIB europ脙漏en.

Quinze ans plus tard, au lendemain d'une crise financi脙篓re d脙漏clench脙漏e par les 脙聣tats-Unis et les banques am脙漏ricaines, nous avons aujourd'hui 80脗聽% de retard sur le PIB des 脙聣tats-Unis d'Am脙漏rique. C'est bien le signe que l'Europe d脙漏croche, qu'elle s'appauvrit. Et pour quelle raison? Parce qu'elle a renonc脙漏 脙聽 produire. Pendant que les Am脙漏ricains subventionnent, nous r脙漏glementons. Il est temps de sortir de cette folie.

J'entendais un coll脙篓gue socialiste nous dire qu'il fallait renoncer au productivisme. Mais que ce soit pour l'agriculture, pour l'industrie, pour la p脙陋che, pour l'脙漏nergie, nous avons au contraire besoin de r脙漏apprendre 脙聽 produire. Produire n'est pas un mot coupable et, pour cela, il faut lib脙漏rer nos entreprises, lib脙漏rer nos agriculteurs, lib脙漏rer ceux qui, partout, font la force de nos pays.

Nous ne laisserons pas la nouvelle Commission europ脙漏enne poursuivre cette strat脙漏gie de d脙漏croissance, que ce soit derri脙篓re Monsieur脗聽Timmermans hier ou脗聽芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, nous le savons bien脗聽芒聙聯 derri脙篓re Madame脗聽Ribera demain. Nous serons aux avant-postes pour faire en sorte qu'enfin, la voix de ceux qui produisent soit entendue partout en Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Philippe Olivier (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, apr脙篓s cinq ans de pacte vert, qui est le nom pudique de la d脙漏croissance, voil脙聽 que, devant l'effondrement industriel, 脙漏nerg脙漏tique et agricole de nos pays, vous brandissez l'id脙漏e de la comp脙漏titivit脙漏 sous l'influence 脙漏clair脙漏e de la Hongrie. Il est vrai qu'il y a urgence: avec son mot d'ordre 脗芦MAGA脗禄, le nouveau pr脙漏sident am脙漏ricain place les Europ脙漏ens face 脙聽 un d脙漏fi de puissance.

Ce pacte de comp脙漏titivit脙漏 porte encore mal son nom; il encha脙庐ne les lieux communs et les contradictions et ne repose sur aucune rupture fondamentale avec les pratiques qui ont conduit l'Europe 脙聽 son d脙漏crochage technique et 脙漏conomique. Comme l'a nettement confirm脙漏 Madame脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen ce matin, vous ne parvenez pas 脙聽 rompre avec votre vision th脙漏ologique du libre-脙漏change d脙漏brid脙漏, avec votre approche normative et, surtout, avec la vision d脙漏croissantiste que les Verts ont instill脙漏e dans les institutions.

Il est 脙漏vident que vous continuerez 脙聽 amener l'Europe 脙聽 l'effacement et 脙聽 la vassalisation tant que vous danserez autour de ce totem de la d脙漏croissance. Nous, nous pr脙漏f脙漏rons, pour l'Europe, la voie de la puissance.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kadri Simson, Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, on behalf of the European Commission, I would like to thank you very much for this rich debate.

This debate shows that across political groups, we have the common goal of ensuring that the EU remains home to a thriving, sustainable and resilient manufacturing industry providing quality jobs across our single market.

In this context, the solution is not less Green Deal, but an industrial deal that helps industry grow 芒聙聯 not despite, but because it decarbonises 芒聙聯 while ensuring a just transition, leaving no one and no territory behind.

The clean industrial deal will help to make decarbonisation an opportunity for growth. This is the spirit expressed in the political guidelines for the next mandate, and that has also guided our industrial policy actions so far.

The Commission looks forward to continuing working with the European Parliament on this issue as a matter of priority.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽J脙隆nos B脙鲁ka, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, Commissioner, thank you for the debate.

The Hungarian Presidency feels particularly privileged to have had the opportunity to facilitate agreement on the Budapest declaration on the new European competitiveness deal, a declaration that also highlights the importance of building economic, social and territorial cohesion.

A declaration that regarding the single market, in line with the recommendations of the Letta report, highlights the need to intensify our efforts to ensure the full functioning of the single market and unlock its full potential.脗聽To that end, the Commission has been invited to present in the first half of 2025, a comprehensive horizontal strategy on deepening the single market with clear timelines and milestones.

But let me conclude by saying this: to bring Europe's competitiveness to the levels we badly need, the competitiveness deal needs to be urgently implemented by all EU institutions in consultation with all stakeholders.

The European Parliament must also play its role. There are several legislative proposals on your desk where you could advance competitiveness by starting or accelerating trilogues, and I encourage you to do so.

For their part, EU leaders will continue to provide further strategic guidance and to regularly review progress over the coming year during the future Polish Presidency and Danish Presidency.

Thank you very much once again for your attention.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The debate is closed.

脗聽

10. Stepping up the fight against and the prevention of the recruitment of minors for criminal acts (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next item on the agenda is the debate on the Commission statement on stepping up the fight against, and the prevention of, the recruitment of minors for criminal acts (2024/2947(RSP).

I give the floor first to Ms Dalli, on behalf of the Commission.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members, criminal networks recruit young people and even children to commit crimes. They are recruited to extract drugs out of ports, to transport, deliver and sell drugs, to be lookouts and even to commit serious violent acts, including murder.

In 2023, in Sweden, more than 300 children aged 15 to 17 were charged with murder or attempted murder. Last month in Marseille, a 14芒聙聭year芒聙聭old boy was recruited online to act as gunman. He killed a taxi driver in an operation orchestrated by drug trafficking groups. These children hired to commit crimes are vulnerable. They are perpetrators, but also victims, and they need our attention as policymakers.

The Commission is determined to take action, to step up prevention and to fight against the recruitment of minors to commit criminal acts. This is why one of the actions of the roadmap to fight drug trafficking and organised crime focused on preventing the recruitment of children into crime. This year, the Commission and the European Crime Prevention Network organised a conference on the recruitment of children. The focus was on actions at local level to prevent youngsters from falling in the hands of criminal groups. Concrete actions should now be part of the European Crime Prevention Network's 2025 work plan.

To tackle the different dimensions of the recruitment of minors into crime, we need a holistic approach. Authorities at all levels of governance and civil society across all sectors need to work together to protect children from violence and abuse. In April this year, the Commission presented recommendations to the Member States on how they can strengthen their child protection systems. Because let's remember that many children who fall in the hands of criminals are victims of violence themselves.

Another dimension is that children and young people spend a lot of time online, and criminals, of course, take advantage of this. Law enforcement and Europol have alerted that children are increasingly recruited via social media. Criminal networks attract young people promoting their lifestyle on social media. They glorify the narco culture and they use influencers to appeal to children. Within the framework of the EU Internet Forum, we are planning to reach out to social media platforms and tech companies to discuss and find solutions to work together against the online recruitment.

Combating the radicalisation, both online and offline, is a key cross芒聙聭cutting EU priority. Central to this effort is the EU Knowledge Hub on Prevention of Radicalisation, launched by the Commission last June. The hub engages with stakeholders beyond the security sector, including education, social policy, media literacy and mental health experts to support Member States. Tackling the root causes of minors' radicalisation will be a core focus for the new Commission. This week, a high芒聙聭level conference is taking place to address the drivers and socio芒聙聭economic factors leading to drug芒聙聭related violence. It will inform our future prevention policies.

And finally, the fight against organised crime will continue to be a top priority in the next Commission. As outlined by President von der Leyen's political guidelines, there can be no hiding place for organised crime in Europe, either offline or online. I trust that a comprehensive policy mix, tackling the different dimensions of minors being recruited into crime, will ensure better protection for children, and that the next Commission will continue to make this a priority. Prevention is at the heart of our strategy, so we ensure that children are safe: safe from organised crime gangs, free to thrive as any child should.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomas Tob脙漏, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, Commissioner, the European Union is actually facing what I would call an epidemic of cross-border organised crime and this is a serious threat to our internal security. Our top priority from this House must be to completely break down the criminal networks and make Europe safe and secure.

The targeted recruitment of minors that are used very tactic by the criminal gangs across Europe, is now also concluded by Europol that minors are involved in more than 70脗聽% of the existing criminal markets.

In my home country, Sweden, it has been reported that we have over 200 children under 18 years old that are suspects in murder investigations. And this is a problem that is not only solely in Sweden. We see this in many, many Member States now and that is why we also need to make sure that we have a strong European response.

Firstly, we need more cross-border police cooperation and a truly operational Europol. We see that when we do things together, we can actually be successful. Just before a couple of weeks, we had an operation coordinated by Europol, but with Spanish, Swedish and Danish police and that led to the arrest of gang members, specifically on the accusation of recruiting minors abroad.

Secondly, we also need to combat the recruitment of minors by criminal networks on social media platforms, and lastly, we also need to work, of course, with crime prevention efforts because that is what is really going to make a result in the long term.

So I very much welcome that we can have this debate today, because we need to go beyond the nice words and actually go into action and stop the criminal gangs.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar, en nombre del Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora comisaria, este debate no solamente es oportuno, sino necesario a la vista del escalofriante informe de inteligencia de la unidad de Europol sobre reclutamiento de menores en la Uni脙鲁n Europea, que pone de manifiesto que hasta el 70脗聽% de la actividad delictiva 芒聙聰sobre todo en ciberdelincuencia y en tr脙隆fico de drogas芒聙聰 implica o involucra a menores con el incentivo econ脙鲁mico, adem脙隆s de la dificultad de su puesta en conexi脙鲁n con las redes internacionales de criminalidad organizada y su procesamiento.

Una primera recomendaci脙鲁n ser脙颅a armonizar las leyes de los Estados miembros en materia de responsabilidad penal de los menores. Pero hay, sobre todo, que actuar para ejecutar la hoja de ruta aprobada por la Comisi脙鲁n Europea para prevenir la delincuencia organizada y el tr脙隆fico de drogas en la medida en que afecta fuertemente a menores.

Y una lecci脙鲁n clara es la prevenci脙鲁n: adem脙隆s de la inteligencia y de la cooperaci脙鲁n judicial, compartir inteligencia e informaci脙鲁n para prevenir la implicaci脙鲁n de menores en el tr脙隆fico de drogas tiene que convertirse en una prioridad absoluta de la pol脙颅tica penal de escala europea, en la que este Parlamento es legislador penal desde la entrada en vigor del Tratado de Lisboa.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Georgiana Teodorescu, 脙庐n numele grupului ECR. 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, viitorul omenirii nu poate exista f脛聝r脛聝 copii. Pentru ei tr脛聝im, pentru ei ne lupt脛聝m 脙庐n fiecare zi, inclusiv aici, 脙庐n Parlamentul European, pentru a construi o lume mai sigur脛聝 脠聶i mai bun脛聝. Inocen脠聸a lor este completat脛聝 de o fragilitate pe m脛聝sur脛聝. Pot deveni victime cu prea mare u脠聶urin脠聸脛聝 ale infractorilor dornici s脛聝 脙庐i foloseasc脛聝. Rapoarte recente arat脛聝 c脛聝 40脗聽% din tinerii implica脠聸i 脙庐n bandele criminale au sub 18脗聽ani, cu rate alarmant de mari ale copiilor de doar 12 sau 13脗聽ani implica脠聸i 脙庐n crime organizate, 脙庐ntruc脙垄t minorii sub 14 ani nu r脛聝spund penal 脙庐n foarte multe 脠聸脛聝ri ale lumii.

Nu putem sta deoparte 脙庐n timp ce copiii vulnerabili devin victime ale exploat脛聝rii criminale. Este timpul s脛聝 intensific脛聝m lupta 脙庐mpotriva acestei pl脛聝gi. M脛聝surile de prevenire trebuie s脛聝 se concentreze pe o educa脠聸ie c脙垄t mai performant脛聝 脠聶i pe o poli脠聸ie c脙垄t mai profesionist脛聝. Totu脠聶i, la fel de important, trebuie s脛聝 ne asigur脛聝m c脛聝 ace脠聶ti copii, atunci c脙垄nd sunt salva脠聸i, beneficiaz脛聝 de sprijin pentru reintegrare 脙庐n societate 脠聶i pentru reabilitare. 脙聨mpreun脛聝 putem rupe cercul exploat脛聝rii, proteja copiii no脠聶tri 脠聶i construi o lume mai sigur脛聝 脠聶i mai dreapt脛聝 pentru to脠聸i.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Morten L脙赂kkegaard, for Renew-Gruppen. 芒聙聯 Hr. formand! Unge i Europa bliver i stigende grad udnyttet til beskidt arbejde i den kriminelle underverdenens kyniske spil. Det er en sk脙娄ndsel, og det skal vi ikke acceptere. N脙楼r unge rekrutteres til kriminalitet, mister vi ikke bare deres potentiale, vi mister ogs脙楼 troen p脙楼 fremtiden. I Danmark, mit hjemland, ser vi det allertydeligst i 脙赂jeblikket. I det seneste 脙楼r er op mod ti svenske unge blevet rekrutteret til at udf脙赂re mord og alvorlige angreb med blandt andet h脙楼ndgranater. De fleste er blevet hyret via de sociale medier, som vi allerede har h脙赂rt om. S脙楼 sent som i g脙楼r blev en svensk dreng id脙赂mt fem 脙楼r og seks m脙楼neders f脙娄ngsel for drab i Danmarks hovedstad K脙赂benhavn. De unge bliver betalt op mod tohundredetusind danske kroner. Det er over femogtyvetusind euro per gang for at beg脙楼 et angreb - fra overfald til drab. Det er ikke blot vanvittigt, det er et totalt gr脙娄nseoverskridende problem. Vi skal virkelig have gjort noget ved det f脙娄lleseurop脙娄isk. EU har brug for en f脙娄lles strategi, der prioriterer forebyggelse, beskyttelse og konsekvente sanktioner mod dem, der st脙楼r bag. Vi m脙楼 intensivere kampen mod organiseret kriminalitet. Det handler om de unge. Det handler om vores fremtid. Det handler om en f脙娄lleseurop脙娄isk indsats.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Giuseppe Antoci, a nome del gruppo The Left. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, come sottolineato da Europol, la criminalit脙聽 organizzata recluta giovani attraverso i social media, utilizzando linguaggi codificati, trasformando la carriera criminale in un gioco a tappe e premi. Questo fenomeno, diffuso in tutto il continente, rappresenta ormai una strategia operativa consolidata.

In Europa l'incremento della criminalit脙聽 minorile 脙篓 allarmante. Troppi giovani, privi di supporto familiare e spesso accomunati dall'abbandono scolastico precoce, cadono preda di sfruttatori senza scrupoli.

Come legislatori europei dobbiamo impedire che il futuro di questi giovani sia segnato dal carcere o dal riformatorio. L'Europa deve trarre ispirazione da figure come padre Pino Puglisi e Don Bosco, che con il loro approccio preventivo hanno dimostrato come si pu脙虏 cambiare il destino dei giovani pi脙鹿 vulnerabili tramite educazione e intervento precoce.

Concludo riflettendo che, ogni volta che si risponde a un reato solo con pene pi脙鹿 severe, segniamo una sconfitta: la vittoria sta nel prevenire, comprendere e intervenire prima che i giovani si perdano.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bart脜聜omiej Sienkiewicz (PPE). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Nie mo脜录na zamkn脛聟脛聡 oczu na rzeczywisto脜聸脛聡 芒聙聯 to has脜聜o przewodnie. Ju脜录 par脛聶na脜聸cie lat temu 脜聸wiatowa opinia publiczna by脜聜a zszokowana informacjami o dzieciach-脜录o脜聜nierzach w Afryce. Ze zgroz脛聟 czytali脜聸my doniesienia o narastaj脛聟cej fali wykorzystywania dzieci jako okrutnych 脜录o脜聜nierzy. Czyny te kwalifikowane s脛聟 przez prawo mi脛聶dzynarodowe jako zbrodnie wojenne. I teraz tu, w Parlamencie Europejskim, dyskutujemy o dzieciach-zab脙鲁jcach w Europie, rekrutowanych przez gangi narkotykowe.

Niech was nie zmyli odmienno脜聸脛聡. Tam 脜录o脜聜nierze, tu gangsterzy. Tam Afryka, tu Europa. To jest to samo, bo na koniec uzbrojone dziecko zabija na ulicach. Co si脛聶 sta脜聜o z Europ脛聟 przez te par脛聶na脜聸cie lat? Nie mo脜录na zamyka脛聡 oczu na rzeczywisto脜聸脛聡. Te gangi maj脛聟 etniczny charakter i powsta脜聜y w wyniku braku umiej脛聶tno脜聸ci integracji imigrant脙鲁w. Aby unikn脛聟脛聡 pi脛聶tnowania imigrant脙鲁w, pa脜聞stwa, kt脙鲁re to dotkn脛聶脜聜o, musz脛聟 wyznaczy脛聡 system drastycznych kar dla przest脛聶pc脙鲁w rekrutuj脛聟cych dzieci. Tylko w ten spos脙鲁b ten proces nie b脛聶dzie si脛聶 op脜聜aca脜聜 europejskim narkotykowym warlordom. Je脜聸li to si脛聶 nie stanie, nadal na tej sali b脛聶dziemy m脙鲁wi脛聡 o warto脜聸ciach europejskich, demokracji i prawach podstawowych, ale na ulicach europejskich miast nadal b脛聶d脛聟 gin脛聟脛聡 ludzie, a zab脙鲁jcami b脛聶d脛聟 dzieci, zarazem ofiary i kaci.

Pani Dalii, jedno zdanie: kiedy s脜聜ysz脛聶 芒聙聻podej脜聸cie holistyczne芒聙聺, to wiem, 脜录e to s脜聜owo oznacza bezradno脜聸脛聡.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Evin Incir (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, gangs across the European Union are recruiting children and young people. Society has failed our children.

Growing up in the district of Bergsj脙露n in Gothenburg in Sweden, I loved my community, but we faced challenges I wouldn't wish on anyone. Already in the 1990s, we witnessed schoolmates being recruited. Today, in 2024, the situation has worsened, with even younger children being targeted and involved in violence. Children are killing grown芒聙聭ups. Children are killing children.

New technology has made also homes unsafe and a place for the gangs to recruit. Gangs, exploit digital platforms to recruit children using influencer tactics and emotional manipulation. They frame illegal activities as challenges or missions, giving kids a false sense of purpose. We must act now to ensure that every child belongs in school, not on the streets with weapons.

I urge, therefore, to the Commission to create a strategy to prevent the recruitment of youth into organised crime. Additionally, we need to hold the digital platforms accountable for protecting our children's welfare and to ensure the security of the whole European Union.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ton Diepeveen (PfE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, de jeugdcriminaliteit is een groot en ernstig probleem. In 2023 werden in Nederland ruim zestienduizend minderjarigen in de leeftijd van 12脗聽tot 18脗聽jaar als verdachte van een misdrijf geregistreerd. In de leeftijd van 18脗聽tot 23脗聽jaar waren dat er nog eens ruim drie脙芦ntwintigduizend. Minderjarigen worden dan ook steeds vaker gebruikt door internationaal opererende bendes voor de uitvoering van hun criminele activiteiten. Wat deze bendes daarbij helpt, is het vrije verkeer van personen binnen de EU. Zo worden minderjarigen door hen gebruikt voor het uithalen van drugs uit containers in de havens van Rotterdam en Antwerpen, en in de andere havens binnen de EU. Maar ook zwaardere misdrijven, waaronder het plegen van aanslagen met explosieven zoals Cobra芒聙聭vuurwerk, worden meer en meer door minderjarigen uitgevoerd.

Het is van groot belang dat we als samenleving duidelijk maken dat criminaliteit niet wordt getolereerd, ongeacht de leeftijd. Jongeren die zich schuldig maken aan misdrijven, moeten verantwoordelijk worden gehouden voor hun daden. Dit betekent dat we striktere maatregelen moeten nemen tegen jeugdige delinquenten en hun ouders, bij wie de opvoedkundige verantwoordelijkheid ligt. In ieder geval dient het jeugdstrafrecht te worden versterkt, waarbij de lidstaten de handen ineen moeten slaan.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Charlie Weimers (ECR). 芒聙聯 Herr talman! F脙露rr i Sverige talade vi om barnsoldater i fj脙陇rran l脙陇nder. Det k脙陇ndes overkligt. I脗聽dag finns dessa barnsoldater i de flesta svenska st脙陇der. Sverige har h脙露gst antal d脙露dsskjutningar per inv脙楼nare i Europa efter Montenegro och Albanien. Ett stort antal av dessa d脙露dsskjutningar i Sverige utf脙露rs av barn. 脙聟r 2013 skedde 24脗聽d脙露dsskjutningar; 2022 var siffran脗聽61 芒聙聯 mer 脙陇n en f脙露rdubbling p脙楼 mindre 脙陇n tio脗聽脙楼r.

Den okontrollerade massinvandringen har f脙露r脙陇ndrat v脙楼ra samh脙陇llen till n脙陇st intill oigenk脙陇nnlighet. Migrantbrottsligheten d脙陇r f脙露r脙露varna 脙陇r bev脙陇pnade barn 脙露kar konstant, och bland personer 15芒聙聯20脗聽脙楼r 脙露kade d脙露dligt v脙楼ld med skjutvapen fr脙楼n cirka 17脗聽fall 2012 till 45脗聽fall脗聽2023 芒聙聯 en 脙露kning med 160脗聽procent.

Tillsammans med kraftfulla 脙楼tg脙陇rder som vi sverigedemokrater drivit igenom p脙楼 hemmaplan mot brottsligheten beh脙露ver hela EU en l脙楼ng paus fr脙楼n asylinvandring. Vi m脙楼ste gemensamt st脙陇rka fokus p脙楼 att bek脙陇mpa organiserad brottslighet och vi beh脙露ver en strategi f脙露r att hantera och neutralisera klanbaserade kriminella n脙陇tverk inom hela脗聽EU.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Mitmenschen! Ich habe acht Jahre lang in K脙露ln芒聙聭Kalk gelebt, einem Stadtviertel mit 脙录berdurchschnittlicher Armut und deshalb 脙录berdurchschnittlicher Kriminalit脙陇t. Acht Jahre lang haben mir 14-J脙陇hrige auf meinem Heimweg Marihuana angeboten, und den Rest der Zeit waren diese Kinder damit besch脙陇ftigt, cool in der Gegend rumzugucken.

Aber in den letzten Jahren ist mir etwas aufgefallen: Die gucken nicht mehr cool in der Gegend rum, die gucken auf ihr Handy. Und warum? Das hat uns Europol Anfang des Jahres verraten: weil das Internet der Hauptort f脙录r die Anwerbung und Organisation von Jugendlichen f脙录r Straftaten geworden ist. Deshalb m脙录ssen wir da mit unserem Rechtsstaat stark reagieren. Wir m脙录ssen die Plattformbetreiber unmittelbar in die Pflicht nehmen, Inhalte, die Straftaten feiern oder dazu anwerben, zu l脙露schen und die entsprechenden Vorf脙陇lle direkt an die Beh脙露rden zu melden. Und wir m脙录ssen Digitalkompetenz in ganz Europa zum Schulfach machen, um bei den jungen Menschen das Bewusstsein f脙录r dieses Problem zu st脙陇rken. Sie sind jung, manchmal dumm, aber sie brauchen uns.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Verena Mertens (PPE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, sehr geehrte Frau Kommissarin, liebe Kollegen! Die Rekrutierung von Kindern und Jugendlichen zeigt uns die Schwachstellen unserer Pr脙陇ventionspolitik und unserer internationalen Zusammenarbeit. Ich fordere deshalb, dass wir nicht nur reden, sondern auch handeln. Wir brauchen entschlossene Ma脙聼nahmen von der EU und den Mitgliedstaaten.

Wir brauchen erstens eine europaweite Strategie zur Pr脙陇vention, die gezielt soziale Brennpunkte in den Blick nimmt. Wir m脙录ssen strukturelle Probleme wie soziale Ausgrenzung und Perspektivlosigkeit bek脙陇mpfen, bevor kriminelle Netzwerke diese L脙录cken f脙录llen. Und wir m脙录ssen gefl脙录chtete Menschen und ihre Kinder wirksam und nachhaltig in unsere Gesellschaft integrieren.

Zweitens: Um die T脙陇ter ermitteln und verfolgen zu k脙露nnen, brauchen wir bessere Daten, mehr Ressourcen und effektivere Mechanismen zur Strafverfolgung 脙录ber Staatsgrenzen hinaus. Grenz脙录berschreitende Kriminalit脙陇t erfordert grenz脙录berschreitende Antworten, auch gemeinsam mit Europol.

Und drittens: Die St脙陇rkung der Medienkompetenz muss Priorit脙陇t haben. Junge Menschen m脙录ssen verstehen, wie sie in der digitalen Welt gesch脙录tzt bleiben. Europa kann und muss hier Vorreiter sein f脙录r eine Jugend, die in Freiheit und Sicherheit aufw脙陇chst.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Cecilia Strada (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signora Commissaria, pensare di combattere la criminalit脙聽 giovanile con metodi securitari 脙篓 un approccio fallimentare. Basta osservare quanto sta succedendo nel mio Paese: da quando il governo italiano, approvando il decreto Caivano, ha deciso di usare il pugno di ferro contro i minori, gli istituti penali minorili italiani sono diventati sovraffollati e questo ovviamente a scapito delle attivit脙聽 formative e di reinserimento.

Quando quelle ragazze e quei ragazzi usciranno dal carcere minorile, torneranno nei loro quartieri ghettizzati e abbandonati dalle istituzioni, dove, se non offriamo loro delle alternative vere, rischiano di tornare nell'orbita della criminalit脙聽.

La prevenzione della criminalit脙聽 giovanile si fa dando strumenti e opportunit脙聽 ai ragazzi e alle ragazze cresciuti nella vulnerabilit脙聽, non buttandoli in galera per tenerli lontani dalla nostra vista. Il securitarismo abbandona i pi脙鹿 deboli, li bolla come feccia, ci spinge a pensare che i bambini, i minori stranieri non accompagnati, siano dei pericolosi criminali anzich脙漏 le prime vittime. Ma 脙篓 un approccio che non risolve il problema, anzi.

Che l'Europa non segua l'esempio distruttivo del governo italiano: ci sono altre strade pi脙鹿 giuste e pi脙鹿 efficaci da perseguire.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matthieu Valet (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, 脙聽 Marseille, Socayna, 24脗聽ans, 脙漏tudiante en droit, habitante d'une cit脙漏 de gens modestes, a 脙漏t脙漏 abattue 脙聽 l'arme de guerre dans sa chambre par un tueur 脙聽 gage de 16脗聽ans. 脙聙 Ch脙垄teauroux, Matisse, 15脗聽ans, a 脙漏t脙漏 tu脙漏 sauvagement par un mineur afghan de 15脗聽ans, d脙漏j脙聽 connu des policiers et des juges, comme d'habitude, malheureusement. 脙聙 Cr脙漏pol, Thomas, 16脗聽ans, a 脙漏t脙漏 lynch脙漏 mort 脙聽 coups de couteau, certains des auteurs 脙漏taient mineurs. Vous remarquerez que j'ai toujours cit脙漏 les victimes avant les auteurs. C'est essentiel dans le combat contre la d脙漏linquance.

Quand cette ultra-violence va-t-elle arr脙陋ter de tuer? D'assassiner nos enfants? Quand aurez-vous le courage, en France et dans toute l'Europe, de supprimer l'excuse de minorit脙漏 pour les mineurs de 16 脙聽 18脗聽ans? Car, n'en d脙漏plaise au Syndicat de la magistrature d'extr脙陋me gauche, chez nous, en France, ce ne sont pas des bisounours. On n'est plus en脗聽1945 avec Casimir: ce sont des criminels.

脙聙 part les mots, les recueillements, les tweets, les bougies: qu'ont fait nos gouvernants? Rien! Ils ont regard脙漏 les trains passer avec leur lot de victimes. Je suis en col脙篓re, car, dans cette Assembl脙漏e, ancien policier de la R脙漏publique, je pense 脙陋tre l'un des seuls 脙聽 avoir d脙禄 annoncer la mort d'un enfant 芒聙聯脗聽la mort d'un bambin脗聽芒聙聯 脙聽 des parents sans histoire, qui avaient tout donn脙漏 pour 脙漏duquer leur enfant, droit dans les yeux et l'脙漏motion 脙聽 la gorge.

脙聙 ces parents qui ont perdu une partie de leur 脙垄me, je veux dire que je me bats pour eux, pour que justice soit rendue et que cette culture de l'excuse ne fasse plus jamais d'autres victimes. Tous ensemble, nous devons 脙陋tre mobilis脙漏s contre ceux qui assassinent notre jeunesse.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mario Mantovani (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, c'脙篓 un caso emblematico di minori reclutati dalla criminalit脙聽 e riguarda in particolare i minori stranieri non accompagnati.

Si tratta di oltre 50.000 giovani, soprattutto provenienti dal Nord Africa, che negli ultimi due anni sono facile preda della criminalit脙聽 organizzata.

Finora l'Unione europea su questo tema ha fallito. 脙聢 stata assente addossando il problema agli Stati nazionali, gi脙聽 oberati di emergenze e di problemi di bilancio.

Servono nuove politiche che prevedano, da un lato, regole ferme perch脙漏 questi minori, che hanno et脙聽 superiore a 15 anni, frequentino corsi professionali finalizzati all'inserimento lavorativo.

In caso contrario si deve prevedere il loro rimpatrio nei Paesi d'origine, luogo certamente migliore rispetto agli ambienti oscuri dove droga, furti e prostituzione regnano sovrani.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

IN THE CHAIR: SOPHIE WILM脙聢S
Vice-President

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alice Teodorescu M脙楼we (PPE). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Europas s脙陇kerhet hotas s脙楼v脙陇l utifr脙楼n som inifr脙楼n. Vi m脙楼ste nu orka bist脙楼 s脙楼v脙陇l Ukraina som Israel, samtidigt som vi hanterar den organiserade brottsligheten, som hotar v脙楼ra medborgares trygghet och d脙陇rmed frihet.

I dag 脙陇r 70脗聽procent av de kriminella g脙陇ngen i EU verksamma i minst tre medlemsl脙陇nder, vilket illustrerar behovet av ett intensifierat gr脙陇ns脙露verskridande samarbete.脗聽Vi beh脙露ver st脙陇rka Europol med resurser, ett ut脙露kat utbyte av databaser och brottsregister, liksom ett intensifierat r脙陇ttsligt samarbete med tredjel脙陇nder och med EU:s kandidatl脙陇nder.

Europa, och inte minst Sverige, beh脙露ver prioritera friheten f脙露r sk脙露tsamma och laglydiga medborgare nu. De som betalar f脙露r EU-samarbetet ska 脙楼tminstone kunna f脙楼 h脙露gsta m脙露jliga s脙陇kerhet i geng脙陇ld.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Birgit Sippel (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Die Warnung von Europol ist deutlich: Immer mehr kriminelle Banden suchen gezielt Minderj脙陇hrige, um sie f脙录r die Durchf脙录hrung von Straftaten zu missbrauchen und selbst der Strafverfolgung zu entgehen. Neu daran ist, in wie vielen L脙陇ndern dieses Verfahren strategisch genutzt wird, dass die Minderj脙陇hrigen f脙录r immer schwerere Straftaten脗聽芒聙聯 auch Mord 芒聙聯 rekrutiert werden und mit welchen Taktiken das geschieht脗聽芒聙聯 脙录ber soziale Medien in Jugendsprache und mit als Spiel verharmlosten Straftaten.

Nicht neu ist, wer meist die Opfer dieser Taktik sind: junge Menschen, die in Armut leben, einen schlechteren Zugang zu guter Bildung und damit weniger Chancen auf gute Arbeit haben, junge Menschen auf der Suche nach Zugeh脙露rigkeit und Anerkennung. Auf diese jungen Menschen m脙录ssen wir uns konzentrieren. Sie brauchen Anerkennung und echte Chancen, sich in unserer Gesellschaft zu entfalten, unabh脙陇ngig von ihrer sozialen, finanziellen oder sonstigen Herkunft 芒聙聯 frei von Diskriminierung.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Caterina Chinnici (PPE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, il reclutamento di minori per commettere reati 脙篓 particolarmente odioso, perch脙漏 le prime vittime sono gli stessi minori reclutati, sottratti alla loro innocenza e sfruttati da criminali privi di scrupoli.

Non 脙篓 una novit脙聽, certo, ma negli ultimi anni il fenomeno si 脙篓 intensificato: secondo un'allarmante nota diramata da Europol pochi giorni fa, bambini e ragazzi sono coinvolti in pi脙鹿 del 70% dei settori criminali: cybercrime, frodi online, traffico di droga, traffico di migranti, reati contro il patrimonio e atti sempre pi脙鹿 violenti come estorsioni e omicidi.

Le tecniche di reclutamento si fanno sempre pi脙鹿 sofisticate e subdole, con il massiccio ricorso ad app, social media e piattaforme online che assicurano, anonimato e contatti agevoli. I criminali poi manipolano facilmente i pi脙鹿 giovani, adottando il loro stesso linguaggio e tecniche di cosiddetta gamification.

Tutto ci脙虏 richiede azioni mirate di contrasto e misure di prevenzione ma anche interventi sul disagio sociale e sull'abbandono scolastico. Perch脙漏 l'illegalit脙聽 spesso 芒聙聯 e lo so per esperienza personale 芒聙聯 trova terreno fertile dove i ragazzi non vedono via d'uscita a solitudine e disagio.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alexandre Varaut (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, l'avocat que je fus entretient un dialogue difficile avec le d脙漏put脙漏 que je suis devenu. Malgr脙漏 tout, notre piti脙漏, nous devons l'orienter essentiellement vers les victimes, qui subissent des actes sauvages, parfois barbares, perp脙漏tr脙漏s de plus en plus souvent et de plus en plus t脙麓t. Tout le monde a encore en t脙陋te ces hommes martyris脙漏s, comme ce chauffeur de taxi tu脙漏 d'une balle 脙聽 Marseille par un mineur 脙垄g脙漏 de quatorze ans.

Les mineurs criminels sont des mineurs, mais ce sont surtout des criminels. Nous devons savoir discerner, en fonction des personnes et des actes, lesquels de ces mineurs, souvent des immigr脙漏s, mineurs isol脙漏s, sont pour partie des victimes de leur milieu, et lesquels ont d脙漏j脙聽 une personnalit脙漏 violente et endurcie. Aux uns, nous pouvons chercher 脙聽 leur redonner une chance; 脙聽 l'脙漏gard des autres, nous devons appliquer et renforcer notre l脙漏gislation.

Nous devons punir plus t脙麓t, avant qu'ils ne s'enfoncent davantage dans les 脗芦avantages脗禄 de la d脙漏linquance. Les N脙漏erlandais ont su appliquer avec succ脙篓s des peines courtes, mais imm脙漏diates. Voil脙聽 le seul chemin: surveiller, contr脙麓ler et punir, avant que les avantages du crime soient sup脙漏rieurs aux risques encourus.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

Catch-the-eye procedure

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maria Grapini (S&D). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, stima脠聸i colegi, cred c脛聝 nimic nu este mai important dec脙垄t s脛聝 protej脛聝m minorii, viitoarea genera脠聸ie. Totu脠聶i, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, nu prin conferin脠聸e rezolv脛聝m aceast脛聝 problem脛聝. Avem noi o strategie? Repet, ca s脛聝 asculta脠聸i, nu prin conferin脠聸e, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, rezolv脛聝m aceast脛聝 problem脛聝. Avem noi o strategie armonizat脛聝 脙庐n Uniunea European脛聝 pentru distrugerea re脠聸elelor infractorilor? Asta ar trebui s脛聝 fie prima m脛聝sur脛聝, s脛聝 nu mai existe ofert脛聝 脙庐n pia脠聸a Uniunii Europene, s脛聝 nu mai existe cine s脛聝 atrag脛聝 ace脠聶ti minori.

Apoi, o strategie pentru a avea pedepse armonizate la fel de mari 脙庐n toate statele membre. Fug dintr-un stat 脙庐n altul. Pedepsele... sigur, justi脠聸ia este de competen脠聸脛聝 na脠聸ional脛聝, dar 脙庐脠聶i g脛聝sesc refugiu 脙庐ntr-o 脠聸ar脛聝 脙庐n care pedepsele sunt mai mici. Educa脠聸ia 脙庐n 脠聶coli, educa脠聸ia 脠聸intit脛聝 脙庐n 脠聶coli, nu conferin脠聸e. Cine vine la conferin脠聸e? Vin infractorii, vin copiii? Vin minorii? A脠聶adar, dac脛聝 nu o s脛聝 facem aceast脛聝 strategie, vom face mereu conferin脠聸e, vom face mereu dezbateri 脠聶i num脛聝rul copiilor ataca脠聸i de aceste grup脛聝ri va cre脠聶te.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, Suomessakin ollaan ajauduttu jo tilanteeseen, jossa yleisin pahoinpitelyst脙陇 ep脙陇ilty on 14-vuotias. Ruotsissa nuoria jopa rekrytoidaan Tanskassa teht脙陇viin palkkamurhiin ja pommien r脙陇j脙陇ytyksiin.

Mist脙陇 t脙陇m脙陇nkin ongelman kasvu p脙陇脙陇osin johtuu? Miksi rikoksiin on helppo houkutella ja yllytt脙陇脙陇 yh脙陇 nuorempia ja nuorempia ihmisi脙陇? Mist脙陇 t脙陇llainen v脙陇kivallan kulttuuri on pulpahtanut pinnalle? Vastaus on hallitsematon maahanmuutto ja heikko integroituminen l脙陇nsimaiseen yhteiskuntaan.

Vasemmistopuolueiden ikuinen mantra on ollut, ett脙陇 puolueet oikealla puhuvat vain maahanmuutosta. Pakkohan siit脙陇 nyt on puhua. Katsokaa mit脙陇 Ruotsissa ja Saksassa ja Ranskassa tapahtuu hallitsemattoman maahanmuuton seurauksena. Hiljaako t脙陇st脙陇 pit脙陇isi olla? Eurooppa on ajettu kaaokseen, kun on hyssytelty tosiasioita. Se on voitava sanoa 脙陇脙陇neen. Hallitsematon maahanmuutto on uhka Euroopalle.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Thank you, President, and thank you all, honourable Members, for your contribution to this important debate. Criminal networks do not hesitate to recruit young people to perpetrate crimes. This is a complex reality as these minors are criminals and victims at the same time.

We need to protect all our citizens. The radicalisation of minors is a cross-cutting priority in combating radicalisation. Tackling minors' recruitment by criminals also requires the recruitment of children via online platforms. The EU Internet Forum can be useful and we will need the involvement of Eurojust.

Tackling the root causes of minors' radicalisation and the fight against organised crime will be a core focus for the new Commission. Our response shall be commensurate with the challenges. It is about the future of our children, the future of our societies, and we count on this Parliament's contribution to this fight.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The debate is then closed.

脗聽

11. Critical infrastructure vulnerabilities and hybrid threats in the Baltic Sea (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next item on the agenda is the debate on the Commission statement on critical infrastructure vulnerabilities and hybrid threats in the Baltic Sea (2024/2956(RSP).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members, this month, we witnessed the disruption of submarine cables between Lithuania and Sweden, and between Finland and Germany. Once again, we saw serious security challenges to the EU's critical infrastructure. The maritime domain such as the Baltic Sea is especially vulnerable. We share the strong concerns expressed by all affected Member States.

These incidents are too familiar. Remember the cable disruptions of the Balticconnector only a year ago. With Russia on the warpath and with China on the rise, Europe needs to be on the ball to ensure its security and to stand united in the face of hybrid threats. We cannot stand idly by and just watch these incidents repeating.

We welcome the swift launch of national investigations. Disruptions may have ripple effects beyond the national borders and beyond economic sectors, potentially affecting millions of EU citizens. This is why the EU has a role to play. The security and resilience of national critical infrastructure resilience is vital to Europe as a whole. What is vital for one is vital for all.

Putin's war of aggression is, of course, a tragedy. We passed the terrible mark of 1脗聽000 days of war in Ukraine 芒聙聯 1脗聽000 days of resistance, but also of death and also of pain. We are also witnessing an increasing number of hybrid attacks, cyberattacks, information manipulation and interference campaigns, signals jamming, cases of arson, vandalism and sabotage. Last month, the EU High Representative strongly condemned Russia's intensifying campaign of hybrid activities against the EU and its partners. We must be on our guard and take action.

Under this mandate, the Commission put forward a proposal for a Directive on the resilience of critical entities that you adopted two years ago. With this agreement, we already ensure better coordination and preparedness of our societies and economies to threats and disruptions. At the same time, you adopted the Directive on a high level of cybersecurity across the Union to enhance the overall cybersecurity and digital resilience of critical infrastructure. We took action. We expect Member States to now do the same, to transpose both directives as soon as possible. Too few of them have done so yet.

The Cyber Solidarity Act, now in the final stages of adoption, will help us to address cyberattacks. In response to the explosions affecting the Nord Stream 2 gas pipeline, the Commission proposed a Council recommendation on resilience of critical infrastructure, addressing preparedness, response and international cooperation with concrete measures. Member States conducted stress tests of critical infrastructure in the energy sector to identify vulnerabilities. They are now following up to be fully prepared in the event of sabotage.

We are also taking action on the weak spot we are discussing today: the vulnerability of submarine cables. In February this year, the Commission adopted a recommendation on secure and resilient submarine cable infrastructures. The objective is triple: improve coordination, enhance governance and increase funding. It calls for the mapping of submarine cables, assessment of their vulnerabilities and risks, and mitigating measures. These measures on undersea cables were part of the report on preparedness presented by President脗聽Niinist脙露 in October.

Undersea cables also connect us to the world. We need more cooperation with our international partners such as NATO and the G7. During this mandate, keeping Europe safe was our priority and Putin's war of aggression with hostile actors committing sabotaging only increases our need for security.

This Commission is proud to have put the resilience of critical infrastructure on the EU agenda, but more needs to be done, of course. I am confident that the next Commission will continue this work with your support. We must remain vigilant and build our own resilience and be ready to respond quickly and decisively.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mika Aaltola, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, as President Niinist脙露's report highlighted, all economies, whether democratic or totalitarian, are highly dependent on data flows through undersea cables. In the Baltic Sea region, these vulnerable cables are crucial for all coastal states, including my own home country, Finland, which has the longest land border in the EU with Russia.

Recently, a Chinese ship operating from Russia, allegedly severed two of these cables. This action, along with the previous acts on a gas pipeline and another data cable, raises concerns that China is testing responses for its strategic goals. If China were to blockade Taiwan militarily, it might begin with disrupting cables, satellite communications, with similar actions that we are seeing in the Baltic Sea. This also highlights the coordination with Russia. These two countries, as they say, are better than allies.

These cables and pipelines are vulnerable to sabotage in hybrid warfare to enhance security. We should replace them with active versions equipped with sensors and deploy undersea surveillance drones. This would reduce the plausible deniability that Russia so well exploits. In the 1982 United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea, it leaves unambiguous the necessary actions that have to be taken. We must be prepared to board and detain ships, as outlined already in the telegraph cable law from the 1880s.

The European Union aims to safeguard underwater cables through collaboration, but this initiative is set to be completed only by 2035, far too late. We must go beyond sanctions and we have to start thinking about also blocking Russian data flows.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sabrina Repp, im Namen der S&D-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Die j脙录ngsten Vorf脙陇lle in der Ostsee sind ein alarmierendes Signal. Am 18.脗聽November wurde das Unterseekabel C-Lion1 zwischen Finnland und Deutschland besch脙陇digt, kurz zuvor ein weiteres Kabel zwischen Litauen und Schweden. Russland und China verst脙陇rken ihre Aktivit脙陇ten in der Region, kartographieren die Ostsee mit sogenannten Forschungsschiffen, und gezielte Sabotage scheint wahrscheinlich. Solche Angriffe auf kritische Infrastruktur sind nicht nur inakzeptabel, sie sind ein direkter Angriff auf die Stabilit脙陇t und den Zusammenhalt unserer Gesellschaft. Das verurteilen wir aufs Sch脙陇rfste.

Unsere Infrastruktur ist verwundbar. Darum brauchen wir robustere Kabel, Satellitenl脙露sungen und klare Resilienzpl脙陇ne, um besonders wichtige Daten脙录bertragungen zu priorisieren. Gleichzeitig muss die EU mehr in eigene Infrastruktur investieren und die grenz脙录berschreitende Zusammenarbeit st脙陇rken. F脙录r meine Heimatstadt Rostock und ganz Europa ist das eine dringende Notwendigkeit. Jetzt ist der Moment, um entschlossen zu handeln脗聽芒聙聯脗聽gezielt und gemeinsam. Wir k脙露nnen Angriffe auf unsere kritische Infrastruktur nicht zulassen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rihards Kols, on behalf of the ECR Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, today the lifeblood of our civilisation lies in its infrastructure 芒聙聯 facilitating communication, transport, commerce and security. Severing these lifelines, as we have seen with two undersea cables in the Baltic Sea, can have catastrophic consequences for our societies and economies.

Once might have been an accident; twice demands a deliberate ignorance to call it anything but sabotage. These calculated hybrid attacks are designed to test our defences, spread fear and weaken European resolve.

According to some, the Baltic Sea had become a NATO lake, a secure zone for our allies. But recent events revealed vulnerabilities that demand immediate action. We must first strengthen maritime surveillance and monitoring. Second, deploy rapid response teams for critical infrastructure. Third, establish exclusive controlled shipping routes to reduce risks. And lastly, impose real consequences 芒聙聯 economic and political 芒聙聯 on those responsible.

Europe is under attack and it's time we defend it. Otherwise they will keep on coming.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ville Niinist脙露, Verts/ALE-ryhm脙陇n puolesta. 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, uusin tapaus on vain yksi tapaus siin脙陇 sarjassa, jossa It脙陇merell脙陇 tapahtuu EU:n kannalta kriittisten yhteyksien katkomista.脗聽Viime kerran, kun n脙陇in tapahtui Balticconnector-kaasuputken ja sen yhteydess脙陇 olevan datakaapelin osalta, niin silloinkin p脙陇脙陇ep脙陇ilty oli kiinalainen alus. Niin on nyt my脙露s t脙陇ll脙陇 kertaa. Me ep脙陇ilemme, ett脙陇 kyseess脙陇 on kiinalaisen aluksen aiheuttama vaurio.

Me tied脙陇mme, ett脙陇 Ven脙陇j脙陇 harrastaa hybridisodank脙陇ynti脙陇 ja Ven脙陇j脙陇 pyrkii aiheuttamaan erilaisia ilkeyksi脙陇 Euroopan unionin j脙陇senmaiden toimintakyvylle t脙陇ss脙陇 tilanteessa. Mutta se, ett脙陇 kiinalaiset alukset ovat ep脙陇iltyin脙陇 t脙陇ss脙陇 tilanteessa, tekee t脙陇st脙陇 erityisen haitallisen Euroopan tulevaisuuden kannalta, sill脙陇 meid脙陇n pit脙陇脙陇 my脙露s tunnistaa se, ett脙陇 Kiinan ja Ven脙陇j脙陇n v脙陇linen strateginen yhteys on sellainen, jota Eurooppa ei voi antaa jatkua t脙陇llaisena.

Eli aivan olennaista on nyt komission puolesta ja j脙陇senmaiden johdon puolesta ottaa tiivis yhteys Kiinan valtionjohtoon ja osoittaa, ett脙陇 heid脙陇n kauppalaivastonsa ei voi olla osallinen t脙陇llaisessa toiminnassa ja heid脙陇n pit脙陇脙陇 t脙陇ysim脙陇脙陇r脙陇isesti osallistua tutkintaan, jotta syyt脙露kset saadaan tutkittua kunnolla. N脙陇in ei voi en脙陇脙陇 jatkua.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bart Groothuis, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Commissioner, Russia is actively mapping drinking water supplies in Sweden and Finland with the intent to sabotage. And Russia is trying to map and sabotage cargo aeroplanes in Germany, bringing goods to Ukraine. And yes, Russia is addressing the threat of internet cables with how they perceive it, and they are sabotaging our internet cables in our seas. But then, why, colleagues? Why are we still talking about hybrid threats? Aren't these clear examples of clear escalation by Russia on European soil? There can be only one response. Time to hit back hard.

Now, I wish to remind the Commission that the Russian economy is in big trouble. Inflation is at 9脗聽%. Interest rates in Russia are at 20脗聽%, coming close to 30脗聽%. And their reserves will run out in a year. And the rouble is falling fast. Russia will soon be a country with tanks but no food. Crippling the Russian economy further should be our target. It was effective in bringing down communism. Let's do it again. Maximise the support to Ukraine with amounts that Russia cannot surmount. And do work together with Donald Trump for the final blow: lower oil and gas prices, flood the market so the war machinery ends not just in Russia, but also in Tehran.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Merja Kyll脙露nen, on behalf of The Left Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, the recent cable damage in the Baltic Sea is a concrete reminder of how vulnerable our critical infrastructure really is. The EU must be a united and active actor in order to prevent hybrid influence in the Baltic Sea.

The Baltic Sea littoral states, especially Finland, are extremely dependent on functioning connections to the sea. The majority of imports to Finland are brought through the Baltic Sea, and Finland is practically completely dependent on maritime traffic in the Baltic Sea and the infrastructure located there.

Eight of the European Union Member States are geographically connected in the Baltic Sea. This is therefore a significant issue for the EU, and it should clearly be considered one of the security policy priorities. Security changes in the Baltic Sea will cause extensive changes in all EU Member States, so securing it is in the interest of the entire EU.

The new Commission should actively implement this, as the price we pay if the security situation in the Baltic Sea is jeopardised is too high.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alexander Sell, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Unsere Infrastruktur ist in Gefahr, aber woher kommt diese Gefahr? Die gr脙露脙聼te Gefahr f脙录r unsere Infrastruktur ist unsere eigene Regierung. Gestern hat Angela Merkel in Berlin ihre Memoiren vorgestellt. 16脗聽Jahre lang hat sie Deutschland ruiniert. In ihrem Buch schreibt Merkel, der Bau von Nord Stream脗聽2 war eine Notwendigkeit. Der Ausstieg aus der Kernenergie habe den massiven Import von russischem Gas erfordert.

Das ist die bittere Wahrheit hinter Deutschlands Energiewende. Den W脙陇hlern hat man erz脙陇hlt, dass Wind und Sonne Energie liefern, und zwar gratis. Tats脙陇chlich hat man sich abh脙陇ngig gemacht von russischem Gas. Die katastrophalen Folgen dieser Politik sehen wir heute. CDU und Ampel haben unsere Industrie und unseren Wohlstand auf dem Altar einer schwachsinnigen Klimareligion geopfert. Im Februar hat Deutschland die Wahl. Wollen wir unser Land weiter ruinieren oder wollen wir unser Schicksal wieder selbst in die Hand nehmen? Wir brauchen zuverl脙陇ssige und kosteng脙录nstige Energie in Deutschland. Dann sind wir auch nicht anf脙陇llig f脙录r Angriffe auf Infrastruktur in der Ostsee.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lena D脙录pont (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, disinformation, deception, disruption, destabilisation, destruction 芒聙聯 the playbook by now is well known. It is clearly visible in broad daylight.

We see GPS jamming, we see cyber attacks, we see disinformation campaigns, attacks on logistical chains and service providers, critical infrastructure on land and sea being under attack, and there are worrying links to organised crime.脗聽We see the shadow fleet sailing alongside critical infrastructure and sensitive areas and, from time to time, even filled with highly explosive ammonium nitrate. The recent events are just an ample example and proof of that.

In the midst of the playbook, we 芒聙聯 the European Union, our allies and friends 芒聙聯because what our adversaries share is the refusal of our values, our democracy and our freedom. It can't be said too often: we are already in it, we are under attack and we need to live up to the challenge.

This morning, Ursula von der Leyen has set the tone for the next College. The stakes are high, and freedom will not come for free. We need to be bold and ambitious.

The instruments are on the table. We have an initial report on the preparedness. We will have the internal security strategy. We will have the white paper on defence, with the aim to overcome the fragmentation between the EU and the Member States, within the Member States, EU and NATO, between civilian and military actors based on a common threat assessment and risk perception, increasing the information-sharing so that we can build reliable, capable and resilient structures of response guided by the principles of deterrence by denial and deterrence by punishment.

We have all the tools at hand, and the answer is more Europe, not less.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Evin Incir (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, the latest developments in the Baltic Sea have highlighted the growing threat from Russian hybrid attacks, particularly targeting critical infrastructure such as undersea cables. Attacks from Russia and other anti-democratic countries against our societies are increasing, and their dreadful actions constitute a form of covert warfare.

Hybrid threats know no borders, which means that Sweden and our neighbouring countries need the strong support of the EU to handle the situation. A threat to one of our countries is a threat to all, unless you are Prime Minister Viktor Orb脙隆n in Hungary. It is shameful that we have a government in one of our Member States that acts as Putin's lapdog. It is a shame he can continue holding the EU presidency. At the same time, criminal gangs are being recruited by third countries to carry out terrorist attacks as part of hybrid warfare. We must take strong actions and utilise all of the EU's resources to protect our collective security.

脗聽脗聽Dick Erixon (ECR). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! 脙聞nnu en g脙楼ng pekar allt p脙楼 att ett kinesiskt fartyg har utf脙露rt sabotage mot viktig infrastruktur i 脙聳stersj脙露n. Ett fartyg h脙楼lls kvar i Kattegatt. Sveriges statsminister har i v脙陇nliga ordalag beg脙陇rt att fartyget beger sig till svensk hamn f脙露r att unders脙露kas. Det duger inte. Fartyget borde tvingas till svensk hamn, d脙陇r f脙露rh脙露r kan genomf脙露ras och bevis s脙陇kras.

脗聽脗聽Marie-Agnes Strack-Zimmermann (Renew). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, meine Damen und Herren! Wir sehen ja seit dem russischen Angriff auf die Ukraine, was passiert: Schlachtfelder wie im Ersten Weltkrieg. Gleichzeitig: Hightech von Drohnen, Cyber, Desinformation脗聽芒聙聯 脙录brigens, das ist besonders schlimm, haben Sie auch gerade geh脙露rt hier auf der rechten Seite, was da f脙录r ein Unsinn erz脙陇hlt wird脗聽芒聙聯 und hybride Angriffe. Meine Damen und Herren, das ist 脙录berhaupt nichts Neues. Aber wir sehen jetzt, wie eskaliert wird, wie ganz konkrete Angriffe passieren. Und deswegen ist es von hoher Relevanz, dass Europa sich bereith脙陇lt脗聽芒聙聯 mit allen Mitteln. Es ist 脙录brigens gut, dass in Rostock die Commander Task Force Baltic zusammengekommen ist, dass dort die acht Anrainerstaaten sich eben zusammengetan haben, 脙录brigens auch in Kombination mit der NATO.

Meine Damen und Herren, wir m脙录ssen eine Antwort finden. Und wenn mir hier irgendeiner sagt, dass wir rote Linien 脙录berschreiten, der wei脙聼 genau: Andersherum ist das der Fall. Und es wird Zeit, dass wir Antworten auf diese roten Linien haben und europ脙陇isch antworten, die n脙露tigen Mittel und das n脙露tige Equipment in die Hand nehmen und die europ脙陇ische Flagge ganz hochziehen in der Ostsee.

(Die Rednerin ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹 zu antworten.)

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, Ven脙陇j脙陇n hybridivaikuttamiselle pit脙陇脙陇 olla nollatoleranssi ja sit脙陇 pit脙陇脙陇 torjua kaikilla mahdollisilla tasoilla. T脙陇m脙陇 koskee uhkia niin It脙陇meren alueella kuin EU:n it脙陇isell脙陇 maarajallakin.

Ei suinkaan Ven脙陇j脙陇n hybridihy脙露kk脙陇yksen seurauksena. T脙陇m脙陇 on syyt脙陇 huomioida. Kyse ei ole vain hullusta Putinista. Meill脙陇 on EU:n naapurina vahvasti aivopesty kansa.

脗聽脗聽Ivars Ijabs (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Commissioner, well, last week's attacks towards the C芒聙聭Lion1 cable between Finland and Germany, just like the earlier Balticconnector damages, these are the signals that we have to take these threats seriously.脗聽These are not separate incidents. These are the elements of continuous Russian hybrid warfare against our critical infrastructure.

Right now, I think we have to send a clear signal that we know what is going on and who is standing behind that. Two points about how to address the threat come to mind.

First of all, let's use our technological superiority. There is a lot of innovation going on in many European countries, in particular in my home country, Latvia, in undersea drones, sensors, monitoring. This has to be developed in the long term.

The second issue is that let's keep open the possibility of the closure of the Baltic Sea as an option on the table. We need here a coordinated EU response, because we are dealing with a long芒聙聭term necessity to deter the Russian aggressor.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hannah Neumann (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, two critical data cables destroyed in the Baltic for the second time, with the Chinese vessel nearby navigating incognito 芒聙聯 what we are witnessing here is a textbook example of grey芒聙聭zone tactics, attacks too small to trigger a full military response, yet too significant and frequent to ignore. And this is a tactic our friends in the South China Sea know all too well.

Now, China is testing our limits, and they would advise us to send a strong and united response. Let's not forget China turned the simple transit of a German ship through the Taiwan Strait into a major diplomatic incident. Imagine the outrage if a German vessel had destroyed Chinese infrastructure in the South China Sea.

So, dear colleagues, we need to better protect our critical infrastructure, energy, military internet in the Baltic. The NATO Task Force Baltic in Rostock is a first step: robust actions and clear diplomatic consequences need to follow. Far too long, certain political groups in this House have exaggerated perceived threats in the Mediterranean, downplaying the real ones in the Baltic.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Riho Terras (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, never before and suddenly twice a year. Is that an accident?脗聽I am referring to the destruction of the fibre optic data cables in the Baltic Sea. First, between Estonia and Finland, and between Germany and Finland most recently.

This is exactly how the authoritarian regimes wage a covert war against our societies. By disrupting communication lines and attacking our infrastructure, they spread distrust and fear among our people.

We must not lay back and watch countries such as Russia and China operate in our backyard. Our task is to ensure that our critical infrastructure will not be ruined. We need to pay more attention to the hybrid threats originating from our enemies. The ultimate goal is to dismantle our way of life. We cannot let them do that.

脗聽脗聽Anna-Maja Henriksson (Renew). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! 脙聳stersj脙露n 脙陇r ett k脙陇nsligt innanhav p脙楼 m脙楼nga s脙陇tt, ocks脙楼 ur s脙陇kerhetspolitisk synvinkel. P脙楼 havsbotten finns undervattenskablar som utg脙露r ytterst s脙楼rbar infrastruktur.脗聽Det 脙陇r inte okej att sabotera dem. Hybridattacker 脙陇r oacceptabla.

De senaste h脙陇ndelserna m脙楼ste tas p脙楼 allvar. Friheten att navigera p脙楼 havet inneb脙陇r inte en r脙陇tt att f脙露rst脙露ra kritisk infrastruktur p脙楼 havsbotten. Om h脙陇r finns en lucka i havsr脙陇tten s脙楼 m脙楼ste den t脙陇ppas till. Det m脙楼ste finnas l脙露sningar f脙露r att ingripa ocks脙楼 p脙楼 internationellt vatten eller inom den s脙楼 kallade ekonomiska zonen f脙露r att kunna stoppa sk脙陇ligen misst脙陇nkta fartyg.

Vad kan EU g脙露ra? Jo, vi m脙楼ste agera best脙陇mt f脙露r att skydda v脙楼r kritiska infrastruktur i 脙聳stersj脙露n och vi m脙楼ste agera tillsammans. Kommissionen har h脙陇r en j脙陇tteviktig uppgift, och i president Niinist脙露s rapport finns det m脙楼nga goda f脙露rslag. L脙楼t oss ta vara p脙楼 dem.

(Talaren avb脙露jde en fr脙楼ga ("bl脙楼tt kort") fr脙楼n Bogdan Rzo脜聞ca)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽M脛聛rti脜聠脜隆 Sta脛路is (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, so Putin's goals go beyond destroying Ukraine. He seeks to break the unity of the West and destroy our values.

Russia's first weapon is fear. Through arson, infrastructure attacks, cyberattacks, assassination plots and disinformation, Russia spread fear in our societies. A frightened person is easy to intimidate.

The second weapon is greed. They use politicians who are willing to accept any peace on Putin's terms just to resume their personal business with Russia. A greedy person is easy to buy.

We must respond to these tactics with strength, tougher sanctions, defend critical infrastructure and combat disinformation on every level. Supporting Ukraine is central to defeating this threat. But let's not keep fooling ourselves by vague and obscure terms such as hybrid threats, just so we have an excuse not to act. We must call Russia's actions by their true name: state芒聙聭sponsored terrorism.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bart脜聜omiej Sienkiewicz (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo! Nie ma znaczenia, czy kable 脜聜脛聟czno脜聸ci internetowej i energetycznej czy gazoci脛聟gi zrywane s脛聟 przez kotwice statk脙鲁w Rosjan czy przez cywilne statki pod bander脛聟 chi脜聞sk脛聟. Wa脜录ne jest to, 脜录e nie mo脜录emy si脛聶 podda脛聡 tej presji. Dzia脜聜ania pod progiem pe脜聜noskalowej wojny jest takim samym aktem agresji jak konflikt zbrojny.

Dzi脜聸 premier Donald Tusk og脜聜osi脜聜 inicjatyw脛聶 navy policing na wodach mi脛聶dzynarodowych Morza Ba脜聜tyckiego, bo tylko si脜聜a jest odpowiedzi脛聟 na ataki hybrydowe. I t脛聟 si脜聜脛聟, w sensie niewojskowym, dysponuje Unia Europejska.

Wprowad脜潞my c脜聜a zaporowe na towary pochodz脛聟ce z Rosji. Przesta脜聞my kupowa脛聡 gaz, nawozy, nikiel i stal, w zamian za to miliardami finansuj脛聟c t脛聶 wojn脛聶. Prosi脜聜 nas o to na tej sali prezydent Ze脜聜enski. Przesta脜聞my finansowa脛聡 wojny Putina w odpowiedzi na t脛聶 wojn脛聶, kt脙鲁r脛聟 ju脜录 nam wypowiedzia脜聜.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marina Kaljurand (S&D). 芒聙聯 Proua president, proua volinik, kolleegid! Venemaa r脙录ndab j脙陇rjest j脙碌hkramalt ja j脙陇rjekindlamalt Euroopa ehk meie k脙碌igi julgeolekut. N脙陇iteid on k脙录llalt. S脙碌da Ukrainas, k脙录berr脙录nnakud, infos脙碌jad, illegaalsete migrantide kasutamine h脙录briidrelvana, terrorir脙录nnakud kriitilisele taristule L脙陇脙陇nemeres jne. Venemaa ja tema kuritegelike liitlaste tegevus ei tohi j脙陇脙陇da vastuseta. Vaatan uue komisjoni ja ka uue v脙陇lispoliitika k脙碌rge esindaja Kaja Kallase poole. H脙录briidr脙录nnakute ennetamine, t脙碌rjumine ja nendele vastamine peab saama Euroopa Liidu prioriteediks. Mitte ainult s脙碌nades, vaid ka tegudes. Ei tohi alahinnata Euroopa Liidu ja NATO tugevust. Vastupidi, seda tuleb kasutada. Ja l脙碌petuseks. Euroopa Liidu toetus Ukrainale peab mitte ainult j脙陇tkuma, vaid oluliselt suurenema. Et Ukraina v脙碌idaks, et Venemaa kaotaks ja vastutaks. Ainult nii saame tagada Euroopa julgeoleku ja tuleviku oma lastele. Ait脙陇h!

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Aurelijus Veryga (ECR). 芒聙聯 Gerbiama Pirmininke, kolegos, 脜隆iuo metu su nedraugi脜隆komis Ryt脜鲁 valstyb脛聴mis besiribojan脛聧ios 脜隆alys nar脛聴s ir pati Europos S脛聟junga yra intensyviai i脜隆bandoma ve脜戮ant prie sienos nelegalius migrantus, skleid脜戮iant dezinformacij脛聟, vykdant kibernetines atakas. Dabar yra i脜隆bandoma kita kritin脛聴 infrastrukt脜芦ra 芒聙聯脗聽pa脜戮eid脜戮iami kabeliai, siun脛聧iami savaime u脜戮sidegantys siuntiniai, trikdomas GPS signalas. Tuo bandoma patikrinti m脜芦s脜鲁 tarnyb脜鲁 pasirengim脛聟 ir darb脛聟, 脜隆ali脜鲁 reakcijas ir atsak脛聟, visuomeni脜鲁 reakcijas ir atsparum脛聟.

Ir tik nuo Europos S脛聟jungos reakcijos priklausys, kaip toli ar toliau bus einama. Tod脛聴l Europos S脛聟junga privalo labai rimtai reaguoti 脛炉 脜隆iuos prie脜隆i脜隆k脜鲁 valstybi脜鲁 bandymus destabilizuoti 脜隆ali脜鲁 kritin脛聶 infrastrukt脜芦r脛聟 ir visuomenes, ir kritin脛聴s infrastrukt脜芦ros apsauga turi tapti svarbiu Europos S脛聟jungos pasirengimo gintis objektu.

脗聽脗聽Karin Karlsbro (Renew). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Fr脙陇mmande makt hotar kritisk infrastruktur p脙楼 脙聳stersj脙露ns botten. Att skydda och freda v脙楼rt hav 脙陇r en angel脙陇genhet f脙露r hela EU i en tid n脙陇r Ryssland eskalerar kriget mot Ukraina. Vi beh脙露ver samarbeta mer f脙露r att skydda havets infrastruktur. Det kan vara att gemensamt patrullera havet och att tillsammans med f脙露rsvarsindustrin utveckla teknik och innovativa l脙露sningar f脙露r att kunna 脙露vervaka i realtid.

EU m脙楼ste samarbeta med och inom Nato f脙露r att ocks脙楼 skydda havet b脙陇ttre. Vi beh脙露ver anv脙陇nda oss av juridiken f脙露r att agera, inte f脙露r att l脙楼ta den脗聽begr脙陇nsa insatserna. Skyldiga m脙楼ste d脙露mas och straffas. Det 脙陇r helt enkelt dags att vi tar tillbaka kontrollen 脙露ver 脙聳stersj脙露n.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jan Farsk脙陆 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, we must protect European citizens. Our rivals, and especially Russia, are increasing the pressure on us, concretely in the Baltic region. And I am sorry to say, but words are not enough.

The GPS signal jamming is one of many examples. The Russians are targeting civilian planes in the region. Every week, tens of thousands of passengers, European citizens, are threatened by our enemies.脗聽If someone says that Putin is not targeting Europe, then what is this? Let's be clear: our society is a legitimate target of European hybrid activities.

Words of condemnation are no longer enough. We need an immediate, unified and decisive response. We must strengthen our defence capabilities, modernise its measures and, above all, protect our citizens from this aggression. History will judge us by our actions, not by our declarations.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Virginijus Sinkevi脛聧ius (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, the recent incidents involving undersea communication cables in the Baltic Sea have raised alarm bells across Europe.脗聽The possibility of sabotage is now at the centre of discussions, which reflects the tense geopolitical climate that we are living in. The fact that two fibre optic cables were affected simultaneously has only fuelled suspicions.

But one thing is clear: the situation underscores the vulnerability of undersea infrastructure. The civilian vessels might be used for strategic disruptions without direct military engagement.

So, that is why it is becoming urgent to better address these challenges at EU level and we need a fully coordinated approach also with NATO. This should include enhancing intelligence sharing among Member States, investing in robust cybersecurity measures, increased surveillance, and conduct joint exercises to improve readiness.

The EU must act swiftly and decisively to protect its critical infrastructure, rather than waiting for another accident to occur.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

Catch-the-eye procedure

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Krzysztof Hetman (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Sieci kabli do transmisji danych, sieci energetyczne czy ruroci脛聟gi to krytyczna infrastruktura podmorska, od kt脙鲁rej zale脜录y stabilno脜聸脛聡 naszych gospodarek i nasze bezpiecze脜聞stwo. Niestety w obecnej sytuacji geopolitycznej coraz cz脛聶脜聸ciej dochodzi do akt脙鲁w sabota脜录u lub akt脙鲁w hybrydowych w脜聜a脜聸nie na tego rodzaju infrastruktur脛聶, kt脙鲁re nie omin脛聶脜聜y tak脜录e Morza Ba脜聜tyckiego. Ich celem jest destabilizacja funkcjonowania pa脜聞stw i prowokacja.

W obliczu takich zagro脜录e脜聞 kluczowa jest 脜聸cis脜聜a wsp脙鲁脜聜praca na linii Unia Europejska 芒聙聯 NATO. Ciesz脛聶 si脛聶, 脜录e w 2023 roku powsta脜聜a Grupa Zadaniowa NATO-UE ds. odporno脜聸ci infrastruktury krytycznej, takiej jak infrastruktura transportowa, cyfrowa i energetyczna. Wa脜录ne w tym kontek脜聸cie jest mapowanie s脜聜abych punkt脙鲁w i ocena zagro脜录enia. Ale co z inn脛聟 infrastruktur脛聟 krytyczn脛聟? Portami, wybudowanymi i ju脜录 budowanymi farmami wiatrowymi? Musimy z wyprzedzeniem dzia脜聜a脛聡 na tego typu dzia脜聜ania hybrydowe skierowane przeciwko destabilizacji Europy i 脜聸wiata zachodniego. I dzisiaj musimy sobie jasno i wyra脜潞nie powiedzie脛聡: dzi脜聸 Morze Ba脜聜tyckie, a za chwil脛聶 krytyczna infrastruktura na terenie Polski i ca脜聜ej Unii Europejskiej.

脗聽脗聽Bogdan Rzo脜聞ca (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! P脜聜aczemy tu dzisiaj nad tym, 脜录e Rosja atakuje, 脜录e Chiny atakuj脛聟. Przez dwie dekady Parlament Europejski, Unia Europejska wsp脙鲁脜聜pracowa脜聜a z Putinem, dostarczaj脛聟c mu pieni脛聶dzy, kupuj脛聟c tanie w脛聶glowodory. Teraz, w ostatnich latach, Unia Europejska wsp脙鲁脜聜pracuje z Chinami. Kraje Unii Europejskiej dostarczaj脛聟 pieni脛聶dzy Chinom, bo 97% paneli fotowoltaicznych kupowanych jest w Chinach. I te kraje maj脛聟 pieni脛聟dze i 脜聸miej脛聟 si脛聶 z Unii Europejskiej i kpi脛聟 z niej, bo wiedz脛聟, 脜录e jest coraz s脜聜absza.

Ta dyskusja jest bardzo wa脜录na, absolutnie bardzo wa脜录na tak脜录e dla Polski, dlatego, 脜录e mi脛聶dzy innymi przez Morze Ba脜聜tyckie biegnie ruroci脛聟g Baltic Pipe. On jest bardzo wa脜录ny z punktu widzenia bezpiecze脜聞stwa energetycznego Polski. Wi脛聶c jak na posiedzeniu Komisji Bud脜录etowej rozmawiamy o bezpiecze脜聞stwie i zg脜聜aszamy wnioski, 脜录eby wzmocni脛聡 bud脜录et Unii Europejskiej na bezpiecze脜聞stwo, wtedy lewica, wtedy socjali脜聸ci, wtedy libera脜聜owie m脙鲁wi脛聟 芒聙聻nie芒聙聺, nie dajemy wi脛聶cej pieni脛聶dzy, nie potrzeba wi脛聶cej pieni脛聶dzy na bezpiecze脜聞stwo. Wi脛聶c nie p脜聜aczmy, tylko r脙鲁bmy. Stw脙鲁rzmy sytuacje bezpieczne dla ka脜录dego kraju Unii Europejskiej i dla Morza Ba脜聜tyckiego.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Brigitte van den Berg (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, I'm sure I'm not the only one that has once written their password on a Post-it or clicked on a phishing email, because that's human and it's not nice to always live on your guard. But this is how we have treated our critical infrastructure up till now and it's time to lose our naivety.

It's time to not only protect our cables, but also our elections and our media. We need to protect our citizens and it's not enough if every Member State just takes care of their own backyard.

We have to work towards an integrated approach and we have to really put together our intelligence services, our police and our militaries.脗聽I think we have to put it ambitiously and make a NATO芒聙聭like command structure to counter hybrid threats.

I urge the Commission and the Member States to take action before it's too late.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Menschen Europas! Ich pers脙露nlich hatte ja schon immer Schwierigkeiten, an Zuf脙陇lle zu glauben. Je gr脙露脙聼er der Zufall, desto schwerer f脙陇llt mir das. Zum Beispiel war es ein gro脙聼er Zufall, als ich an dem Tag der Ernennung unseres Delegationsleiters f脙录r China mit ihm in der Members' Bar sa脙聼, und als wir hinauskamen, standen da zuf脙陇llig drei chinesische Lobbyisten, von denen einer genauso wie der Delegationsleiter auch T脙录rkisch spricht.

Genauso ist es f脙录r mich v脙露llig unm脙露glich zu glauben, dass dieses chinesische Schiff mit seinem russischen Kapit脙陇n sich zuf脙陇llig genau in der N脙陇he dieser Datenkabel befand. Das war ein wiederholter Angriff auf unsere kritische Infrastruktur脗聽芒聙聯 nicht mehr und nicht weniger. Und f脙录r den Kollegen Bausemer und seine ach so schlauen Fragen: Es f脙陇llt mir auch schwer zu glauben, dass das mit Nord Stream ein Zufall war, dass da die Ukrainer in der N脙陇he waren. Aber es ist eben so, wie die Kollegin Strack芒聙聭Zimmermann gesagt hat: Entweder waren das die Russen, die diese Ukrainer bewusst eingesetzt haben, damit Sie jetzt hier schlaue Fragen stellen, oder die Regierung Selenskyj hat uns einen riesengro脙聼en Gefallen getan damit.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Grzegorz Braun (NI). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, hypocrisy at its top. You are discussing security of critical infrastructure in and on the Baltic Sea, and you are obviously turning this sitting into just another hour of hatred against Russia, China.

And you don't mention the case of Nord Stream 2.脗聽According to the rule, one does not discuss ropes in the house of a hanged person. So, you are not mentioning that more than one source attributes responsibility for this case to the United States of America and its agents in central Europe, probably Ukrainian.
