脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Moritz K脙露rner (Renew). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Die Rechte hier beschwert sich, dass wir diese Debatte 脙录berhaupt f脙录hren m脙录ssen. Wir m脙录ssen diese Debatte nur f脙录hren, weil es sie gibt und ihre Politik in den L脙陇ndern die Rechte von Homosexuellen 脙录berhaupt angreift. Wir k脙露nnten einfach diese Debatte hier lassen, das f脙陇nden wir alle gut, wenn sie einfach zur Vernunft kommen w脙录rden.

Es wird gesprochen 脙录ber sexuelle Propaganda in den Schulen. Das klingt so, als w脙录rde man einfach ein bisschen erz脙陇hlen, man kann schwul sein, und dann w脙录rden automatisch die Kinder alle schwul werden in der Klasse. Wenn in der Netflix芒聙聭Serie ein schwuler Charakter ist 芒聙聯 ups, auf einmal werden sofort alle schwul. So funktioniert es wirklich nicht.

Das einzige, was passiert, wenn in einer Schulklasse unterrichtet wird, dass auch zum Beispiel zwei M脙陇nner oder zwei Frauen sich lieben k脙露nnen, das einzige, was passiert, ist, dass m脙露glicherweise die Sch脙录ler etwas toleranter werden. Aber f脙录r die Sch脙录lerinnen und Sch脙录ler, die tats脙陇chlich queer sind, die mit sich selber struggeln, die 脙录berlegen: Bin ich da anders? Werden mich meine Eltern so akzeptieren? F脙录r diese Sch脙录ler ist es wichtig, denn die haben immer noch ein vielfach h脙露heres Selbstmordrisiko in Europa. Solange wir das nicht ver脙陇ndern, bitte ich Sie, nie wieder von der Sicherheit von Kindern zu sprechen. Sie machen das Gegenteil.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rasmus Nordqvist (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, I don't know if I'm more sad or angry about this debate today. Yes, perhaps indeed it's 'sad'. Sad to see that we are actually discussing rights, fundamental rights. I mean, I'm soon turning 50 芒聙聯 I know you can't see it, but I am! 芒聙聯 and in my lifetime I didn't think I would live to see rights be turned back in Europe as we see right now. Fundamental rights for people to live as who they are. That's what we are seeing right now in Bulgaria, in Hungary, in Italy, and that's what we have to be aware of here in the Union.

We must insist on full implementation of European judgement from the Court all over Europe. We have to really monitor and be after the Member States who are not following these rules. We also have to look into what this is about. You talk about families, you talk about kids, but as the former speaker said, if there is no education in schools about what people are, how people live, that is what actually turns against these kids. That's why we see the well-being of LGBTQI kids so low. That is why we see higher rates of suicide among LGBTQI people. So this is about protecting people living in Europe, millions of people, and not just discriminating as we see from over there.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Isabel Serra S脙隆nchez (The Left). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ores de ultraderecha, el derecho sexual integral en la educaci脙鲁n sexual integral es un derecho de todos los ni脙卤os y todas las ni脙卤as, independientemente de lo que digan sus padres, independientemente de las familias de las que formen parte.

Porque la educaci脙鲁n sexual integral es una garant脙颅a para educar frente al acoso, para respetar, para educar en el respeto por la diversidad de la orientaci脙鲁n sexual, de la identidad de g脙漏nero; tambi脙漏n para respetar las diversidades de los cuerpos, para identificar violencias, para no reproducir las violencias, para construir relaciones basadas en el buen trato y en el consentimiento y para que, como sociedad, sepamos que podemos transformar las relaciones y ser mejores.

Se脙卤ora comisaria, usted dice que el odio contra las personas LGBTQI est脙隆 creciendo y eso es verdad. No es nuevo que la ultraderecha identifique a las personas LGBTQI como su principal enemigo, pero lo que s脙颅 es nuevo es que hoy, aqu脙颅, se ha firmado un nuevo Gobierno en Europa que tiene en el centro las ideas de la ultraderecha y que tiene a la ultraderecha 芒聙聰que odia a las personas LGBTQI芒聙聰 en el mando de ese Gobierno.

脗驴C脙鲁mo no se va a te脙卤ir Europa de odio si tiene a los odiadores profesionales en su Gobierno? La ultraderecha habla mucho de inseguridad, pero es precisamente脗聽ella la que genera inseguridad contra las personas LGBTQI. Por eso, si no lo hacen aqu脙颅, si no lo hace la Comisi脙鲁n Europea, si no lo hace el Gobierno de Europa...

(la presidenta retira la palabra a la oradora)

脗聽脗聽Grzegorz Braun (NI). 芒聙聯 Dlaczego kwestionujecie t脛聶 prawd脛聶, 脜录e jest to z jednej strony narz脛聶dzie oddzia脜聜ywania psychologicznego, 脜录eby cz脜聜owieka obrabowa脛聡 i pozbawi脛聡 wszystkich naturalnych jego potrzeb? Jak tu cz脜聜owieka uderzymy, to potem ju脜录 wszystko mo脜录emy z nim zrobi脛聡. I jest to narz脛聶dzie oddzia脜聜ywania politycznego. Sodomici s脛聟, jak s脛聟dz脛聶, tym zaginionym proletariatem rewolucji 脜聸wiatowej, kt脙鲁rego tak bardzo potrzebujecie, 脜录eby prowadzi脛聡 wasze projekty. Trzymajcie si脛聶, zbocze脜聞cy, z daleka... (Przewodnicz脛聟ca przerwa脜聜a m脙鲁wcy) ... trzymajcie si脛聶 z daleka od naszych dzieci. Prawa rodzicielskie to podstawa cywilizacji.

脗聽脗聽Grzegorz Braun (NI), odpowied脜潞 na pytanie zadane przez podniesienie niebieskiej kartki. 芒聙聯 Szanowna Pani, Czcigodni Zebrani, takie by脜聜o stanowisko nauki w naszej cywilizacji przez stulecia i tysi脛聟clecia. I dopiero ca脜聜kiem niedawno, dopiero ca脜聜kiem niedawno rozmaite zboczenia moc脛聟 nie ustalenia naukowego, ale decyzji politycznej i moc脛聟 demokratycznych, przemocowych wi脛聶kszo脜聸ci zosta脜聜y skre脜聸lone z list zaburze脜聞, schorze脜聞, kt脙鲁re wcze脜聸niej s脜聜u脜录y脜聜y dobrze przez pokolenia medykom i okre脜聸la脜聜y po prostu stan swoisty, w脜聜a脜聸ciwy dla naszej cywilizacji, kt脙鲁ra, jak ju脜录 powiedzia脜聜em, stoi twardo...

(Przewodnicz脛聟ca odebra脜聜a m脙鲁wcy g脜聜os)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adri脙隆n V脙隆zquez L脙隆zara (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, lo acabamos de ver. Vemos en el extremo derecho de la c脙隆mara a gente que odia a todo aquel que no vive como ellos o, por lo menos, que no vive como ellos p脙潞blicamente. Pero tambi脙漏n hemos visto y escuchado en el extremo izquierdo de la c脙隆mara a los que te cancelan si no dices o piensas como ellos quieren que digas y pienses.

Pero Europa es mucho m脙隆s que esto, Europa se percibe en el mundo como un basti脙鲁n de valores y de principios democr脙隆ticos, con instituciones s脙鲁lidas que promueven la participaci脙鲁n ciudadana y protegen las libertades individuales. Y esos valores europeos son el n脙潞cleo de los valores occidentales.

Pero, por desgracia, esos valores han sufrido algunos retrocesos en los 脙潞ltimos a脙卤os y este debate da buena cuenta de ello, con el retroceso que han sufrido las personas LGTBI en Hungr脙颅a, donde lo que han hecho es aprobar una ley dirigida a estigmatizar a aquellos que no piensan como la mayor脙颅a y que equiparan sus derechos a la pedofilia, incluso prohibiendo la adopci脙鲁n a parejas del mismo sexo.

El se脙卤or Orb脙隆n, que dice estar tan preocupado por defender esos valores occidentales, debe entender que no hay valor m脙隆s occidental que la libertad: la libertad para votar, para pensar, para expresarse, pero tambi脙漏n la libertad para amar.

Quienes creemos en la libertad y en la familia 芒聙聰en todas las familias, no solo en un 脙潞nico modelo芒聙聰 no podemos permitir que esa actitud intolerante recorra nuestro continente.

Y Orb脙隆n compra su influencia financiando partidos en otros pa脙颅ses, como el caso de VOX en Espa脙卤a, con el objetivo de alcanzar su sue脙卤o de una Europa m脙隆s estrecha, una Europa menos libre, menos diversa y, en consecuencia, menos unida.

Por ello, es el momento de redoblar nuestro compromiso y hacer una estrategia europea seria y sin sectarismos, dirigida a blindar los derechos de aquellos que se sienten discriminados por vivir su amor en libertad.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

VORSITZ: SABINE VERHEYEN
Vizepr脙陇sidentin

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alessandro Zan (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, lo abbiamo sentito dai deputati dell'estrema destra che in Europa la comunit脙聽 LGBTQ+ 脙篓 sotto attacco.

Ho sentito dal partito di Giorgia Meloni delle cose agghiaccianti e dico che la Commissione europea non pu脙虏 essere in alcun modo complice di Paesi come la Bulgaria e l'Ungheria che hanno approvato leggi anti-LGBTQ+; non pu脙虏 essere complice del governo Meloni che sta perseguitando le famiglie arcobaleno e i loro figli e ha decretato unilateralmente che alcuni Paesi rappresentati in quest'Aula hanno delle leggi criminali.

Allora lo dico alla Commissione europea, che 脙篓 guardiana dei trattati, che non pu脙虏 permettere che un cittadino bulgaro, ungherese e italiano abbia meno diritti di un cittadino spagnolo, maltese o irlandese. Le discriminazioni si combattono con la cultura ma si combattono anche con le leggi.

Ecco perch脙漏 serve una legge europea contro i crimini d'odio, serve un regolamento europeo sulla genitorialit脙聽, serve rimettere i diritti al centro dell'agenda dell'Unione europea, perch脙漏 l'Europa 脙篓 nata su questi valori?

(L'oratore accetta di rispondere a una domanda "cartellino blu")

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Paolo Inselvini (ECR), domanda "cartellino blu". 芒聙聯 Collega, premettendo il fatto che La considero una persona intelligente e che crede nella giustizia e nella libert脙聽, Le chiedo per脙虏 in quale modo il governo Meloni starebbe discriminando la comunit脙聽 LGBT?

E soprattutto Le chiedo: Lei 脙篓 d'accordo con la teoria secondo la quale si possono comprare e vendere dei bambini? 脙聢 d'accordo nello sfruttare il corpo delle donne? 脙聢 d'accordo con il fatto che noi riduciamo delle persone a un oggetto di commercio?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alessandro Zan (S&D), risposta a una domanda "cartellino blu". 芒聙聯 Il governo Meloni sta perseguitando le famiglie arcobaleno. 脙聢 partita una circolare del ministro Piantedosi che vieta ai sindaci di trascrivere i figli nati dalle coppie omogenitoriali. E questa 脙篓 una violenza che discrimina i bambini nati dalle famiglie arcobaleno rispetto agli altri bambini.

Sulla GPA, che volgarmente voi chiamate "utero in affitto", c'脙篓 una GPA solidale, etica e sociale, dove la donna, nella sua piena autodeterminazione, decide fino alla fine se farlo o non farlo. Ecco, su questo io sono d'accordo.

Non sono d'accordo su tutto il resto ma quando c'脙篓 la piena autodeterminazione della donna, allora, da questo punto di vista va bene e voi chiamandolo "utero in affitto" e continuando a parlare di compravendita dei bambini, non fate altro che avere un atteggiamento persecutorio verso la comunit脙聽 LGBT.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sophie Wilm脙篓s (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, aujourd'hui, seuls douze 脙聣tats membres, dont mon pays, la Belgique, ont mis en place des plans solides pour prot脙漏ger les droits des personnes LGBTQI+. Si beaucoup d'脙聣tats membres continuent 脙聽 avancer dans la bonne direction, tous ne font pas ce choix. Tous les d脙漏put脙漏s europ脙漏ens ne font pas ce choix non plus, manifestement; c'est dommage, mais c'est comme 脙搂a.

Rappelons que la loi anti-LGBT de脗聽2021 en Hongrie, qui assimile, en fait, l'homosexualit脙漏 脙聽 de la pornographie, a heureusement, sous l'impulsion de la Belgique, encore, fait r脙漏agir le Conseil et la Commission. Cette loi inacceptable impose, par exemple, la mise au banc de livres comme Heartstopper, romans et s脙漏ries tr脙篓s populaires qui parlent d'amour entre deux ados, tout simplement.

L'arr脙陋t de la Cour de justice de l'Union europ脙漏enne attendu pour脗聽2025 constituera, je l'esp脙篓re, un pr脙漏c脙漏dent irr脙漏futable et n脙漏cessaire face 脙聽 d'autres cas comme, r脙漏cemment encore, celui de la Bulgarie, et ainsi permettre que des proc脙漏dures d'infraction syst脙漏matiques puissent 脙陋tre ouvertes par la Commission dans pareil cas. L'Europe est et doit rester une terre de libert脙漏, de r脙漏alisation de soi. Je me r脙漏alise si je veux et comme je veux.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tineke Strik (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear Commissioner, last week the EU wrote history. The Commission, Parliament and 16 Member States stood united in the courtroom to fight against Hungary's anti芒聙聭LGBTIQ law.

The silent majority finally spoke out loud and clear that the attack on the human rights of the LGBTIQ community undermines the core values of our Union. That was what the community in Hungary, after years of hate campaigns, persecution and humiliation by Orb脙隆n, was desperately waiting for.

This court case shows us exactly how the EU's fundamental values should be defended in a strong, united and interconnected way. Because an attack on fundamental rights is an attack on the rule of law and a threat to Europe's DNA.

I urge the Commission to use the EU values as a basis for all actions against violations of fundamental rights, democracy and the rule of law, and I urge Member States to join continuously cases of the Commission against Hungary, Bulgaria or Italy. In that courtroom last week, the EU showed real fire and determination. Now, let's keep that fire burning.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andr脙隆s L脙隆szl脙鲁 (PfE), k脙漏kk脙隆rty脙隆s k脙漏rd脙漏s. 芒聙聯 Magyarul fogok besz脙漏lni. Annyi hazugs脙隆g hangzott m脙隆r el Magyarorsz脙隆g kapcs脙隆n, nincs alkalmam mindenkinek v脙隆laszolni. Biztos vagyok benne, hogy van, aki csak hazudik, van, aki csak...

Annyi hazugs脙隆g hangzott m脙隆r el Magyarorsz脙隆ggal kapcsolatban, vagy eg脙漏sz egyszer脜卤en t脙漏ved脙漏s, amit nem lehet sz脙鲁 n脙漏lk脙录l hagyni, viszont nincs lehet脜聭s脙漏gem, hogy mindenkinek v脙隆laszoljak. Szeretn脙漏m krist脙隆lytiszt脙隆n elmondani, hogy itt nemzeti szuverenit脙隆sr脙鲁l van sz脙鲁. 脙聳n脙露knek nem Magyarorsz脙隆ggal kell foglalkozniuk, mert ez az Alapjogi Charta szerint is a 14. cikkely szerint nemzeti szuverenit脙隆s, 脙漏s a sz脙录l脜聭k joga, hogy megv脙隆lassz脙隆k, hogy milyen 脙漏rt脙漏krend szerint akarj脙隆k tan脙颅ttatni gyermekeiket. A 14. cikk az Alapjogi Chart脙隆ban. Magyarorsz脙隆gon semmilyen b脙录ntet脙漏s nincsen a melegek ellen. Az 脙潞j t脙露rv脙漏ny, a 2021-es t脙露rv脙漏ny semmi m脙隆st nem tesz, mint a kiskor脙潞akat...

(az eln脙露k megszak脙颅tja az el脜聭ad脙鲁t)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Sorry, you have to ask a question. The half minute is over and there is not really a question.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andr脙隆s L脙隆szl脙鲁 (PfE). 芒聙聯 OK, I have a question, will you respect the Fundamental Rights Charter Article 14?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tineke Strik (Verts/ALE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 I am really happy to hear that you are very well aware of the Charter of Fundamental Rights of the EU, because this is what all citizens of the EU should be protected by and can invoke.

And that is very clear in the Treaty of the EU: that our Union is based on those fundamental rights, and one of the fundamental rights is the right to be who you are, to celebrate and express your identity. And this is exactly what the EU is for, to protect every individual citizen against awkward and aggressive policies of EU Member States that try to demolish them and use hate speech against those people.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Liesbet Sommen (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, de verwerpelijke en kwetsende uitspraken van extreemrechts hier in dit plenum onderstrepen het jammer genoeg nog maar eens: wij hebben moeten zien dat de rechten van personen uit de lgbtqi芒聙聭gemeenschap de laatste jaren zeker worden teruggeschroefd, ook hier in Europa. Het lijkt wel alsof de klok wordt teruggedraaid. Onder het mom van "neutraal onderwijs"脗聽en de "bescherming van traditionele waarden" worden boeken verboden, regenboogvlaggen verwijderd en mensen monddood gemaakt.

Het is totaal onacceptabel. Dergelijke wetgeving staat haaks op waar de Europese Unie op gebaseerd is. Het zijn onze waarden. Mensen uit de lgbtqi芒聙聭gemeenschap ervaren nu meer discriminatie dan vijf jaar geleden en hetzelfde geldt voor betrokken middenveldorganisaties in hun contacten met bepaalde EU芒聙聭lidstaten.

Wij mogen nooit opgeven. We moeten ons blijven uitspreken tegen homofobie en tegen haatzaaiende retoriek. In de Europese Unie gaan wij uit van tolerantie, respect en verdraagzaamheid, ongeacht iemands seksuele identiteit of genderidentiteit.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Krzysztof 脜職miszek (S&D). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Jeszcze kilka lat temu patrzy脜聜em na map脛聶 Polski ze z脜聜amanym sercem. Przez polityk脛聶 nienawi脜聸ci prowadzon脛聟 w imi脛聶 wypaczonej i chorej prawicowej ideologii 1/3 terytorium mojej ojczyzny zamieni脜聜a si脛聶 w tzw. strefy wolne od LGBT. Dzisiaj z dum脛聟 tutaj, w sercu Europy, mog脛聶 powiedzie脛聡 o tym, 脜录e w脜聜a脜聸nie wczoraj demokratyczny polski rz脛聟d przyj脛聟脜聜 projekt ustawy zakazuj脛聟cej homofobicznej mowy nienawi脜聸ci. B脛聶dzie ona chroni脛聡 wszystkich tych, kt脙鲁rzy do脜聸wiadczaj脛聟 pogardy i hejtu z powodu swojej orientacji seksualnej.

Ciesz脛聶 si脛聶, 脜录e jako wiceminister sprawiedliwo脜聸ci rozpocz脛聟脜聜em t脛聶 prac脛聶. Dzisiaj ze stu stref wolnych od LGBT w Polsce zosta脜聜a ju脜录 tylko jedna, kt脙鲁ra dzi脛聶ki wysi脜聜kom spo脜聜ecze脜聞stwa obywatelskiego za chwil脛聶 i tak upadnie. Z tego miejsca chcia脜聜em bardzo mocno podzi脛聶kowa脛聡 pani komisarz Helenie Dalli za jej determinacj脛聶, zaanga脜录owanie w t脛聶 kwesti脛聶 i bezwzgl脛聶dn脛聟 obron脛聶 warto脜聸ci Unii Europejskiej w moim kraju.

Demokracja to nie t脛聶pe rz脛聟dy wi脛聶kszo脜聸ci. Demokracja to obrona praw mniejszo脜聸ci. A Unia Europejska to nie bankomat. To przede wszystkim zestaw konkretnych warto脜聸ci i bezpiecze脜聞stwo dla wszystkich. Wszystkim tym, kt脙鲁rzy walcz脛聟 dzisiaj o prawa LGBT we W脜聜oszech, na W脛聶grzech, w Bu脜聜garii, w S脜聜owacji i wci脛聟脜录 w Polsce, chc脛聶 powiedzie脛聡 tylko jedno: nie poddawajcie si脛聶. Prawa os脙鲁b LGBT to prawa cz脜聜owieka.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Veronika Cifrov脙隆 Ostriho脜聢ov脙隆 (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, I cannot believe what I'm hearing in this Chamber, right here in the year 2024. I thought the European Union was supposed to be a union of equality, no? Am I wrong? I'm a new MEP 芒聙聯 am I in the wrong Chamber? What is happening?

So let me be clear: targeting or silencing minorities does not demonstrate strength, it highlights division and misunderstanding. The recent laws in Bulgaria and Hungary don't help or protect anyone. All they do is spread fear, deepen division and undermine the values we all stand for in Europe.脗聽What makes this especially troubling is that these laws often target children.

What we need is clear: we need better schools, we need quality healthcare, reliable transport, competitive businesses and communities where people feel safe and included.

That's what builds strong societies. That's what builds strong Europe, not targeting those who already feel vulnerable. Our responsibility is to protect fairness, equality and democracy for everyone, because no one should live in fear just because of who they are. And that is our mission here in Parliament.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tilly Metz (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, chers amis LGBTQI+, les d脙漏bats d'aujourd'hui portent sur des personnes, ils portent sur des vies, ils portent sur la libert脙漏, ils portent sur notre avenir.

Dans diff脙漏rents pays de l'Union europ脙漏enne, des l脙漏gislations nationales ciblant les personnes LGBTQI portent atteinte 脙聽 nos valeurs communes: la dignit脙漏, l'脙漏galit脙漏, l'脙漏tat de droit et les droits humains pour tous. L'extr脙陋me droite se r脙漏jouit d'adopter des lois qui, litt脙漏ralement, co脙禄tent des vies en encourageant la discrimination, en alimentant la violence.

L'histoire n'avance pas toujours dans le bon sens: elle peut reculer si on ne r脙漏siste pas. Les forces progressistes en Europe ont un devoir: un devoir de s'exprimer, un devoir d'agir, un devoir de d脙漏fendre les droits de toutes les personnes en Europe, sans exception.

L'extr脙陋me droite se nourrit de la peur, de la diff脙漏rence. Nous sommes ici pour le dire et redire que l'amour et la diversit脙漏 seront toujours plus forts que la haine. Ainsi je vous exhorte 脙聽 choisir l'action, 脙聽 choisir la solidarit脙漏, 脙聽 choisir les valeurs qui rendent notre Union plus forte et plus belle.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Carolina Morace (The Left). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signora Commissaria, purtroppo dobbiamo prendere atto che oggi esistono due Europe: un'Europa in cui le coppie gay possono sposarsi, realizzare la loro famiglia e vivere in pace e armonia nella societ脙聽, in sostanza libere di amare e di essere amate; e un'Europa in cui prevalgono discriminazioni e disuguaglianze, che 脙篓 quella della Bulgaria, dell'Ungheria e dell'Italia, che attaccano la comunit脙聽 LGBTQI, la cui unica colpa 脙篓 quella di esistere.

Il governo Meloni ha emanato una circolare in cui dichiara illegittimo un atto di nascita con due mamme mentre il Parlamento italiano ha approvato una legge che manda in galera i pap脙聽 dei bambini nati attraverso la gestazione per altri, anche quando 脙篓 legale in altri Paesi, peraltro senza specificare che fine faranno questi bambini.

Ricordiamoci che l'articolo 2 del trattato sull'Unione europea parla chiaramente di un'Europa inclusiva, fondata sui diritti umani, sulla parit脙聽 e sul rispetto delle minoranze.

(L'oratrice accetta di rispondere a una domanda "cartellino blu")

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Paolo Inselvini (ECR), domanda "cartellino blu". 芒聙聯 Signora Morace, buonasera, forse me lo sono perso: come 脙篓 possibile nascere da due madri?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Carolina Morace (The Left), risposta a una domanda "cartellino blu". 芒聙聯 Semplice, perch脙漏 la mia attuale moglie ha una figlia di 13 anni normalissima, avuta con un'altra donna. Siamo nel 2024. So che per te 脙篓 molto difficile capirlo: ma quando ci si ama la filiazione 脙篓 normale.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Angel (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, in a union of equality, there is no room for discrimination. I ask the right side of this House, would you not be outraged if one of your relatives or one of your friends would be discriminated against because of the colour of their skin, or because of their religion, or because of their disability? Why are some of you not shocked when people continue to suffer, on a daily basis, discrimination and violence because they are lesbian, gay, bi, trans, non-binary or an intersex person?

In a union of equality there is no place for double standards. In Europe the anti-gender movement 芒聙聯 notably supported by Putin, Orb脙隆n and Meloni and others 芒聙聯 is getting increasingly vocal and is taking discriminatory measures against the LGBTI community. Europe must counter this axis of hate and actively oppose legislation that undermines equality, freedom and rule of law. Europe must denounce discriminatory laws and ensure compliance with EU law and the case law of the ECJ. The Commission must not hesitate to launch infringement procedures. We Socialists & Democrats will never sell our values for political gain, and we will never waver in our commitment to workers' rights to human rights, including LGBTQI+ rights.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Raquel Garc脙颅a Hermida-Van Der Walle (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Madam脗聽Commissioner, I heard a colleague saying 'we can't negate nature, we can't deny nature.' But you know what nature is? Nature is the pain in the eyes of the Bulgarian LGBTIQ+ activists who came to visit the European Parliament when they were telling us about being targeted by their own government.

And you know why governments are targeting them? It is to hide their own corruption, their own clientelism, their own nepotism. Either they can't solve their real problems or they are part of the problem.脗聽So to distract society, they limit the rights of innocent people, keeping the lid on their own mess.

We need to keep the pressure high. From the brand new Commission, I expect immediate action towards Bulgaria and any other country breaking down our fundamental freedoms. And no endless exchanges that take years. Close the money tap for the guilty governments and ensure that those who protect our Union values do have the means to keep doing so.

And to those brave activists, I will tell you: you will always find partners in the European Parliament. We will stand with you and we will fight for you.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Vicent Marz脙聽 Ib脙隆脙卤ez (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, 脗芦al percibir que un ni脙卤o se expresa de modo afeminado, el educador debe conducirlo como un im脙隆n hacia las realidades masculinas脗禄. Esto, y otras barbaridades m脙隆s, es parte de las terapias de conversi脙鲁n que hab脙颅a en las instrucciones que se les daba a maestros y que hemos destapado ahora mismo, en el siglo XXI, en Valencia: pr脙隆cticas que promet脙颅an curar lo que no es una enfermedad, que perpetuaban el odio y el sufrimiento y que se publicitan con total impunidad aqu脙颅, en Europa.

Tambi脙漏n en Bulgaria, en Hungr脙颅a o en Italia hemos visto que se persiguen y se silencian los derechos fundamentales de las personas LGTBI. Y aqu脙颅, en este Parlamento 芒聙聰lo estamos viendo en este debate芒聙聰, algunos tambi脙漏n han dado apoyo a comisarios como el de Sanidad, que son antiabortistas y anti-LGTBI.

Y eso, se脙卤or脙颅as, duele; y no nos duele solo a nosotros y a la gente de la comunidad LGTBI, nos duele a todas y todos. Por eso, desde aqu脙颅, la mayor脙颅a de este Parlamento estamos lanzando un mensaje al conjunto de las personas LGTBI y les decimos que no est脙隆n solas, que vamos a dejarnos la piel en defender sus derechos y sus libertades. Y lo vamos a hacer a pesar de toda esta gente que lo 脙潞nico que quiere es que vayamos a脙卤os atr脙隆s en las libertades que hemos adquirido.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽M脙漏lissa Camara (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, tout d'abord, je voudrais adresser un mot 脙聽 l'extr脙陋me droite: nos enfants vont bien. Leur seul probl脙篓me dans la vie, c'est votre haine.

Aujourd'hui est un jour particulier pour l'Union europ脙漏enne.

Nous, LGBT, voyons le rempart que doit 脙陋tre l'Europe pour la garantie de nos droits fondamentaux 脙陋tre fragilis脙漏 par l'arriv脙漏e de l'extr脙陋me droite 脙聽 la vice-pr脙漏sidence de la Commission.

Nous, LGBT, savons plus que quiconque ce que signifie l'extr脙陋me droite au pouvoir, et les discours d'aujourd'hui nous l'ont encore montr脙漏. Cela signifie la remise en cause de nos familles, de la filiation de nos enfants; la stigmatisation, les violences, le recul de nos droits si durement conquis.

Le n脙漏ofascisme n'a pas sa place dans une Commission cens脙漏e prot脙漏ger nos droits. L'Agence europ脙漏enne des droits fondamentaux a tir脙漏 la sonnette d'alarme cet 脙漏t脙漏: les violences contre les minorit脙漏s sexuelles et de genre ne font qu'augmenter en Europe.

Alors je voudrais dire ici que je me battrai, pendant cinq ans, pour toutes les m脙篓res lesbiennes italiennes qui ont vu leurs droits remis en cause, pour les activistes hongrois victimes de l脙漏gislations anti-LGBT, pour celles et ceux qui subissent la torture des th脙漏rapies de conversion, et pour toutes les victimes de l'homophobie et de la transphobie. Nous ne baisserons pas la t脙陋te.

La compromission avec l'extr脙陋me droite envoie un signal clair aujourd'hui: nos droits, nos vies ne sont pas votre priorit脙漏. Il est temps que l'Europe agisse pour nos droits.

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora comisaria Dalli, despu脙漏s de quince脗聽a脙卤os de vigencia de la Carta de los Derechos Fundamentales de la Uni脙鲁n Europea 芒聙聰con el mismo valor que los Tratados芒聙聰, hay una lecci脙鲁n muy clara, y es que aquellos Gobiernos escorados a la ultraderecha que hostigan a la comunidad LGTBI o restringen los derechos de esas personas plantean un problema sist脙漏mico al Estado de Derecho, a la independencia judicial, al pluralismo informativo y a derechos fundamentales consagrados por la Carta 芒聙聰el de no discriminaci脙鲁n para empezar芒聙聰, pero tambi脙漏n al derecho a la vida privada y al inter脙漏s superior de los menores, porque atenta contra la vida privada y los derechos de los menores el pa脙颅s que, no reconociendo el matrimonio entre personas del mismo sexo, sin embargo, no permite la libre circulaci脙鲁n ni residencia de las personas casadas del mismo sexo y porque, adem脙隆s, atenta tambi脙漏n contra el derecho al inter脙漏s superior de los menores el que discrimina a los menores, nacidos o adoptados en el seno de una pareja del mismo sexo, por raz脙鲁n de su origen.

Por tanto, comisaria Dalli, usted que ha hecho un trabajo sobresaliente 芒聙聰que le reconocemos y agradecemos芒聙聰 d脙颅gale a su sucesora que se comprometa con la Estrategia para la igualdad de las personas LGBTIQ 2020-2025 y se comprometa a completar el trabajo de hacer del delito de odio que incita a la violencia de odio contra las personas LGTBI un delito europeo incompatible con el Derecho europeo.

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, LGBTIQ+-termi on se, mit脙陇 ilmeisesti t脙陇ll脙陇 hetkell脙陇 tulisi k脙陇ytt脙陇脙陇 seksuaali- ja sukupuoliv脙陇hemmist脙露ist脙陇. Kohta alkaa loppua aakkoset kesken, joten puhutaanpa nyt hetki n脙陇ist脙陇 nykyisist脙陇kin kirjaimista. Ty脙露 LGB:n hyv脙陇ksi eli lesbojen, homojen ja biseksuaalien tasavertaiseksi kohteluksi on sit脙陇 tavallista tasa-arvoty脙露t脙陇. Sit脙陇 on Euroopassa tehty pitk脙陇脙陇n ja t脙陇t脙陇 kannattaa jokaisen olla tukemassa. Maailmanlaajuisesti siin脙陇 on viel脙陇 paljon teht脙陇v脙陇脙陇.

Mutta kun tullaan t脙陇h脙陇n kirjainyhdistelm脙陇n loppup脙陇脙陇h脙陇n, se on sitten asia erikseen. Transaktivismi on tuottanut meille my脙露s hirvitt脙陇vi脙陇 vaatimuksia, joita v脙陇itet脙陇脙陇n tasa-arvoty脙露ksi. Min脙陇 en tule koskaan puolustamaan sellaista woke-ideologiaa, joka sallii lasten satuttamisen sukupuolenkorjausprosessin eri vaiheilla. Siin脙陇 ei ole kyse tasa-arvosta. Siin脙陇 on kyse pahoinpitelyst脙陇.

脗聽脗聽Dainius 脜陆alimas (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madame脗聽President,脗聽dear colleagues, the European Union is a promise to uphold democracy and human rights. However, as regards LGBTIQ脗聽persons, we are still falling short of this promise.脗聽We failed to create a common European area of human rights, where dignity and family life of each person is equally recognised and protected.

In some EU countries, including my own country, Lithuania, same芒聙聭sex couples are denied of legal recognition of their family life. Children growing up in those families are subject to humiliation, laws prohibiting information about the LGBTIQ community are being adopted and even LGBTIQ 'free zones' are being declared. Such discriminatory ideas perfectly resonates to one of the pillars of Russian world ideology, under which the LGBTIQ community has been declared to be the first enemy after Ukrainians.

Therefore, the EU must decisively act by first launching timely infringement procedures against any violation of fundamental rights. Second, ensuring full enforcement of court judgements protecting LGBTIQ persons in all Member States. Third, restricting EU funding to those Member States who gravely break the Charter of Fundamental Rights.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Katrin Langensiepen (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Ich bin, glaube ich, hier die einzige Frau im progressiven Lager, die nicht lesbisch, queer oder sonst was ist 芒聙聯 und ich f脙录hle mich nicht bedroht. Ist das verr脙录ckt! Und ich finde diese Wortbeitr脙陇ge bisher wirklich schmerzhaft. Was nimmt man Menschenkindern weg, wenn sie sagen: 芒聙聻Ich bin lesbisch, ich bin schwul芒聙聹? Ich merke es auf dem Schulhof 芒聙聯 als Junge 芒聙聯, dass der Nachbar, der neben mir ist, dass ich den irgendwie sexy finde. Und mit dem will ich vielleicht mal ein Eis essen gehen, wenn ich 14, 15 bin. Es tut niemandem weh, wir nehmen niemandem etwas weg.

Und wir haben 芒聙聯 um jetzt mal auf den Boden der Professionalit脙陇t zur脙录ckzukommen, liebe Kommissarin Helena Dalli, liebes Team hier im Parlament 芒聙聯 sehr lange gek脙陇mpft und wir k脙陇mpfen sehr lange bereits f脙录r die Umsetzung der Antidiskriminierungsrichtlinie. Und das ist ein sehr, sehr wichtiger Punkt. Diese Antidiskriminierungsrichtlinie umfasst einmal Menschen aus der LGBTIQ+-Gruppe, aber auch Menschen mit Behinderungen. Und wir hoffen, dass wir es endlich schaffen, dass Deutschland das deblockiert, dass wir hier nicht der boo guy oder die boo woman sind in der Europ脙陇ischen Kommission, im Rat, dass wir sagen, wir wollen hier weitergehen, wenn es um Menschenrechte geht, wenn es um unsere Rechte geht. Das ist jetzt hier unsere Arbeit f脙录r die n脙陇chsten Monate, wenn es hier um Neuwahlen Ende Februar geht. Daf脙录r werden wir k脙陇mpfen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear people of Europe, I would like to use this opportunity to challenge one fundamentally wrong talking point of the intolerant, the religious zealots and those who use the discrimination of people to gain power.

They say tolerance for people of the LGBTIQ+ community is an ideology. How can you claim the nature of people is an ideology? How can you claim that tolerance for that is an ideology? This idea is so inhumane and backward it leaves me speechless every time I hear it.

Mr Tynkkynen, for example: is the name used to describe this group of people really relevant? And in what political movement 芒聙聯 and it is, of course, a political movement because it had to become a political movement to fight their suppression 芒聙聯 are there only good ideas?

I myself, I am not a member of this community, but I am proud to be an ally.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Micha脜聜 Wawrykiewicz (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Madam脗聽Commissioner, I would like to refer to one legal issue because the representative of Orb脙隆n's regime pointed out today in this House that here in this House we are telling lies and manipulating about Hungary.脗聽He pointed at Article脗聽14 of the Charter of Fundamental Rights and I will answer this nonsense as a lawyer.

This is not about freedom of education of our kids. This is about disgusting discrimination in the middle of Europe in the twenty芒聙聭first century.脗聽We and the Commission fortunately reacted and we have infringement procedure in the Court of Justice.

It is not Article脗聽14 that is violated, it is about Article脗聽2 of the Treaty on European Union and violation of fundamental rights of the European Union, like tolerance and dignity, and you will soon find out this from the verdict of the European Court of Justice.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Ende der spontanen Wortmeldungen)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, thank you, honourable Members, for this debate. Equality, human dignity, freedom, democracy, the rule of law and respect for human rights are core values of the EU, enshrined in the Treaty of the European Union and in the Charter of Fundamental Rights of the EU.

The Commission is concerned about any development, including legislative acts, that could put at risk the effective implementation of EU law, and remains fully committed to addressing inequalities and discrimination targeting LGBTIQ persons, as outlined in our LGBTIQ Equality Strategy of 2020 to 2025.

As regards the law adopted by the Bulgarian Parliament on the 7 August, amending and supplementing the Pre-School Education Act, I can clarify that I have asked the Bulgarian minister to explain the potential consequences that breaches of this law will have, and I urged him to ensure that the implementation of the law is fully in line with the principle of equality and respect, dignity and the human rights of children and LGBTIQ persons.

Once more, let me reassure you that the Commission will use all the instruments at its disposal to protect the EU's core values, and will not hesitate to act as necessary.

But now, please allow me to take this opportunity to thank you, to thank this Parliament for these five years. I am sure that the Commission would not have done what it managed to do without your support, without your knowledge, without your experience and, above all, without your love.

Your hearts are in the right place and your passion to see that no matter how we are born 芒聙聯 nobody chooses how they are born 芒聙聯 we must not let that interfere with our lives, with our freedom, with our dignity, and that we can all reach our full potential without discrimination.

So I wish you all to continue fighting in this Parliament as you have done in these past five years. You were really power to my elbow and we have managed to do these things together. We did them together. And please 芒聙聯 I'm sure you will 芒聙聯 continue holding the fort so that Europe 芒聙聯 the European Union 芒聙聯 will be an example to the world when it comes to the respect of human dignity, of human rights, of love.

脗聽

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Die Aussprache ist geschlossen.

脗聽脗聽Marcos Ros Sempere (S&D), por escrito. 芒聙聯 Pese a la aprobaci脙鲁n de la Estrategia para la igualdad de las personas LGBTIQ 2020-2025, nuestro grupo S&D se mantiene cr脙颅tico, pues la igualdad para las personas LGBTIQ a脙潞n no es una realidad. Este debate ha permitido se脙卤alar c脙鲁mo los Gobiernos escorados a la ultraderecha est脙隆n hostigando a la comunidad LGTBI y restringiendo sus derechos, lo que plantea un problema sist脙漏mico al Estado de Derecho, la independencia judicial, el pluralismo informativo y los derechos fundamentales consagrados en la Carta de los Derechos Fundamentales de la Uni脙鲁n Europea, como el derecho a la vida privada y el inter脙漏s superior de los menores.

A su vez, se ha puesto en valor la aprobaci脙鲁n de esta primera estrategia de igualdad LGBTIQ, con el fin de crear las condiciones para que todos puedan vivir, prosperar y liderar, independientemente de su orientaci脙鲁n sexual, caracter脙颅sticas sexuales, identidad o expresi脙鲁n de g脙漏nero.

脗聽

14. Promoting a favourable framework for venture capital financing and safe foreign direct investments in the EU (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache 脙录ber die Erkl脙陇rung der Kommission zur F脙露rderung eines g脙录nstigen Umfelds f脙录r die Risikokapitalfinanzierung und sichere ausl脙陇ndische Direktinvestitionen in der EU [2024/2949(RSP)].

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mairead McGuinness, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, it's nice to see a little bit of celebration in the Chamber before I speak, so let's just give them a moment 芒聙聯 well done! I'm afraid I'm not going to match the passion of the last debate.

Commissioner Dalli may say 'we're all Mediterranean' and they may be Mediterranean, but the Irish are quite passionate, I can say!

I suppose there's an air maybe, among the outgoing Commissioners 芒聙聯 and I don't know whether it's freedom, relief or just joy 芒聙聯 that we're happy to be in the Chamber this evening. I know it's for my last debate, on this really important topic which matters to everybody, citizens, small businesses and large companies. It fits very well 芒聙聯 this debate 芒聙聯 with the morning's activities in Parliament and the voting in of the new Commission, so very timely, and I compliment colleagues who tabled this topic.

It's at the very heart of strengthening the European Union's competitiveness. Our start-ups and scale-ups in many sectors, from deep tech, clean tech or biotech, they're key drivers of innovation and growth and job creation in the European Union. And we know that these companies crucially need capital, including private capital, often originating beyond their home country, to allow them grow and expand.

So this issue of financing is core to securing a more competitive European Union. I can say that together we have already set the groundwork in place on creating better financing opportunities for businesses of all sizes, from SMEs to large companies, and I know that many who are taking part in this debate support the SME sector and focus on it. The incoming Commission, as we know, will deepen work in this area, and that was outlined by President von der Leyen in what will be a major collective effort to strengthen the EU's resilience and competitiveness in the years ahead.

The truth is that our companies face real difficulties in accessing the financing they need for growth. We face a significant funding gap for our innovative companies, particularly late-stage scale-ups, and as a result, EU scale-ups often seek capital and, in particular, equity capital from other countries, and are more likely to relocate. We all know companies in our own Member States that have taken the decision to leave the European Union and seek listings overseas. Now, this represents a missed opportunity for growth, for innovation and employment in the European Union, and it is an unwelcome trend, representing also a loss of EU competitiveness.

Now, we have made progress to improve the situation for smaller companies and their investors. We have made it easier for these companies to access EU stock markets through simplified listing rules under the EU Listing Act. We have streamlined the rules for long-term investments into alternative assets, including innovative companies and projects with the reform of our rules on European long-term investment funds. We are making information on listed companies and SMEs more easily available to investors through the European Single Access Point, and we continue to mobilise private capital through the InvestEU Programme.

Boosting the EU's venture and other risk capital is one of the main objectives of the Savings and Investment Union, announced by President von der Leyen in her political guidelines, building on the progress made on Capital Markets Union in my time as Commissioner, working with this Parliament and the Council. The entire financing chain must be strengthened from early-stage companies to more mature ones, which necessitates a solid venture capital ecosystem and well-functioning and integrated capital markets.

The Commission's services are identifying the main challenges for venture capital financing, and the most frequently cited challenge is attracting capital from institutional investors. European institutional investors, insurers or pension funds do not invest in growth capital as much as their counterparts in the US, and there are several reasons for this, including a lack of well-capitalised pension funds or sovereign wealth funds in the European Union. There is also risk aversion, or a lack of familiarity with this more heterogeneous asset class, and certain requirements at national level. This could be partly addressed by mobilising private capital with the support of public programmes. The InvestEU Programme as well as initiatives like the European Tech Champions Initiative are important steps in this direction, but we need to do more to attract private sector investments to key EU policy areas.

The second challenge is insufficient scale of EU venture and growth capital funds. There are very few growth capital funds in the EU able to finance large financing operations, let me just be very clear. This again reinforces the dependence on non-EU capital for scale-ups, where around half of financing for large deals comes from outside the European Union. This was highlighted in the Commission communication on the EU Biotech Initiative. In line with the communication, we have just launched a study to explore the barriers to consolidation and scaling up investment funds, including venture capital and private equity funds, and this work will support the incoming college in formulating further policy directions.

Last but not least, this form of financing is hindered by the fragmentation of rules across the European Union from the perspective of companies and investors. Our investors face different national legal systems in some areas of corporate law, including different incorporation requirements for companies as well as securities law and taxation, and this lack of harmonisation results in significant additional costs and legal uncertainty. This really deters EU investors from engaging in what I prefer to say are 'investments in the single market' 芒聙聯 but we often say 'cross-border'. And that's one problem: the mindset is still 'cross-border', in a single market. This is not how it should be, so I would appeal in this coming and fresh and new Parliament to drop the words 'cross-border'. We need to talk 'single market', because these obstacles are also deterring foreign investors from investing in the European Union.

Now, a word about foreign investors. We have to ensure that the European Union becomes a more attractive investment destination, and that is both for EU and foreign investors. And we have to do that while safeguarding our economic security. There is an external dimension to our investment policy. Europe is and will remain open to foreign direct investment, and it is interesting that despite a global decline in FDI inflows, the EU-27 experienced an increase in net FDI inflows last year. Attracting and promoting foreign direct investments is vital for the Union's economic development.

Now, at the same time, this openness of the EU to foreign direct investments is not unconditional. The European Economic Security Strategy emphasises the need to balance economic openness with the protection of our economic security and open strategic autonomy, given the growing geopolitical risks. This includes taking a more assertive stance on security and public order risks arising from foreign direct investments that may negatively affect Europe. The FDI Screening Regulation provides a framework for the EU and the Member States to assess and, if necessary, address and mitigate these risks, and the Commission's legislative proposal, which will amend the FDI Screening Regulation, will strengthen that. I now look forward to hearing your contributions.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marion Walsmann, im Namen der PPE-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, sehr geehrte Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Europa steht vor der Herausforderung, ein attraktiver Standort f脙录r Wagniskapital und sichere ausl脙陇ndische Direktinvestitionen zu bleiben. Das ist nicht nur eine Frage der Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit, sondern auch der Zukunftsf脙陇higkeit unseres Kontinents. Die Zahlen sprechen eine ern脙录chternde Sprache. W脙陇hrend die USA und China massiv in Start-ups und disruptive Technologien investieren, bewegt sich Europa kleinschrittig fort und riskiert, Innovationspotenzial zu verlieren. Europa h脙陇lt aktuell nur 5脗聽% der aufgelegten globalen Risikokapitalfonds und nur 6脗聽% der weltweiten Finanzmittel f脙录r KI芒聙聭Neugr脙录ndungen.

Die Gr脙录nde liegen auf der Hand. Wir haben es mit einer unzureichenden F脙露rderung von Innovationen zu tun, die oft schon auf halbem Weg zur Kommerzialisierung ins Stocken geraten. Der Binnenmarkt bleibt durch Fragmentierung und b脙录rokratische H脙录rden geschw脙陇cht. Dies schreckt nat脙录rlich Investoren ab, insbesondere in strategisch wichtigen Zukunftsbranchen. Dabei ver脙陇ndern wagniskapitalfinanzierte Unternehmen ganze Wirtschaftszweige und haben nicht zuletzt auch ausl脙陇ndische Direktinvestitionen in vielen Branchen angekurbelt. Bahnbrechende Ideen brauchen daher vor Ort das entsprechende finanzielle Backing. Wir brauchen in Europa dringend ein starkes, f脙录r KMU zug脙陇nglicheres und b脙录rokratie脙陇rmeres Finanzierungsumfeld.

Es ist entscheidend, dass wir eine st脙陇rkere politische Koordinierung bei strategischen Investitionen anstreben. Dazu geh脙露rt auch, die Innovationsf脙露rderung st脙陇rker zu harmonisieren und Finanzmittel gezielt auf Schl脙录sselbereiche zu konzentrieren. Nur mit mutigen Reformen und einer besseren Koordinierung k脙露nnen wir sicherstellen, dass Start-ups und Scale-ups nicht nur hier entstehen, sondern auch in Europa wachsen. Hier braucht es klares Handeln und Engagement. Dazu fordere ich auch im Namen von SME Europe auf.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bruno Gon脙搂alves, em nome do Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Caros Colegas, antes da crise financeira, o PIB da zona euro era compar脙隆vel ao dos Estados Unidos. Hoje, 脙漏 apenas cerca de 60 %. Perante esta anemia econ脙鲁mica, a resposta n脙拢o pode nem deve ser mais do mesmo.

Precisamos de um Fundo Europeu para a Competitividade que promova a nossa ind脙潞stria e o emprego qualificado. Mas tamb脙漏m devemos mobilizar investimento privado, criando melhores condi脙搂脙碌es para o nosso ecossistema de startups inovadoras, ao tornar a Europa num destino apetec脙颅vel para o capital de risco.

Queremos investimento em setores produtivos com potencial inovador, sim. Mas este exerc脙颅cio de capta脙搂脙拢o de investimento estrangeiro n脙拢o pode ser feito 脙聽 pressa, sem crit脙漏rio. N脙拢o devemos alimentar a especula脙搂脙拢o, como acontece tantas vezes no caso da habita脙搂脙拢o. Nem podemos depauperar a nossa autonomia estrat脙漏gica vendendo ativos p脙潞blicos em setores estrat脙漏gicos a pa脙颅ses terceiros.

Ao respeitar estes princ脙颅pios, evitando os erros do passado, temos todas as condi脙搂脙碌es para construir um mercado europeu de capitais mais din脙垄mico, que seja justo e seguro.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽St脙漏phanie Yon-Courtin, au nom du groupe Renew. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame la Commissaire, pour gagner en comp脙漏titivit脙漏, il faut laisser nos entreprises europ脙漏ennes innover. Mais si l'argent public est rare et que les banques ne financent pas le risque, vers qui peuvent se tourner nos entreprises? Les march脙漏s financiers, oui, mais lesquels?

Aujourd'hui, l'Europe n'est pas 脙聽 la hauteur. Nos p脙漏pites n'ont pas d'autre choix que de se financer outre-Atlantique: les 脙聣tats-Unis investissent en moyenne six fois plus dans les start-up qu'en Europe. Pour garantir notre autonomie strat脙漏gique, notre continent doit reprendre la main sur les secteurs-cl脙漏s comme la tech et la d脙漏fense. Il est donc temps de passer 脙聽 la vitesse sup脙漏rieure.

Cela veut dire d'abord des investissements publics plus cibl脙漏s et plus cons脙漏quents. Donnons plus de moyens au Fonds europ脙漏en d'investissement pour faire plus et mieux. Il a financ脙漏 plus de 350脗聽000脗聽PME en脗聽2023 et mettra 脙聽 disposition un fonds pour financer les champions europ脙漏ens de la tech.

Il faut 脙漏galement stimuler les investissements priv脙漏s en cr脙漏ant un march脙漏 europ脙漏en des capitaux. Levons les obstacles pour les PME avec des r脙篓gles plus simples et moins lourdes, faisons de l'Europe un terrain d'innovation. Ce n'est qu'en d脙漏bloquant notre potentiel que nous cr脙漏erons des superstars de l'innovation europ脙漏enne.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Damian Boeselager, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, first of all, I want to thank you, Commissioner, for your five years of service: we will spend another five years in this Chamber, but you are free to go.

I think the problem awareness is there. We all know that we don't have enough VC capital, and this has been said, I'm very happy that Draghi put it in a new report so we can all see it very clearly again.

But then, I wonder, why don't we do anything? The reason that currently nothing is happening 芒聙聯 and that Nvidia, Microsoft, Alphabet, all these companies are basically being established in the US 芒聙聯 is because of our national egos. We had this conversation recently, the Capital Markets Union is in everyone's ears, but nobody is actually committing because they all want their nations to prevail. That is weakness and that is the perspective of cross-border rather than interior market.

So I think we really need to do a couple of things now: we need to unlock the Capital Markets Union. We need to ensure that we really work with the 28th regime, wherever we can, to establish an 'EU Inc', at least to make it easier to invest and scale. We need to make it easier to leverage mezzanine funds and EIB and EIF investments, and we need to make it easier for institutional investors to invest in VCs.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fernando Navarrete Rojas (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora comisaria, se脙卤or脙颅as, que Europa se haya quedado rezagada en la revoluci脙鲁n tecnol脙鲁gica de los 脙潞ltimos quince a脙卤os tiene, efectivamente, m脙隆s que ver con el infradesarrollo del ecosistema del capital riesgo que con debates econ脙鲁micos que, a veces, concitan m脙隆s atenci脙鲁n.

Y esto es as脙颅 porque el capital riesgo es, en primer lugar, la forma natural de financiar proyectos innovadores que aspiran a suponer una disrupci脙鲁n en los mercados o a competir globalmente. Y, en segundo lugar, porque es un ecosistema que impulsa la difusi脙鲁n tecnol脙鲁gica y del conocimiento, generando as脙颅 nuevas historias de 脙漏xito.

Desafortunadamente, como ya se ha dicho, nuestro mercado tiene un tama脙卤o de solo un 80脗聽% del estadounidense. Muchas buenas ideas empiezan en Europa y acaban dando el salto a Estados Unidos para triunfar en el mundo. Adem脙隆s, alrededor del 40脗聽% de los fondos de capital riesgo que se invierten en la Uni脙鲁n provienen del exterior. Esto es un reconocimiento global al potencial de nuestros emprendedores, pero tambi脙漏n la demostraci脙鲁n de que no tenemos ni el ecosistema ni el apetito de riesgo necesario para propulsar nuestras buenas ideas.

Europa es excedentaria en capital financiero, pero muy deficitaria en apetito de riesgo y de disrupci脙鲁n. Nuestros mercados financieros deben, por tanto, permanecer abiertos al mundo. Esto no implica ignorar los riesgos geopol脙颅ticos ni las diferencias de comportamiento que importantes inversores pseudoestatales desarrollan en sus pa脙颅ses de origen. Pero la soluci脙鲁n no pasa por cerrar nuestros mercados, sino por el fortalecimiento del marco institucional a nivel nacional y europeo, garantizando que quienes inviertan aqu脙颅 cumplan nuestras normas.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nikos Papandreou (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, with my turn, I would like to thank the Commissioner for her good work. I've only been here a year, but it's been great to hear you.

I'd like to comment on what my colleague from Berlin just said 芒聙聯 I think you were from Volt, I forget which party you are in now 芒聙聯 the capital markets union will be difficult unless we break the backs 芒聙聯 that sounds a little bit radical 芒聙聯 of the banks in the countries. No country wants to give that one up, because for many reasons. I won't go into the details. So how do we do that in the next five years? It's not clear.

Second of all, the lack of venture capital, among the many bad things that it causes, is that the talent that we produce with our free education 芒聙聯 more or less subsidised 芒聙聯 goes abroad, comes back as a foreign company and runs the show here. That's the third thing. And Draghi 芒聙聯 this is why it's very topical in the new five years 芒聙聯 supports 芒聙聯 and I think that's also the S&D position 芒聙聯 more public investment to de-risk and to allow for venture capital. It's not an easy thing. We have a few years to do it. I hope we succeed because that really is the way we will enter Web3 and the 21st century.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Francesco Torselli (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, gli investimenti diretti dall'estero possono rappresentare un'opportunit脙聽 di crescita e di innovazione per chi li riceve. Ma quando si affronta questo argomento, per脙虏, sono necessari almeno un paio di distinguo.

Il primo: la strategicit脙聽 del settore di investimento: dobbiamo evitare il rischio di controlli in ambiti nevralgici per la vita di ogni singola nazione. Il secondo: l'Unione europea ad oggi non ha un sistema omogeneo di controllo degli FDI e questo ci rende ancora troppo permeabili.

Gli FDI possono rappresentare per脙虏 uno strumento per la tutela occupazionale. Nell'ottobre scorso il Primo ministro italiano, Giorgia Meloni, ha incontrato il governo cinese invitandolo ad investire nel nostro Paese, cos脙卢 da trasformare un'aggressione al mercato interno in un'opportunit脙聽 di lavoro.

In definitiva, un'Europa competitiva non si sottrae al capitale straniero, ma difende gli ambiti fondamentali, ponendo al primo posto tra le priorit脙聽 la tutela della sicurezza e degli asset strategici nazionali.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Niels Flemming Hansen (PPE). 芒聙聯 Fru formand! If脙赂lge Det Internationale Energiagentur er 35 % af de teknologier, vi skal bruge for at n脙楼 Net Zero i 2050, endnu ikke p脙楼 markedet. Det viser behovet for at 脙赂ge investeringerne i gr脙赂n innovation. Venturekapital er centralt i det 脙赂kosystem, der finansierer innovative virksomheder. Det kr脙娄ver villighed til at tage h脙赂j risiko for at opn脙楼 et h脙赂jt afkast. Men Europa halter bagefter USA, fordi institutionelle investeringer i EU engagerer sig langt mindre i venturekapital. Det m脙楼 脙娄ndres, hvis vi skal sikre EU's konkurrenceevne og gr脙赂nne f脙赂rerposition. Jeg har tre klare anbefalinger: G脙赂r det lettere for institutionelle investeringer at deltage i venturekapitalmarkedet og styrke Den Europ脙娄iske Investeringsfond rolle, m脙楼lret st脙赂tte til cleantechfonde og s脙赂rg for at sikre strenge b脙娄redygtighedskriterier og udvid direkte investeringer i europ脙娄iske cleantech-startups. Den Europ脙娄iske Investeringsbank, EIB, b脙赂r st脙赂tte udvalgte virksomheder direkte. Vi skal ogs脙楼 huske, at for hver enkelt euro, vi skal spendere, skal vi tiltr脙娄kke mellem to og fem private euro for at lykkes med den gr脙赂nne omstilling. Derfor b脙赂r det v脙娄re en prioritet for den nye Kommission at fremskynde arbejdet med at rydde op i junglen af lovgivning, der h脙娄mmer gr脙娄nseoverskridende investeringer. Fremtiden venter ikke. Vi skal handle nu for klimaet, for industrien og for Europas gr脙赂nne lederskab.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matthias Ecke (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Frau Kommissarin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Mit einem Umsatz von 2脗聽Milliarden Euro ist der Zahlungsdienstleister Klarna genau die Art von Unicorn, die wir eigentlich hier in der EU brauchen. Aber Klarna zeigt uns eigentlich unsere Schw脙陇chen ziemlich gnadenlos auf. 2020 verkauft das Unternehmen einen Minderheitenanteil an den chinesischen Tech-Giganten Alibaba. Jetzt geht Klarna an die B脙露rse, aber nicht in Stockholm, Paris oder Frankfurt, sondern in den USA.

Europa hat ein Problem: W脙陇hrend andere Weltregionen mit gezielten Investitionen Innovationen f脙露rdern, bleiben wir oft zur脙录ck, und zwar nicht wegen mangelnder Ideen 芒聙聯 die gibt es hier genug 芒聙聯, sondern wegen der fehlenden Finanzierung. Wir sind stark, wenn es um Forschung geht, aber zu schwach, wenn es darum geht, die Ergebnisse der Forschung zur Marktreife zu bringen. Uns fehlt halt oft das Risikokapital.

Wir brauchen ein 脙聳kosystem, das Wissenschaft, Wirtschaft und Kapital besser vernetzt. Ausl脙陇ndische Direktinvestitionen k脙露nnen da eine Rolle spielen, aber wir d脙录rfen auch nicht naiv sein. Wo jetzt das Kapital aus dem Ausland noch willkommen ist, schafft es morgen vielleicht neue Abh脙陇ngigkeiten. Deswegen braucht Europa selber mehr Wagniskapital durch einen starken Binnenmarkt, durch die Kapitalmarktunion und auch durch gezielte staatliche Instrumente, damit die Einh脙露rner der Zukunft am Ende des Regenbogens vielleicht auch mal die EU finden.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gheorghe Piperea (ECR). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, China este un cal troian construit pe dumping, pe subven脠聸ii care nu 脠聸in cont de regulile pie脠聸ei libere 脠聶i pe produse ieftine. Un acord UE-China s-ar putea transforma dintr-un succes al pie脠聸ei europene 脙庐ntr-o manipulare agresiv脛聝 a pie脠聸ei unice a UE. Pentru multe companii europene, a concura cu pre脠聸urile 脠聶i volumele de m脛聝rfuri ale chinezilor nu 脙庐nseamn脛聝 doar pierderi financiare, ci chiar faliment. Acordul privind investi脠聸iile dintre UE 脠聶i China, semnat 脙庐n decembrie 2020, trebuie s脛聝 脙庐脠聶i 脙庐nt脛聝reasc脛聝 politica de de-risking.

Unele state sunt permeabile 脙庐n continuare pentru investi脠聸iile chineze, pentru c脛聝 au toleran脠聸脛聝 din partea UE. Altele, 脙庐ns脛聝, nu. Este un dublu standard 脠聶i o 脙庐nc脛聝lcare a pie脠聸ei unice. 脙聨n alt脛聝 ordine de idei, investi脠聸iile str脛聝ine 脙庐n Europa, inclusiv cele chineze脠聶ti, nu ar trebui s脛聝 scape principiului impozit脛聝rii plusvalorii acolo unde se produce. Companiilor Big Pharma, Tech sau Finance ar trebui s脛聝 li se interzic脛聝 exportul de plusvaloare 脙庐n paradisuri fiscale.

脗聽脗聽Isabel Benjumea Benjumea (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora comisaria, le deseo todo lo mejor en la nueva etapa que comienza.

Les voy a leer unos datos de un informe que compara el mercado de capital de riesgo en los Estados Unidos y en Europa. Y, verdaderamente, es demoledor.

Por un lado, nos dice que la Uni脙鲁n Europea representa menos del 50脗聽% del tama脙卤o del mercado de capital de riesgo americano.

Luego nos dice que las rondas de capital en los Estados Unidos son m脙隆s grandes en todas las etapas, en las iniciales y en las de crecimiento.

Tambi脙漏n nos pone de manifiesto que las inversiones en Europa se destinan principalmente a gastos del Pacto Verde Europeo, mientras que en los Estados Unidos se est脙隆n destinando a inversiones en inteligencia artificial, en salud o en defensa.

Nos pone de manifiesto que no creamos start-ups altamente valoradas por los mercados y que las que creamos simplemente se van. Y el n脙潞mero de 脗芦unicornios脗禄 en los Estados Unidos es tres veces superior al de 脗芦unicornios脗禄 en Europa.

Y, finalmente, pone de manifiesto el grave da脙卤o de la fragmentaci脙鲁n regulatoria en Europa, que lo que impide es un mercado 脙隆gil de mercados de capital de riesgo.

La gran pregunta es si queremos una Europa de emprendedores, de empresas que generen trabajo, que generen riqueza, o queremos una Europa de inversi脙鲁n p脙潞blica o de funcionarios. Simplemente tenemos que reaccionar y reaccionar ya.

脗聽脗聽Francisco Assis (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, nesta Casa, neste Parlamento, poderemos divergir e divergiremos certamente nas solu脙搂脙碌es, mas n脙拢o discordamos em rela脙搂脙拢o ao diagn脙鲁stico tra脙搂ado no Relat脙鲁rio Draghi. Uma Europa com sinais de decad脙陋ncia econ脙鲁mica, com n脙颅veis baix脙颅ssimos de investimento, com perda de relev脙垄ncia no quadro internacional e com desafios complexos de ordem demogr脙隆fica e geopol脙颅tica.

Nesta nova economia global de r脙隆pida transforma脙搂脙拢o e inova脙搂脙拢o, o capital de risco 脙漏 essencial para apoiar a cria脙搂脙拢o e desenvolvimento de novos neg脙鲁cios, fun脙搂脙拢o n脙拢o integralmente assegurada pelo setor banc脙隆rio, como sabemos.

Apesar dos avan脙搂os dos 脙潞ltimos anos, a Europa continua com uma express脙拢o diminuta no mercado mundial. 脙聣 preciso fomentar uma maior cultura de risco na sociedade europeia e estimular a oferta de capital de risco. Neste campo, o Banco Europeu de Investimento poder脙隆 desempenhar um papel importante.

Contudo, devemos afirmar tamb脙漏m aqui, com clareza, o seguinte: o modelo europeu de desenvolvimento passa pela articula脙搂脙拢o virtuosa da moderniza脙搂脙拢o e crescimento econ脙鲁mico com um Estado social que protege e tamb脙漏m liberta.

Esse 脙漏 o nosso modelo, no qual, creio eu, a grande maioria da popula脙搂脙拢o se continua a rever. N脙拢o h脙隆 prosperidade econ脙鲁mica sem bem-estar social.

(O orador aceita responder a uma pergunta 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄)

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left), Pergunta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Senhor Deputado Francisco Assis, o Relat脙鲁rio Draghi diz e cito: 脗芦Precisamos de uma pol脙颅tica externa econ脙鲁mica genu脙颅na da UE. A UE deve coordenar acordos comerciais preferenciais e investimento direto脗禄. Fim de cita脙搂脙拢o. Nesse relat脙鲁rio 脙漏 proposta tamb脙漏m a cria脙搂脙拢o de um mecanismo de decis脙拢o de aprova脙搂脙拢o de investimento direto externo comum, ou seja, um mecanismo que permita 脙聽 Uni脙拢o Europeia decidir do investimento direto externo que 脙漏 feito em cada pa脙颅s da Uni脙拢o Europeia, naturalmente feito por empresas, por investidores exteriores ao espa脙搂o da Uni脙拢o Europeia.

Isto, s脙鲁 por si, j脙隆 significa um fator de preocupa脙搂脙拢o, particularmente para pa脙颅ses como Portugal, mas 脙漏 um fator de preocupa脙搂脙拢o ainda acrescido, porque isto, junto 脙聽 pol脙颅tica de facilita脙搂脙拢o de fus脙碌es e aquisi脙搂脙碌es, naturalmente remete o nosso pa脙颅s para um papel de muita dificuldade em lidar com o investimento direto externo, se ele for condicionado pela Uni脙拢o Europeia. Gostava de saber qual 脙漏 que 脙漏 a opini脙拢o do Partido Socialista relativamente a esta mat脙漏ria.

脗聽脗聽Francisco Assis (S&D), Resposta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Muito obrigado, Senhor Deputado Jo脙拢o Oliveira, pela pergunta que me coloca. Como eu comecei por dizer, n脙鲁s estamos de acordo com o diagn脙鲁stico constante do Relat脙鲁rio Draghi. As solu脙搂脙碌es s脙拢o mat脙漏rias que agora devem ser objeto de discuss脙拢o. E essa 脙漏 uma delas, 脙漏 uma mat脙漏ria que deve ser discutida.

Creio que temos de encontrar uma solu脙搂脙拢o que articule as prioridades europeias com as prioridades nacionais. N脙拢o me passa pela cabe脙搂a, naturalmente, que os Estados nacionais fiquem numa posi脙搂脙拢o secund脙隆ria em rela脙搂脙拢o 脙聽s pol脙颅ticas que devem seguir em mat脙漏ria de ado脙搂脙拢o, de incremento do investimento externo. Temos que articular as duas dimens脙碌es e penso que 脙漏 isso que tem vindo a suceder, 脙漏 esse o caminho que devemos prosseguir. N脙拢o tenho nenhum receio nesse dom脙颅nio.脗聽Creio que a melhor pol脙颅tica ser脙隆 a pol脙颅tica em que se consiga articular op脙搂脙碌es nacionais com op脙搂脙碌es europeias. E 脙漏 esse o caminho que desde h脙隆 muitos anos, como sabe, n脙鲁s temos seguido, nomeadamente o Partido Socialista em Portugal.

脗聽脗聽J脙露rgen Warborn (PPE). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Tack kommission脙陇ren. Jag inser att det 脙陇r sista g脙楼ngen jag kan adressera dig som kommission脙陇r. Tack f脙露r ditt fina arbete under den h脙陇r tiden.

Det har n脙陇mnts tidigare att Europa har ett konkurrenskraftsproblem, och visst 脙陇r det s脙楼. Det 脙陇r naturligtvis sorgligt att det 脙陇r s脙楼, men det 脙陇r ocks脙楼 bra att det 脙陇r s脙楼 m脙楼nga som sl脙楼r fast att vi delar samma 脙楼sikt och att vi m脙楼ste g脙露ra n脙楼gonting f脙露r att ta tag i Europas konkurrenskraft.

F脙露r att l脙露sa utmaningen med Europas tillv脙陇xt 脙陇r det flera olika saker vi m脙楼ste g脙露ra. En av de viktigaste sakerna 脙陇r att s脙陇kerst脙陇lla att vi f脙露rb脙陇ttrar tillg脙楼ngen till kapital. N脙陇r jag tr脙陇ffar sm脙楼f脙露retagare s脙陇ger n脙陇stan alla att det 脙陇r ett av deras st脙露rsta problem. D脙陇rf脙露r m脙楼ste vi skapa ett europeiskt ISK, ett investeringssparkonto, som skapar incitament f脙露r europ脙漏er att investera i aktiemarknaden. Det skulle vara bra f脙露r dem, f脙露r det skulle 脙露ka deras ekonomiska m脙露jligheter, men det skulle framf脙露r allt ocks脙楼 vara bra f脙露r sm脙楼f脙露retagen, s脙楼 att de kan forts脙陇tta expandera, anst脙陇lla fler och skapa v脙陇lst脙楼nd till hela Europa.

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, Yhdysvalloissa maan suosituimman sosiaalisen median sovelluksen omistaa Kiina. Maan suurimman porsaanlihan tuottajan ja jalostajan omistaa Kiina. 900 miljardia dollaria maan julkisesta velasta omistaa Kiina.

Kiina-uhkaan on Yhdysvalloissa her脙陇tty toden teolla. Esimerkiksi viime viikolla Texasin osavaltion kuvern脙露脙露ri m脙陇脙陇r脙陇si kaikki osavaltion virastot myym脙陇脙陇n Kiinassa olevat sijoituksensa ja kielsi virastoja olemaan sijoittamasta en脙陇脙陇 maahan. Huhtikuussa Yhdysvaltojen edustajainhuoneessa hyv脙陇ksyttiin kiinalaisomisteisen TikTokin kielto.

T脙陇t脙陇 samaa tiukkuutta tarvitaan nyt Eurooppaan. Me emme voi antaa Kiinan dumpata halpatuotantoaan t脙陇nne ja sys脙陇t脙陇 omia yrityksi脙陇mme vaikeuksiin. Me emme voi antaa itsemme joutua Kiina-investoijien taloudelliseksi orjiksi. Kiina on vaarallinen valtio. Se pit脙陇脙陇 n脙陇ky脙陇 siin脙陇, miten Eurooppa toimii. Diplomatiaa on nyt korvattava tiukkuudella.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear Commissioner, being a new Member to the Parliament, I was not able to see your work in action, but I was told a lot of good things and having a great sense of love for your culture, I must say, you have the most Irish name that I've ever heard in my life.

Our newly elected President of the Commission said we need an economy that works for the people and I fully agree. Our strong economy and our unique single market is our biggest advantage in that more and more hostile world of ours.

I really hope that we will especially work together on the capital markets union, not only as a tool to foster both venture capital and foreign investment in the EU, but also strengthen a united Europe as a whole. Because our economy was the one thing that brought us together in the beginning and I think it can also bring us together in the future.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Ende der spontanen Wortmeldungen)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Now I would like to pass the floor to the Commissioner for the last time in this House as Commissioner. I would like to thank you very, very much, Madam Commissioner, for the work you have done throughout the years.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mairead McGuinness, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, thank you. I loved being in that chair and I love this Parliament and the fun and debates and the rows we've had, and I've been here 20 years, so I have some memory of that.

I just need to clarify something to my colleague at the very back of the Chamber. In Ireland we have a phrase, 'the poor relation'. I am McGuinness, not Guinness, so I qualify as the poor relation, but a happy one, that said.

Look, it's been a really frank debate and I'm glad that I'm here for it. I think everybody in this House really is passionate about solutions. You've all agreed on the problem and I think that's important. The word used was we agree on the diagnosis. We're losing influence. We're losing opportunities. We're losing companies. We're failing workers. We're were failing SMEs. It doesn't have to be that way.

And if I could say, MEP脗聽Boeselager, you very clearly said 芒聙聯 and I think it's a word we need to repeat 芒聙聯national egos are an issue. I mean, we had a bit of an exchange at the start of this debate, my Maltese colleague and I, I said, 'I'm Irish', but our nationality should not blind us to the reality that if we have fragmented capital markets, if we have national boundaries, we have less.

I really hope that you who are passionate about this topic can persuade the leaders of Member States, their finance ministers, that achieving a savings and investment union or capital markets union and banking union is about added value. I have said to the finance ministers on many occasions, please don't look at this as you're winning and I'm losing. This is not what it's about.

I would hope that our citizens and businesses would push us all a little bit harder because they are losing out as citizens. Their money is not earning what it should be. They're not investing for their future, for their pensions. The SME sector, large companies, they're not getting the capital they need in Europe and, therefore, Europe is losing out.

But on a day when the incoming Commission has been voted through, I salute you who put this debate on the agenda. I think it has to be constantly addressed and you have rather got to put pressure on the Member States to look at the big picture here and really, I said at the beginning, think and talk about the single market, not about cross芒聙聭border, because cross芒聙聭border is a killer.

My last word is again one of thanks and appreciation. Somebody asked me, what am I most proud of? And because I come from Ireland and a large family, we don't do pride because it comes before a fall. But I am proud that I survived and that I made good friends and we got some good work done. Thank you all for that and I wish you well for the future.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you very much, Commissioner McGuinness. I wish you all the best.

脗聽

15. Debates on cases of breaches of human rights, democracy and the rule of law (Rule 150) (debate)

15.1. Hong Kong, notably the cases of Jimmy Lai and the 45 activists recently convicted under the national security law

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache 脙录ber f脙录nf Entschlie脙聼ungsantr脙陇ge zu脗聽Hongkong, insbesondere den F脙陇llen von Jimmy Lai und den 45 Aktivisten, die vor Kurzem gem脙陇脙聼 dem Gesetz 脙录ber die nationale Sicherheit verurteilt wurden脗聽(2024/2950(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Miriam Lexmann, author. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, I want to thank all of you across the parties to support the people of Hong Kong and all those oppressed by the Chinese Communist Party.

I am sorry that once again, this evening, we must discuss the worsening oppression in the city. Jimmy Lai, and the 45 pro-democracy activists, do not belong in jail. They represent the best of Hong Kong: its spirit and its love of freedom and democracy.

Therefore, I want to call on the new European Commission 芒聙聯 especially the new High Representative 芒聙聯 to stand strong with the people of Hong Kong and to hold responsible those destroying the city's freedom and autonomy.

The evasion of EU sanctions through Hong Kong shows us why the city and its people must matter to us. It is both a moral obligation and a security necessity to stand with Hong Kong.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Chlo脙漏 Ridel, auteur. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, dix ans apr脙篓s ce qui a 脙漏t脙漏 surnomm脙漏 la 脗芦r脙漏volution des parapluies脗禄, 脙聽 Hong脗聽Kong en脗聽2014, un constat s'impose: la situation 脙聽 Hong Kong continue de se d脙漏grader, elle est catastrophique.

Pour les manifestants, les parapluies n'脙漏taient pas juste un geste po脙漏tique, c'脙漏tait une n脙漏cessit脙漏 pour leur permettre de se prot脙漏ger le visage des gaz lacrymog脙篓nes ou encore de la reconnaissance faciale, d'une police sur脙漏quip脙漏e et d脙漏cid脙漏e 脙聽 en d脙漏coudre.

La r脙漏solution d'urgence que nous examinons demande la lib脙漏ration imm脙漏diate de Jimmy脗聽Lai et des脗聽47 de Hong脗聽Kong, les militants parmi les personnalit脙漏s les plus connues de ce mouvement pro-d脙漏mocratie. Il y a huit femmes et 39 hommes, 脙垄g脙漏s de 14 脙聽 80脗聽ans.

Jimmy Lai et les脗聽47 de Hong脗聽Kong 脙漏copent de peines allant jusqu'脙聽 dix ans de prison simplement pour avoir manifest脙漏. La plupart ont 脙漏t脙漏 intimid脙漏s au point de plaider coupables et ils payent le prix fort pour avoir exprim脙漏 leur opinion et avoir os脙漏 s'opposer 脙聽 l'ogre chinois. Au total, ce sont des centaines de personnes arr脙陋t脙漏es, fich脙漏es, emprisonn脙漏es pour avoir d脙漏fendu un id脙漏al d脙漏mocratique.

Aujourd'hui, le constat est clair: la Chine n'a pas respect脙漏 la promesse de conserver un syst脙篓me d脙漏mocratique 脙聽 Hong脗聽Kong jusqu'en脗聽2047, promesse qui 脙漏tait au c脜聯ur de la r脙漏trocession de Hong脗聽Kong par le gouvernement britannique en脗聽1997. Et la Chine enferme ceux qui s'y opposent.

Il faut que l'Union europ脙漏enne se tienne et se tienne toujours aux c脙麓t脙漏s de ceux qui d脙漏fendent la libert脙漏 d'expression, la d脙漏mocratie, la libert脙漏 d'informer, de manifester et de s'exprimer, parce que ces manifestants ne se d脙漏fendent pas juste eux-m脙陋mes, mais d脙漏fendent 脙漏galement nos int脙漏r脙陋ts et ceux de tous les d脙漏mocrates 脙聽 travers le monde.

Nous devons donc affirmer haut et fort que la d脙漏cision chinoise d'imposer la loi de s脙漏curit脙漏 nationale sur Hong脗聽Kong est une violation du droit international et des droits humains, que les 脙聣tats membres de l'Union europ脙漏enne doivent 脙陋tre appel脙漏s 脙聽 porter la Chine devant la Cour internationale de justice et que des sanctions doivent 脙陋tre appliqu脙漏es contre les dirigeants chinois et ceux de Hong脗聽Kong.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mariusz Kami脜聞ski, autor. 芒聙聯 Szanowna Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca, Pani Komisarz! Komunistyczne Chiny 脜聜ami脛聟 mi脛聶dzynarodowe zobowi脛聟zania dotycz脛聟ce statusu Hongkongu i praw jego mieszka脜聞c脙鲁w. Do wi脛聶zie脜聞 trafiaj脛聟 osoby chc脛聟ce zrealizowa脛聡 swoje podstawowe prawa cz脜聜owieka: prawo do swobody gromadzenia si脛聶, wyznawania religii, do g脜聜oszenia pogl脛聟d脙鲁w. Symbol wolno脜聸ci s脜聜owa, Jimmy Lai, od czterech lat przebywa w areszcie, a jego proces jest fars脛聟.

Dzi脜聸 Hongkong za przyzwoleniem w脜聜adz chi脜聞skich szeroko otwiera si脛聶 dla firm obchodz脛聟cych sankcje na脜聜o脜录one na Rosj脛聶. Pekin musi zdawa脛聡 sobie spraw脛聶, 脜录e nasz g脜聜os wyra脜录any w tej debacie oraz w rezolucji, kt脙鲁r脛聟 jutro przyjmiemy, jest jasnym sygna脜聜em: prawa cz脜聜owieka nie s脛聟 kwesti脛聟 do negocjacji. Spo脜聜eczno脜聸脛聡 mi脛聶dzynarodowa nie mo脜录e milcze脛聡 wobec narusze脜聞, kt脙鲁re niszcz脛聟 fundamenty wolnego i demokratycznego 脜聸wiata. Jeste脜聸my i b脛聶dziemy solidarni ze wszystkimi mieszka脜聞cami Hongkongu prze脜聸ladowanymi przez chi脜聞ski re脜录im komunistyczny.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Erik Marquardt, Verfasser. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Frau Kommissarin! Wir haben ja in letzter Zeit immer wieder Diskussionen, auch in Europa, man d脙录rfe seine Meinung gar nicht mehr sagen. Man h脙陇tte gar keine richtige Demokratie in Europa, weil die Regierung irgendwas macht, was man nicht gut findet. Und ich finde, auch wenn wir heute 脙录ber die Situation in Hongkong reden, m脙录ssen wir uns immer wieder klarmachen, dass wir uns gl脙录cklich sch脙陇tzen k脙露nnen in der Europ脙陇ischen Union, dass wir hier die Freiheit haben, die Freiheit zu demonstrieren, gegen die Regierung, f脙录r Demokratie, uns einzusetzen f脙录r Pressefreiheit. Und der Rechtsstaat kann uns gar nichts, sondern er sch脙录tzt uns davor, dass wir Repressionen erleiden.

Ganz anders ist das in Hongkong. Ganz anders ist das in China, und deswegen, glaube ich, sollten wir dieses Gl脙录ck, dass wir hier leben, auch nutzen k脙露nnen und sollten es nutzen, um darauf aufmerksam zu machen, wie schlimm die Situation anderswo ist. Es ist richtig, dass wir die Freilassung von politischen Gefangenen in Hongkong fordern wie von脗聽Pui-kuen Chung als einem Journalisten, aber eben auch von Jimmy Lai als einem Demokratieaktivisten oder von den Hong Kong 47. Ich glaube, es ist aber auch wichtig, dass wir die Kommission und auch den Rat auffordern, sich daf脙录r einzusetzen, dass die Verantwortlichen endlich sanktioniert werden. Dazu z脙陇hlen John Lee, aber auch viele andere. Und ganz zum Schluss m脙露chte ich noch allen f脙录r die konstruktiven Verhandlungen danken, dem Berichterstatter, aber auch den Schattenberichterstatterinnen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hermann Tertsch, en nombre del Grupo PfE. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidente, resulta muy simb脙鲁lica la detenci脙鲁n arbitraria del periodista Jimmy Lai y de los cuarenta y cinco activistas que han sido condenados por la mal llamada Ley de Seguridad Nacional, como toda la represi脙鲁n en Hong Kong, porque a lo que nos lleva es a una conclusi脙鲁n: la Rep脙潞blica Popular China no es digna de confianza. Esa es la conclusi脙鲁n que extraemos de toda esta represi脙鲁n.

La Rep脙潞blica Popular China viola masivamente su acuerdo firmado en 1984, porque las libertades deber脙颅an seguir 芒聙聰con sus garant脙颅as芒聙聰 inalteradas hasta el a脙卤o 2047. Nada de eso est脙隆 pasando: han desaparecido las libertades y los derechos por completo.

La ruptura brutal de su contrato pone en cuesti脙鲁n todos los contratos de la Rep脙潞blica Popular China. La represi脙鲁n en Hong Kong es una muestra muy clara de que no cabe una relaci脙鲁n normalizada con quien pisotea los derechos humanos de sus ciudadanos, como tambi脙漏n los acuerdos contractuales.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Petras Au脜隆trevi脛聧ius, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, Communist China has completely engulfed the once芒聙聭democratic Hong Kong and drowned it into the Red swamp. China has broken all its international promises in the Hong Kong Handover Treaty and introduced a draconian, anti芒聙聭democratic system of coercion.

The persecution of Jimmy Lai illustrates Communist China's repressive role in Hong Kong, and is yet another example of its persecution of dissident and democratic opposition. We must continue to call for the unconditional release of Jimmy Lai and the other 45 activists recently sentenced.

Moreover, we must take urgent decisions to suspend Hong Kong's special status as a separate customs territory. We must retaliate against Hong Kong's involvement in helping Russia evade Western sanctions. Finally, we must suspend the extradition treaties with China and Hong Kong.
