脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, vuonna 1997 Hongkong siirtyi brittien hallinnasta Kiinalle. Samalla sovittiin, ett脙陇 Hongkong s脙陇ilytt脙陇脙陇 oman j脙陇rjestelm脙陇ns脙陇 erill脙陇脙陇n manner-Kiinasta viidenkymmenen vuoden ajan.

Kiina on r脙陇ike脙陇sti rikkonut t脙陇t脙陇 lupaustaan. Kes脙陇ll脙陇 2020 voimaan astuneen niin sanotun kansallisen turvallisuuden lain nojalla Kiina on h脙陇ik脙陇ilem脙陇tt脙露m脙陇sti k脙陇ynyt demokratia-aktivistien kimppuun. Kysy itselt脙陇si, kenelle demokratia n脙陇ytt脙陇ytyy uhkana. Sellaiselle, joka ei pysyisi vallassa, jos kansa saisi p脙陇脙陇tt脙陇脙陇. Siit脙陇 Kiinan kommunistisen puolueen toiminnassa lopulta on kyse, pelosta. Pelosta, ett脙陇 kansa huomaa, kuinka demokratian ja vapauden varaan rakentuu paljon parempia yhteiskuntia kuin mit脙陇 Kiinan kommunistisella puolueella on tarjota. Taloudellisen kasvun hyytyess脙陇 j脙陇ljelle j脙陇脙陇 vain synkk脙陇 diktatuuri. Sen todellinen luonne tulee viel脙陇 joku p脙陇iv脙陇 paljastumaan muillekin kuin nyt vangituille demokratian puolustajille.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Ende der spontanen Wortmeldungen)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, in June 2020, Beijing imposed a national security law on Hong Kong and since then we have witnessed a dramatic decline in the rights and freedoms of the people of Hong Kong.

Hundreds of opposition politicians, journalists and civil society activists have been arrested. Most independent media outlets were forced to close. Many civil society organisations were disbanded. The trials against Jimmy Lai and against the pro-democracy politicians and activists, known as the 'Hong Kong 47', are emblematic of the erosion of democracy and fundamental freedoms in Hong Kong.脗聽Both cases further undermine confidence in the rule of law 芒聙聯 a cornerstone of Hong Kong's uniqueness and attractiveness as an international business hub.

Last week, 45 of the Hong Kong 47 were sentenced to long prison terms, ranging from 4 to 10 years. All they had done was organise and participate in a primary election. Criminalising such peaceful and constitutional political activity constitutes a breach of Hong Kong's international commitments, and it is yet another blow to freedom of expression, peaceful assembly and association.

Jimmy Lai has been imprisoned for almost four years now. He is being prosecuted for his support for freedom of expression and democracy in Hong Kong. The independent newspaper, Apple Daily, that he founded was forced to shut down in 2021. This is a striking example of the clampdown on the free and independent press in Hong Kong.

The EU, through its office in Hong Kong and Member States' representations on the ground, has closely followed the trials against the Hong Kong 47 and Jimmy Lai, including by regularly attending the court hearings. And the EU has issued statements on both cases and raised its concerns in meetings with the Chinese and Hong Kong authorities.

You can rest assured that the EU will continue to closely monitor the situation in Hong Kong, in particular the application of national security legislation and its impact on the exercise of rights and freedoms. The EU will continue to call on China and Hong Kong to adhere to the Sino-British Joint Declaration, the Hong Kong Basic Law, respect international commitments and preserve the one country, two systems principle.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Die Aussprache ist geschlossen.

Die Abstimmung findet morgen, Donnerstag, den 28. November 2024, statt.

脗聽

15.2. The increasing and systematic repression of women in Iran
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache 脙录ber sieben Entschlie脙聼ungsantr脙陇ge zur zunehmenden und systematischen Unterdr脙录ckung von Frauen in Iran脗聽(2024/2951(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Mandl, author. 芒聙聯 Madam President, thank you very much, dear colleagues, the mullah regime in Iran was founded in 1979, quite as the European Parliament, which was founded the very same year.

Parliament will exist much longer than the mullah regime in Iran: that is what I am convinced of and what I strive for. Not to mention that I was born the very same year and also intend to live longer than this mullah regime, which is cruel against women in the first place, against everybody who is standing up for freedom, liberty, democracy, human dignity, rule of law, who is just a part of freedom movements such as 'Women, Life, Freedom' which are clearly mentioned in our resolution.

I thank the co-negotiators, I thank Hannah Neumann and Michael Gahler for putting the issue on the agenda, and I thank all the political groups for a nearly unanimous decision on nearly each and every point in this resolution.

The biggest threat for Islam is Islamism. Of course, Islamism part of the problem, but Islam is part of the religious world, and we protect freedom of religion while we fight Islamism, political Islam and the terrorism that results from it.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alessandra Moretti, autrice. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, in Iran il regime continua a intensificare la sua oppressione e le donne ne pagano il prezzo pi脙鹿 alto: cliniche per curare le donne che si rifiutano di indossare correttamente l'hijab. Cos脙卢 opera il governo iraniano! Li definiscono trattamenti scientifici e psicologici facoltativi ma sappiamo che c'脙篓 ben poco di facoltativo in queste misure.

脙聢 ci脙虏 che 脙篓 successo anche a Ahoo Daryaei, studentessa che dopo essersi spogliata in segno di protesta, 脙篓 stata etichettata come pazza, rinchiusa in un sospetto ospedale psichiatrico e, fortunatamente, rilasciata. Queste pratiche non solo violano i diritti umani, ma utilizzano la psichiatria come arma di repressione.

La vera follia, per脙虏, 脙篓 quella di un regime che reprime, tortura, uccide. La vera patologia 脙篓 anche quella delle Guardie rivoluzionarie che, come questo Parlamento ha chiesto pi脙鹿 volte, devono essere inserite nella lista delle organizzazioni terroristiche dell'Unione europea.

Non possiamo tollerare che la lotta per la libert脙聽 venga repressa e violentata, come il regime iraniano sta portando avanti. Ahoo Daryaei e tutte le donne iraniane meritano il nostro sostegno e meritano azioni concrete. Donna, vita, libert脙聽!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Abir Al-Sahlani, author. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, her name is Mahsa Jina Amini. Her name is Nasrin Sotoudeh. Her name is Ahoo Daryaei. Her name is the fight. The fight for every woman in Iran.

Two years ago, I stood here in the plenary trying to echo the message of these women, and two years later, I stand here once more. The situation is even worsening. Women are no longer only facing arbitrary detention, torture and abuse, but also declaration of insanity by the Iranian regime. Women and their right to peaceful, free and equal life is completely discredited, delegitimized and cast aside by the words of lesser men.

Women and their rights, when they fight for that, they are declared insane. These lesser men are afraid because by declaring these women insane, they are depriving them from the right to a just trial where their words would be a fight, a show, a proof of the struggle for the existential rights. They are afraid, these men, because two years later, despite all tools of repression, the struggle continues and the fire and the Iranian women's hearts remain alit.

Let us continue the fight. Let us continue to raise the voices of every woman in Iran, Farsi, Azeri, Kurdish, Bah脙隆脢录脙颅, Armenian, and make the Iranian regime understand that the women of the world will never stop until every woman in Iran is free. Jin, Jiyan, Azad脙庐. Woman, life, freedom. Now, tomorrow and as long as it takes.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Bielan, author. 芒聙聯 Madam President, the human rights situation in Iran is increasingly deteriorating. Compulsory hijab laws, backed by harsh penalties, is just one facet of the oppression. In every aspect of life 芒聙聯 marriage, divorce, custody and public presence 芒聙聯 women face systemic discrimination.

The regime weaponises its interpretation of Islamic law to restrict women's autonomy, criminalising their defiance. In its prisons, women and girls are systematically targeted with acts of sexual assault and rape 芒聙聯 methods of torture that reflect the regime's utter disregard for human dignity and rights.

The international community cannot remain passive. Today, we applaud the resilience of Iranian women and stand with them in their fight for justice. We commend the European Union's recent expansion of sanctions on Iran for its military support to Russia, and call for sanctions under the EU global human rights sanctions regime to be extended to all those responsible for human rights violations in Iran.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hannah Neumann, author. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Ahoo Daryaei, like so many women in Iran, was harassed by agents of the regime. They tore her clothes, claiming she was dressed too loosely, and to expose the absurdity of these actions Ahoo stood her ground in public in her underwear. The regime's response? They declared her mentally ill. Now they even plan to open clinics to treat women 芒聙聯 to 'treat' women 芒聙聯 who refuse to comply with hijab laws. This is not care, dear colleagues, this is repression disguised as medicine.

These tactics are not new. In 16th century Europe, women were called 'crazy' and burned as witches. Not because they were dangerous, but because they challenged misogynist power structures. The Inquisition was a tool of defamation and control, and so are the clinics of this regime.

But the only one who is sick here is the Islamic regime. Sick with paranoia, terrified of women who dare to show their hair or sing a song. And the only thing that Iranian women are suffering from is being sick of this regime 芒聙聯 a regime that crushes their freedom and denies their future.

Ahoo Daryaei is not a patient. She is the remedy, a symbol of courage and resistance against repression, like so many others, whose names we don't even know.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastiaan St脙露teler, auteur. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, beste vrouwen in Iran, jullie hebben het zwaar en jullie kunnen rekenen op de steun van The Patriots for Europe in jullie strijd voor vrijheid. Hoewel andere partijen het cordon sanitaire belangrijker vonden dan een eensgezinde verklaring van dit parlement ter ondersteuning van jullie penibele situatie, hebben de Patriots gewoon mee onderhandeld over de tekst en zijn onze suggesties ook gewoon overgenomen.

Voorzitter, ik citeer: "O Profeet, zeg tegen jouw vrouwen en dochters en tegen de vrouwen van de gelovigen dat zij een deel van hun bovenkleding over zich heen laten hangen. En zeg tegen de gelovige vrouwen dat zij hun blikken neerslaan en hun kuisheid bewaren. De mannen zijn de toezichthouders van de vrouwen, omdat Allah de 脙漏脙漏n boven de ander bevoorrecht heeft en omdat zij van hun eigendommen uitgeven aan de vrouwen. En wat betreft de vrouwen waarvan jullie ongehoorzaamheid vrezen: vermaant hen, negeert hen en slaat hen."

In soera脗聽4, aya脗聽11, staat over de verdeling van erfenissen dat de man recht heeft op een nalatenschap voor een deel dat twee keer zo groot is als dat van een vrouw. En soera脗聽2, aya脗聽282, schrijft met betrekking tot het strafrecht voor dat de getuigenis van een man evenveel waard is als die van twee vrouwen.

En dan hebben we het nog niet eens over de onterechte en neerbuigende aantijgingen over het gebrek aan intelligentie van vrouwen, zoals die in verschillende islamitische rechtsbronnen te lezen staat.

En nu zitten we hier met deze verschrikkelijke situatie van de vrouwen in Iran en is het wachten op een blauwe kaart van een van mijn collega's hier, om mij te vertellen dat die verschrikkelijke situatie van de vrouwen in Iran niets met de islam te maken heeft. Ik kan wachten, beste collega's.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 I would ask the next speaker, Rima Hassan, to take off the scarf, please.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rima Hassan, auteur. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, pour certains, la lutte f脙漏ministe consiste 脙聽 invisibiliser le sort de certaines femmes, comme les femmes palestiniennes, et 脙聽 traiter de mani脙篓re s脙漏lective et id脙漏ologique le sort d'autres femmes. C'est particuli脙篓rement vrai pour les femmes iraniennes.

La gauche s'est toujours battue pour le droit des femmes 脙聽 disposer librement de leur corps, 脙聽 choisir leur chemin de vie et 脙聽 garantir leurs droits fondamentaux. Mais, face 脙聽 cela, l'extr脙陋me droite et la droite tentent aujourd'hui de s'approprier la lutte f脙漏ministe sur fond d'obsession id脙漏ologique islamophobe. Nous ne sommes pas dupes.

L'hypocrisie europ脙漏enne se soucie bien peu du combat courageux et inspirant des femmes iraniennes qui sont trahies par ceux qui instrumentalisent leur lutte. Ce combat doit 脙陋tre men脙漏 au-del脙聽 des r脙漏cup脙漏rations politiques opportunistes. Nous devons exiger la lib脙漏ration imm脙漏diate de toutes les femmes incarc脙漏r脙漏es pour avoir refus脙漏 de porter le voile, sans s'attaquer 脙聽 celles qui le portent par choix. Nous devons soutenir tous les v脙漏ritables alli脙漏s de cette lutte pour les libert脙漏s, toutes les libert脙漏s, et accueillir les r脙漏fugi脙漏s iraniens et les r脙漏fugi脙漏es iraniennes pers脙漏cut脙漏s pour leur engagement.

L'Union europ脙漏enne ne cesse de perdre en cr脙漏dibilit脙漏 politique et diplomatique en se fourvoyant dans des doubles standards. 脗芦Femme, Vie, Libert脙漏脗禄 pour toutes les femmes opprim脙漏es!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Javier Zarzalejos, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, violence against women in Iran is state-sponsored and institutionalised.脗聽How many women have been executed? How many girls and women assaulted, beaten or lethally hurt?脗聽Women are the most powerful force for change. That is why the regime targets women: the ayatollahs fear them.

Maryam Akbari Monfared is one of the longest-held female political prisoners in Iran: she has been in prison for 15 years without one single day of leave. The regime's judiciary kept her in prison for an additional five years, and in January this year they condemned her to an additional three years on fabricated charges.脗聽Her crime? She was seeking justice for her three brothers and one sister executed in the early 80s during the massacre of political prisoners. A mother of three daughters, she was taken to Evin prison to, they say, provide some explanation at midnight, 29脗聽December. She never returned home.

From inside prison, she wrote: 'Finally, one day, I will sing the song of victory from the summit of the mountain, like the sun.' So be it.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Daniel Attard, f'isem il-grupp S&D. 芒聙聯 Sinjura President, meta mara fl-Iran tirrifjuta li tobdi l-li脛隆ijiet li jne脜录脜录g脛搂uha mid-dinjit脙聽, ir-re脛隆im isej脛搂ilha perikolu脜录a jew instabbli. Imma l-verit脙聽 hi li mhux in-nisa huma l-problema; mhux dawk li jirre脜录istu, imma s-sistema li tikkastigahom jekk ma jbaxxux rashom.

Din mhijiex biss il-脛隆lieda tag脛搂hom, hija 脛隆lieda tag脛搂na lkoll. Inutli nitkellmu fuq id-drittijiet tal-bniedem imma ma nag脛搂mlu xejn jew ma nag脛搂mlux bi脜录脜录ejjed biex niddefenduhom. Inkunu qed nittradixxu l-valuri li fuqhom twaqqfet l-Unjoni Ewropea. Irridu mmorru lil hinn mid-dikjarazzjonijiet. Irridu nsa脛搂脛搂u l-mekkani脜录mi li ji脜录guraw ir-responsabbilt脙聽. Irridu ni脜录guraw u nissieltu g脛搂all-脛隆ustizzja, u fuq kollox irridu naraw li nkunu fuq in-na脛搂a tas-sewwa, fuq in-na脛搂a ta' dawk li, b'kura脛隆脛隆, qed jopponu l-bi脜录a' u l-intimidazzjoni, fuq in-na脛搂a ta' dawk li wara kollox qed ji脛隆脛隆ieldu biss g脛搂al dak li kull bniedem g脛搂andu dritt g脛搂alih: il-脛搂ajja, il-libert脙聽 u l-ugwaljanza.

L-indifferenza hija kompli脛聥it脙聽. M'hemmx lok g脛搂al kompromess fuq id-drittijiet tal-bniedem. G脛搂andna d-dmir mhux biss b脛搂ala Ewroparlamentari imma b脛搂ala bnedmin li nkunu spalla ma' spalla mal-vittmi ta' din l-intimidazzjoni. Dan huwa test g脛搂alina lkoll: jew se nkunu fuq in-na脛搂a tal-脛隆ustizzja jew tal-indifferenza. G脛搂alija r-risposta hija 脛聥ara.

脗聽脗聽Margarita de la Pisa Carri脙鲁n, en nombre del Grupo PfE. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidente, se脙卤ora comisaria, se脙卤or脙颅as, la aplicaci脙鲁n de la ley de la shar脙颅a 芒聙聰base del r脙漏gimen islamista en Ir脙隆n芒聙聰 es una realidad que algunos aqu脙颅 no se atreven a condenar, dej脙隆ndola incluso fuera del texto. Ignorar el papel de la shar脙颅a distorsiona las causas fundamentales de la brutalidad ejercida contra las mujeres en Ir脙隆n. Les pido, por lo tanto, el apoyo a la enmienda del Grupo Patriotas por Europa en este sentido.

Reconocer las causas no es xenofobia, es esencial para salvar vidas, tanto en los pa脙颅ses de origen como aqu脙颅 en Europa.

Nos preocupa tambi脙漏n la pol脙颅tica de puertas abiertas que importa esto a Europa. Las mujeres de Ir脙隆n son muy claras: debemos considerar a la Guardia Revolucionaria Isl脙隆mica como una organizaci脙鲁n terrorista y abandonar las relaciones con el r脙漏gimen iran脙颅 y escuchar al pueblo. Que se las escuche a ellas, a las mujeres. Son las mujeres quienes, con gran valent脙颅a, est脙隆n empujando para que haya un cambio verdadero por la libertad y la democracia.

Este Parlamento debe abandonar su hipocres脙颅a y mostrarse unido frente a esta lacra. Es lamentable que por las exigencias de los Grupos Renew y Verts/ALE hayamos sido excluidos de la Resoluci脙鲁n com脙潞n. No estaremos al lado de las mujeres si no se llevan a cabo estas acciones.

脗聽脗聽Beatrice Timgren, f脙露r ECR gruppen. 芒聙聯 Herr talman! En g脙楼ng levde Irans kvinnor med frihet som vi i脗聽dag tar f脙露r givet; m脙露jligheten att kl脙陇 sig som man vill, studera och till och med demonstrera. Nu v脙陇rderas kvinnors r脙露st l脙陇gre 脙陇n m脙陇nnens i r脙陇ttsprocesser.

Samma islamism breder ut sig 脙陇ven h脙陇r. I脗聽st脙陇llet f脙露r att konfrontera hotet springer v脙陇nstern f脙露rtryckarnas 脙陇renden genom att tysta legitim kritik med anklagelser om islamofobi. Vi f脙楼r aldrig b脙露ja oss f脙露r islamisterna. V脙陇nta inte tills det 脙陇r f脙露r sent. Var inte naiva. Bek脙陇mpa islamistf脙露rtrycket med kraft. 脗聽脗聽Sophie Wilm脙篓s, au nom du groupe Renew. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, il y a deux ans, Mahsa脗聽J脙庐na脗聽Amini, 22脗聽ans, perdait la vie suite aux blessures inflig脙漏es par la police iranienne des m脜聯urs. La raison? Un hidjab mal port脙漏.

Il y a 脙聽 peine quelques semaines, ce sont les images d'Ahou脗聽Daryaei qui nous ont fortement 脙漏mus et qui symbolisent un 脙漏ni脙篓me acte de r脙漏sistance des femmes dans la R脙漏publique islamique d'Iran. Par son acte, Ahou脗聽Daryaei, et celles qui l'ont pr脙漏c脙漏d脙漏e, nous rappelle aussi 脙聽 notre devoir, 脙聽 nous, les Europ脙漏ens: celui de soutenir les femmes d'Iran qui d脙漏fendent leurs droits fondamentaux, trop souvent au p脙漏ril de leur vie, face 脙聽 un pouvoir r脙漏pressif et violent.

L'Europe doit renforcer sa pression sur le r脙漏gime de T脙漏h脙漏ran. Exigeons que le droit des femmes soit strictement respect脙漏 et que celles et ceux qui manifestent pacifiquement dans ce sens soient lib脙漏r脙漏s sur-le-champ. Soutenons de fa脙搂on plus appuy脙漏e la soci脙漏t脙漏 civile qui 脜聯uvre pour une soci脙漏t脙漏 iranienne plus juste et plus inclusive et, comme l'a d脙漏j脙聽 demand脙漏 le Parlement, qualifions enfin les gardiens de la r脙漏volution islamique pour ce qu'ils sont, une organisation terroriste.

脗聽脗聽Ingeborg Ter Laak (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, in elkaar geslagen, gemarteld en zelfs verkracht worden, puur en alleen omdat je als veertienjarig meisje je hoofddoek afdoet. Dat is de realiteit voor meisjes in Iran als ze opgepakt worden door de moraalpolitie. Sinds de protesten van twee jaar geleden en de dood van Mahsa Amini is de situatie voor vrouwen en demonstranten alleen maar slechter geworden. In Iran ben je als vrouw een tweederangsburger.

Wil je geen hidjab dragen? Wil je zelf je kleding kiezen? Dan loop je het risico om in elkaar geslagen te worden, in de gevangenis te belanden of zelfs de doodstraf te krijgen. Alleen dit jaar al zijn 27脗聽vrouwen ge脙芦xecuteerd om hun kledingkeuze. En de daders, de verkrachters, de mishandelaars, de onderdrukkers? Zij gaan vrijuit. Het geweld is ge脙炉nstitutionaliseerd. Vrouwen zijn in Iran tweederangsburgers. We moeten dit blijven aankaarten, want zolang vrouwen worden onderdrukt, is het onze plicht om te blijven strijden voor hun rechten, waar dan ook ter wereld.

脗聽脗聽Lina G脙隆lvez (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora comisaria, hoy nos convoca aqu脙颅 un tema urgente: el de la lucha de las mujeres y de las activistas en Ir脙隆n frente a un r脙漏gimen que, desde hace d脙漏cadas, ha hecho de la represi脙鲁n su principal herramienta de control, su propia esencia.

Desde las protestas por la tr脙隆gica muerte de Mahsa Jina Amini, en 2022, hemos sido testigos de un recrudecimiento alarmante de las violaciones de los derechos humanos: mujeres valientes, activistas y defensoras de los derechos humanos son los blancos principales de detenciones arbitrarias, tortura, ejecuciones... entre ellas, Narges Mohammadi, ganadora del Premio Nobel de la Paz de 2023, encarcelada en condiciones inhumanas.

Porque las llamadas polic脙颅as de la moral est脙隆n matando, silenciando, enterrando en vida a las mujeres y, frente a esto, no podemos callar, no podemos permanecer en silencio. Es nuestra responsabilidad hacerlo aqu脙颅, en Europa, y en todo el mundo, porque las mujeres de Ir脙隆n no est脙隆n solas: estamos con ellas.

脗聽脗聽Silvia Sardone (PfE). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, la repressione delle donne in Iran 脙篓 spietata: arresti arbitrari, violenze brutali e persino esecuzioni sono all'ordine del giorno. I diritti femminili vengono sistematicamente schiacciati a causa di motivazioni religiose legate all'Islam. 脙聢 fondamentale sottolineare che il motore di questo terrore 脙篓 l'Islam radicale e la diffusione della sharia.

Dispiace osservare che in quest'Aula la sinistra esita a riconoscerlo chiaramente: vedo i banchi della sinistra totalmente vuoti. Poi, non so, probabilmente a loro non interessa quello che sta succedendo alle donne in Iran.

Condannare il regime iraniano 脙篓 un passo necessario ma non 脙篓 sufficiente. Bisogna anche opporsi con fermezza all'imposizione del velo islamico, che rimane un simbolo di oppressione per le donne.

E le femministe: dove sono quelle che riempiono le piazze, spesso a proposito, per denunciare il patriarcato? Perch脙漏 non scendono in strada per alzare la voce contro certe comunit脙聽 islamiche che trattano le donne come oggetto? Perch脙漏 tacciono di fronte alle atrocit脙聽 subite dalle donne iraniane? Perch脙漏 sono assenti oggi in quest'Aula?

脗聽脗聽Bernard Guetta (Renew). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, g脙漏ographie, histoire et id脙漏ologie, tout oppose l'Iran et la Russie. Tout, sauf la similitude de leurs r脙漏gimes.

L'internement psychiatrique des opposants, c'est Yuri脗聽Andropov, le patron du KGB, qui l'avait invent脙漏, mais l'ayatollah脗聽Khamenei l'a pouss脙漏 脙聽 la perfection en faisant du refus de porter le voile une maladie du d脙漏voilement. Oui, une manifestation d'exhibitionnisme脗聽芒聙聯 il fallait y penser, vraiment.

Tout comme la r脙漏alit脙漏 du pouvoir appartenait en URSS 脙聽 l'appareil du Comit脙漏 central et non pas aux institutions 脙漏lues, elle n'appartient pas 脙聽 T脙漏h脙漏ran, aux institutions r脙漏publicaines, mais au clerg脙漏 chiite. Ces deux r脙漏gimes sont les m脙陋mes et le pr脙漏sident russe, guide supr脙陋me de la Russie, l'a si bien compris qu'il s'appuie d脙漏sormais sur les mollahs, comme il s'est toujours appuy脙漏 sur un patriarche orthodoxe dont la b脙漏n脙漏diction vaut de l'or.

脗聽脗聽Jessika Van Leeuwen (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, I'm here today to stand in solidarity with the courageous women of Iran in their efforts to break free from the chains of the Iranian regime.

Women journalists and activists are imprisoned, tortured, sexually abused and even executed simply because they are fighting for their rights. Iran is one of the few countries regularly executing women.

When I was born, my father's greatest wish for me was that I would never be dependent on a man. His wish was granted. Today, I stand here before you as an independent woman and a mother of four children, three of whom are girls. I wish the same independence and freedom for them.

Let us not forget the privilege we enjoy in this room: the ability to speak freely, to protest and to engage in activism without risking our lives.

But we cannot look away. We must stand with the brave women of Iran who are fighting for their freedom. This is for my courageous Iranian friend watching me right now, who stood up for her freedom so her daughter can grow up in freedom.

Women. Life. Freedom.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Evin Incir (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr President, 'Jin, Jiyan, Azad脙庐', 'Women. Life. Freedom': these three Kurdish words resonate deeply, instilling fear in the Iranian regime. The words embody the strength, the wrath and defiance of women across the whole of Iran. That is why the regime is so afraid. Women, girls and other groups in Iran need our support. We must strive to be at least a bit as brave as they are.

In September 2024, the Iranian regime approved the oppressive protection of the family through promoting the culture of hijab and chastity law. These disgraceful measures must not go unchallenged. We must impose sanctions on those responsible.

Colleagues, the Iranian regime is on edge and so is also the Turkish 芒聙聯 recently, the slogan 'Jin, Jiyan, Azad脙庐' was banned in Diyarbak脛卤r, my birthplace. This is unacceptable, especially as the EU Member States remain silent.

Let us be clear: our priority should be democracy, peace and adherence to international law. If we fail to defend these principles, we will face grave consequences. You cannot pet a snake and expect not to be bitten.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, Iran's regime is one of the most brutal on our planet. It kills and tortures its own citizens, with women most strongly persecuted.

The regime has been trying through all means to suppress the protests triggered by the death of the 22芒聙聭year芒聙聭old Mahsa Amini. In addition to arrests of numerous demonstrators, many death sentences were carried out in connection with nationwide protests.

The way Iranian authorities are dealing with the protesters, especially with young girls and women, is shocking and absolutely unacceptable. Although Iran is a UN member, has formally recognised the Universal Declaration of Human Rights, it is unfortunately among the five states in the world that most frequently apply the death penalty.

We must support the Iranian civil society and widen restrictive measures against the Islamic Republic of Iran.

F脙露r att regionen ska kunna 脙楼terf脙楼 sin stabilitet beh脙露ver mullorna falla. F脙露r kvinnornas skull och f脙露r hela Mellan脙露sterns skull.

Chcia脜聜bym, 脜录eby z tej sali wybrzmia脜聜 dzisiaj jeden przekaz, kt脙鲁ry jest parafraz脛聟 walki o prawa polskich kobiet: nigdy nie b脛聶dziecie sz脜聜y same. Wysoka Izbo! Zbyt d脜聜ugo ira脜聞ski re脜录im stosowa脜聜 przemoc, by uciszy脛聡 g脜聜osy kobiet domagaj脛聟cych si脛聶 godno脜聸ci, wolno脜聸ci i r脙鲁wno脜聸ci. Brutalne zab脙鲁jstwo Mahsy Amini nie by脜聜o odosobnionym incydentem. By脜聜o wynikiem dzia脜聜alno脜聸ci re脜录imu, kt脙鲁ry postrzega autonomi脛聶 kobiet jako zagro脜录enie. Od tego czasu niezliczona liczba kobiet zosta脜聜a pobita, zatrzymana i stracona za korzystanie ze swoich podstawowych praw. Te represje nie dziej脛聟 si脛聶 w pr脙鲁脜录ni. 脗聽脗聽Danu脜隆e Nerudov脙隆 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, imagine being beaten to death for how you wear your scarf. Imagine being thrown in prison for demanding your dignity. Imagine your voice silenced simply for wanting to live free.

This is the daily nightmare for countless women in Iran, and while they march in the streets risking their lives for freedom, we sit here year after year, passing resolutions. We condemn, we debate and yet their suffering does not end.

We must do more than just speak. We must act with all the power we have to stand with these women, to stand for the values we claim to protect. Because if we do nothing, their silence becomes our shame.

脗聽脗聽Davor Ivo Stier (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, kolegice i kolege, u tjednu kada obilje脜戮avamo Dan protiv nasilja protiv 脜戮ena posebno je va脜戮no okrenuti pogled na situaciju u Iranu.

Vlasti sve vi脜隆e ograni脛聧avaju prava 脜戮ena, uklju脛聧uju脛聡i kroz nove zakone i propise. 脜陆ene koje kr脜隆e zakone o hid脜戮abu pojavljuju脛聡i se u javnosti bez hid脜戮aba 脛聧esto su zlostavljane, zatvarane, mu脛聧ene, pa 脛聧ak i ubijene.

Istovremeno, vidimo da Saudijska Arabija ide u drugom smjeru. Postupno, ali ipak u smjeru ve脛聡ih sloboda i prava za 脜戮ene. I to moramo, dakle, uzeti u obzir u jednom 脜隆irem regionalnom aspektu.

Stoga, podr脜戮avaju脛聡i prava 脜戮ena u Iranu podr脜戮avamo ve脛聡u slobodu, stabilnost i prosperitet u cijeloj regiji 脜隆ireg Bliskog istoka.

脗聽脗聽Isabel Benjumea Benjumea (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, este debate coincide con el segundo aniversario del terrible asesinato de Jina Mahsa Amini, una joven kurda iran脙颅 de veintid脙鲁s脗聽a脙卤os, detenida en Teher脙隆n el 13 de septiembre de 2022 por 芒聙聰seg脙潞n el r脙漏gimen芒聙聰 violar las leyes sobre el uso del velo en Ir脙隆n. Simple y llanamente, por no querer llevar el velo. Tres脗聽d脙颅as despu脙漏s, falleci脙鲁 en el hospital debido a los abusos f脙颅sicos sufridos bajo supuesta custodia policial. Su muerte desencaden脙鲁 protestas masivas, lideradas por mujeres, en todo el pa脙颅s, unidas bajo el lema 脗芦Mujer, vida, libertad脗禄.

Mujeres que est脙隆n pidiendo algo que aqu脙颅 disfrutamos y que tal vez no valoramos: simple y llanamente, poder vivir en libertad y decidir la vida que quieren llevar.

Un r脙漏gimen asesino, que ha asesinado el a脙卤o pasado a 853脗聽personas con la pena de muerte, un r脙漏gimen que debe recibir m脙隆s sanciones y al que no podemos permitir que campe a sus anchas por Europa con representaci脙鲁n diplom脙隆tica.

脗聽脗聽Tiago Moreira de S脙隆 (PfE). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, sobre a revolta, lembrava Albert Camus que a liberdade 脙漏 esse nome terr脙颅vel inscrito na carruagem das tempestades. No Ir脙拢o, esse nome ecoa hoje na voz das mulheres que recusam o v脙漏u e, mesmo sob o a脙搂oite das chibatas e a escurid脙拢o dos c脙隆rceres, ousam dizer: 脗芦N脙鲁s somos a tempestade脗禄. O v脙漏u que lhes 脙漏 imposto oculta a carne, mas n脙拢o cobre a verdade que carregam, nem encobre a tempestade que os c脙漏us da P脙漏rsia, pesados da mais indom脙隆vel promessa jamais feita 脙聽 alma humana, j脙隆 anunciam. Elas s脙拢o a tempestade. Os opressores que n脙拢o compreendem a liberdade, porque n脙拢o compreendem nem a chegada das tempestades nem a determina脙搂脙拢o das mulheres, est脙拢o condenados a ser varridos por umas e por outras. Elas s脙拢o a tempestade. Um dia, o longo mas j脙隆 decr脙漏pito grilh脙拢o da teocracia iraniana ser脙隆 abalroado por uma tempestade persa com o nome de mulher. Porque na sua ess脙陋ncia, o Ir脙拢o ser脙隆 sempre persa e a mulher ser脙隆 sempre livre. Os c脙漏us da P脙漏rsia j脙隆 n脙拢o enganam e os v脙漏us do Ir脙拢o j脙隆 n脙拢o aguentam. Elas s脙拢o a tempestade.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, islamissa naiset ovat toisen luokan kansalaisia. Katsokaapa islamistisia valtioita, kuten Irania. Hallinto pakottaa naiset k脙陇ytt脙陇m脙陇脙陇n peitt脙陇v脙陇脙陇 huivia, hijabia. Jos n脙陇ytt脙陇ydyt julkisesti ilman huivia, voit joutua vangituksi, kidutetuksi tai tapetuksi.

Kun iranilaista naista hiljattain ep脙陇iltiin huivirikkomuksesta ja h脙陇n kohtasi viranomaiset, h脙陇n protestoi riisumalla muitakin vaatteita. Lopputulos: pid脙陇tys ja passitus psykiatriseen laitokseen. Kyseess脙陇 ei ole vain yksitt脙陇istapaus. Iranin hallitus on kertonut aikeistaan avata niin kutsuttuja hoitoklinikoita naisille, jotka eiv脙陇t tottele huivilakeja.

Kun Euroopassa on haluttu olla suvaitsevaisia kaikkia kohtaan, on suvaittu my脙露s t脙陇llaisten ajatusten vy脙露rymist脙陇 keskelle Eurooppaa. Se on ollut valtava virhe. Jos tulijalla on edes pienint脙陇k脙陇脙陇n ymm脙陇rryst脙陇 t脙陇llaista naisten sortoa kohtaan, silloin viestin pit脙陇isi olla selv脙陇: Eurooppaan ei ole sinulla asiaa.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Fin de las intervenciones con arreglo al procedimiento de solicitud incidental de uso de la palabra (脗芦catch the eye脗禄))

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members, the EU has a clear and strong position on the human rights situation in Iran, and in particular on the situation of women and girls. This House plays a crucial role as your debates and resolutions highlight the need to keep holding the Iranian authorities accountable for human rights violations, particularly regarding Iranian women and girls.

The European Parliament 2023 Sakharov Prize for Freedom of Thought was rightly awarded to Mahsa Amini and the Woman Life Freedom movement in Iran, as clear recognition of the protest movement. We stand by them and support their aspiration for a future where their universal human rights and fundamental freedoms are respected, protected and fulfilled.

The Mahsa Amini protests have exposed the systematic discrimination against women and girls by Iranian authorities and, since then, women and girls have been one of the main forces trying to push for real change in Iran's policies.

In this context, we will continue to monitor the situation of student Ahoo Daryaei, also now that she has returned to her family after being detained by the Iranian authorities.

Both publicly and privately, we continue to call on Iran to eliminate, in law and in practice, all forms of systemic discrimination against women and girls in public and private life, and to prevent and ensure protection for women and girls against sexual and gender芒聙聭based violence in all its forms.

Since 2011, we have sanctions in place to target those responsible for human rights violations under the Iran Human Rights sanctions regime. In response to the 2022 protests alone, we adopted ten sanctions packages.

Our commitment to the situation of women and girls in Iran extends to UN fora as well, where the EU is a strong advocate of accountability mechanisms.

The EU will continue to monitor very closely the situation of women and girls and stands ready to react to all issues of concern, including through sanctions. At the same time, we continue to use every opportunity in our direct bilateral contacts with the Iranian authorities to convey the EU position and solicit a change in state policies: this will continue to be zero tolerance for human rights violations.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽El presidente. 芒聙聯 Se cierra el debate.

La votaci脙鲁n tendr脙隆 lugar ma脙卤ana.

脗聽

15.3. The shrinking space for civil society in Cambodia, in particular the case of the labour rights organisation CENTRAL
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽El presidente. 芒聙聯 El punto siguiente en el orden del d脙颅a es el debate sobre seis脗聽propuestas de Resoluci脙鲁n relativas a la reducci脙鲁n del espacio para la sociedad civil en Camboya, en particular el caso de la organizaci脙鲁n de defensa de los derechos de los trabajadores CENTRAL (2024/2952(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebasti脙拢o Bugalho, Autor. 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, as rela脙搂脙碌es entre a Uni脙拢o Europeia e o Camboja remontam a 1997 e t脙陋m por base um acordo de coopera脙搂脙拢o que prev脙陋 a promo脙搂脙拢o da democracia e dos direitos humanos. Infelizmente, regista-se hoje um retrocesso not脙鲁rio, sobretudo desde 2017, que n脙鲁s devemos condenar e n脙拢o ignorar.

O caminho seguido pelo regime do Camboja obriga-nos a exigir aqui a liberta脙搂脙拢o de todos os presos pol脙颅ticos e a cessa脙搂脙拢o imediata da opress脙拢o das liberdades c脙颅vicas. Recordo, em particular, a organiza脙搂脙拢o cambojana central, especialmente perseguida depois de ter denunciado pr脙隆ticas que desrespeitam flagrantemente direitos laborais que esta Uni脙拢o Europeia defende aqui e no resto do mundo.

H脙隆 um prov脙漏rbio no Camboja que diz: 脗芦Se fores paciente no momento de raiva, poupar脙隆s a ti mesmo 100 dias de l脙隆grimas脗禄. As autoridades do Camboja deveriam seguir a sabedoria do seu povo e inverter este rumo e poupar tantas l脙隆grimas desnecess脙隆rias.

A Uni脙拢o tem de ser cada vez mais vigilante em casos como este e instar o Camboja a que cumpra, sem demora ou reserva, a letra e o esp脙颅rito de um acordo que nos aproxima, em vez de insistir neste caminho, que s脙鲁 nos distancia.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Francisco Assis, Autor. 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, a Europa 脙漏 o continente que melhor cuida dos direitos laborais e sindicais. Foram, como sabemos, direitos conquistados 脙聽 custa de sangue, suor e muitas l脙隆grimas. Mas hoje as democracias europeias beneficiam de produtos oriundos de pa脙颅ses onde esses direitos ainda n脙拢o est脙拢o consolidados ou est脙拢o mesmo sob ataque. E esse dilema, como se v脙陋 pelo caso do Camboja, tamb脙漏m se coloca em rela脙搂脙拢o a pa脙颅ses com os quais a Uni脙拢o Europeia firmou mesmo acordos de coopera脙搂脙拢o.

H脙隆 uma reflex脙拢o que 脙漏 preciso fazer desde j脙隆. O esquema de prefer脙陋ncias generalizadas da Uni脙拢o Europeia n脙拢o est脙隆 a ter o resultado que se esperava. A suspens脙拢o parcial desses benef脙颅cios, no caso do Camboja, n脙拢o surtiu, aparentemente, qualquer efeito. A persegui脙搂脙拢o e a pris脙拢o de sindicalistas e ativistas por raz脙碌es pol脙颅ticas s脙拢o a prova disso mesmo.

A Uni脙拢o Europeia tem de deixar claro ao governo do Camboja que n脙拢o pactua com a criminaliza脙搂脙拢o da luta dos cambojanos e com os ataques 脙聽 sociedade civil, recorrendo para isso a todos os mecanismos de que disp脙碌e, sejam as san脙搂脙碌es individuais, seja mesmo a suspens脙拢o total das tarifas preferenciais.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Catarina Vieira, author. 芒聙聯 Mr President, ever since Cambodian labour rights organisation CENTRAL published a critical report on freedom of association violations, they have been confronted with a government-led smear campaign. CENTRAL and its programme manager, Khun Tharo, are facing unfounded legal threats and harassment. We see the same pattern with Mother Nature Cambodia, a youth芒聙聭led organisation that exposes environmental crimes. Several of their members, most of which are students in their 20s, have been sentenced to prison, while others have been harassed to the point of leaving. They are not safe in their own country because they dare to speak out.

Cambodian civic space is shrinking rapidly, and the government is instrumentalising the judiciary and affiliated trade unions to silence opposition, human rights and environmental defenders, and journalists. We need targeted sanctions against those responsible for the political repression, and we must ensure that further engagement with the country is conditional upon the improvement of human rights and civil society freedoms. The EU must use its leverage to ensure that the Cambodian civil society is not silenced.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Isabel Serra S脙隆nchez, autora. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, el Gobierno de Camboya est脙隆 haciendo una persecuci脙鲁n pol脙颅tica contra los trabajadores y trabajadoras que se organizan por defender sus derechos, contra los sindicalistas, activistas y periodistas, contra la organizaci脙鲁n de defensa de los derechos de los trabajadores CENTRAL y su director.

Hoy pedimos desde este Parlamento que el Gobierno retire los cargos, la persecuci脙鲁n, la caza de brujas contra quienes defienden un pa脙颅s democr脙隆tico, una sociedad democr脙隆tica basada en los derechos laborales y en los derechos humanos. Tambi脙漏n pedimos que la Uni脙鲁n Europea ponga en marcha sanciones.

Pero, se脙卤or脙颅as, con esto no basta. La pregunta es: 脗驴qui脙漏nes se benefician de la vulneraci脙鲁n de los derechos laborales en Camboya? Porque muchos son due脙卤os de grandes empresas del textil, empresas europeas. Camboya es un pa脙颅s en el que trabaja un mill脙鲁n de personas en la industria textil (el 90脗聽% son mujeres en condiciones absolutamente inhumanas) y la Uni脙鲁n Europea es el segundo mercado para exportaciones de Camboya.

Marcas como H&M o Inditex 芒聙聰cuyo due脙卤o es uno de los hombres m脙隆s ricos del mundo芒聙聰 se aprovechan de esta persecuci脙鲁n del Gobierno de Camboya contra quienes defienden los derechos laborales. As脙颅 pues, pensamos que Europa debe tomar acciones contra las empresas europeas que se aprovechan de esa persecuci脙鲁n que todos decimos combatir.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marco Tarquinio, a nome del gruppo S&D. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signora Commissaria, difendere la libert脙聽 di associazione, i diritti dei lavoratori, i diritti umani essenziali, e agire a tutela dell'ambiente non pu脙虏 essere motivo di azioni penali o comunque pretesto per pressioni e attacchi sistematici tipici dei governi illiberali.

Accade, purtroppo, e continua ad accadere in Cambogia. L'UE non deve stare a guardare, mentre il governo di Phnom Penh stringe in una vera morsa la societ脙聽 civile.

Vanno perci脙虏 usati 芒聙聯 e mi attendo da Lei delle risposte a riguardo 芒聙聯 tutti gli strumenti a disposizione: politico-diplomatici, commerciali e fiscali, affinch脙漏 le autorit脙聽 cambogiane ritirino le accuse contro l'organizzazione sindacale umanitaria CENTRAL, accuse gravemente e palesemente infondate.

Fermino una volta per tutte gli attacchi contro gli attivisti delle organizzazioni civili, tra cui Equitable Cambodia, Madre Natura, impegnate per difendere nobili cause quali la difesa dell'ambiente, lo sviluppo sostenibile, la giustizia sociale.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members, human rights continue to be at the centre of the EU engagement with Cambodia. The decision taken by the Commission in 2020 to partially withdraw the Everything But Arms (EBA) trade preferences is a clear example of the EU commitment to the human rights agenda. As we stated at the time of the withdrawal in the GSP report of 2023 and in our political interactions since 2020, EBA preferences could be fully restored if there is a substantial improvement on the serious and systematic violations of the principles of the UN International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights that motivated the decision to withdraw.

Civil and political space in Cambodia continues to shrink. The most recent parliamentary elections in 2023 were conducted in a restricted political and civic space. Civil society organisations are subject to the restrictive law on associations and non-governmental organisations, which imposes cumbersome registration and reporting procedures. Shrinking civic space also featured prominently on the agenda of the last Universal Periodic Review for Cambodia in May 2024.

Cambodia needs to do much more to overcome the violations of labour rights, for instance stopping the ongoing harassment of trade union leaders. Cambodia must also urgently implement the ILO's fundamental Convention脗聽87 on freedom of association and Convention脗聽98 on collective bargaining and prohibition of anti-union discrimination.

The EU is closely following the situation of the labour rights group, the Center for Alliance of Labor and Human Rights. The EU Head of Delegation in Cambodia has been consistently raising these issues with his counterparts in the Cambodian administration, including this specific case. The EU also publicly reiterated its concern about harassment of non-governmental organisations and labour rights groups at the Human Rights Council in Geneva two months ago.

The agenda of the EU political dialogue with Cambodia reflects our preoccupations. In March 2024, Commissioner for International Partnerships Jutta Urpilainen visited Cambodia and met with representatives from civil society. The question of shrinking civil space also featured on the 12th EU芒聙聭Cambodia Joint Committee held in Brussels on 25脗聽April of this year. Substantial discussion covered, inter alia, civil and political, labour and women's rights.

The EU will continue to support human rights and democracy in Cambodia through political dialogue with the authorities and in multilateral fora. The EU will also continue to provide support through its cooperation instruments under bilateral cooperation programmes.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽El presidente. 芒聙聯 Se cierra el debate.

La votaci脙鲁n tendr脙隆 lugar ma脙卤ana.

脗聽

16. The arrest of the Franco-Algerian writer Boualem Sansal and the call for his immediate and unconditional release, and the repression of freedom of speech in Algeria (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽El presidente. 芒聙聯 El punto siguiente en el orden del d脙颅a es el debate a partir de las declaraciones del Consejo y de la Comisi脙鲁n sobre la detenci脙鲁n del escritor francoargelino Boualem Sansal, el llamamiento en favor de su liberaci脙鲁n inmediata e incondicional y la represi脙鲁n de la libertad de expresi脙鲁n en Argelia (2024/2963(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members, this debate shows the importance we attach to the rule of law and human rights, in particular freedom of expression, which are core values of the EU that we are committed to uphold inside the European Union and promote consistently and coherently with all our partners. Algeria is a long芒聙聭term partner and close neighbour of the EU, with whom we can and must address matters of concern openly and frankly, I would say.

The circumstances of the arrest and detention of the renowned writer Boualem Sansal raise many questions and concerns. Reportedly, he has been taken by the police at Algiers airport on the 16脗聽November, but his arrest was only confirmed 6 days later, and only yesterday he could meet a particular prosecutor who, according to his lawyer, charged him based on a terrorism芒聙聭related article of the Algerian Penal Code. If this accusation were based exclusively on opinions expressed publicly by Boualem Sansal, it would represent a clear attack against freedom of expression.

It is essential that the Algerian judicial authorities address all these issues, which are clearly related to the rule of law and respect for human rights, including the fair administration of justice. Human rights and fundamental freedoms, including freedom of expression, are in fact enshrined not only in international law, notably the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights, to which Algeria is a party, but also in the Algerian Constitution. These universal values are at the core of the relations between the EU and Algeria, as reflected in the Association Agreement and in our partnership's priorities.

This is why we continue to follow, discuss developments and raise concerns in these fields with our Algerian counterparts, in line with commitments from both sides. In particular, the EU regularly addresses human rights in the framework of the EU芒聙聭Algeria Association Agreement, notably in the political, security and human rights dialogue subcommittee and through continuous contacts with civil society representatives in Algiers and Brussels.

Algeria has been elected as a member of the UN Human Rights Council for the period 2023芒聙聭2025, and as with all members of the council, we expect Algeria to champion the respect, promotion and protection of human rights. I look forward to hearing your opinion and views on this important human rights issue that concerns a close neighbour and important partner of the EU.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fran脙搂ois-Xavier Bellamy, au nom du groupe PPE. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Boualem脗聽Sansal a 脙漏t脙漏 arr脙陋t脙漏 par le r脙漏gime alg脙漏rien pour une seule et unique raison: pour avoir os脙漏 parler, pour avoir os脙漏 脙漏crire.

Chers coll脙篓gues, si nous sommes r脙漏unis ce soir脗聽芒聙聯脗聽et je suis heureux, nous sommes heureux, que ce soit 脙聽 la demande de tous les groupes du Parlement europ脙漏en, montrant par l脙聽 le soutien de tous les pays et de tous les partis d'Europe脗聽芒聙聯, c'est d'abord pour dire notre immense inqui脙漏tude, car aujourd'hui encore, personne ne sait vraiment quel sort sera r脙漏serv脙漏 脙聽 cet immense 脙漏crivain et 脙聽 ce combattant de la libert脙漏.

Si nous sommes r脙漏unis ce soir, c'est pour dire脗聽芒聙聯 Madame la Commissaire, merci pour les mots que vous avez eus脗聽芒聙聯, que nous attendons maintenant l'action de l'Europe tout enti脙篓re pour sortir Boualem脗聽Sansal de cette situation terrible.

Nous avons des leviers pour agir. L'Alg脙漏rie re脙搂oit de l'Europe des fonds au titre de l'aide au d脙漏veloppement. L'Alg脙漏rie entretient avec beaucoup de nos pays, et la France au premier rang, des coop脙漏rations qui lui sont infiniment favorables et qui sont en particulier favorables 脙聽 ce r脙漏gime failli qui cherche 脙聽 exporter ses 脙漏checs plut脙麓t que de les r脙漏soudre. Je pense en particulier 脙聽 cet accord franco-alg脙漏rien sur les questions migratoires qu'il est temps enfin de d脙漏noncer, comme notre famille politique le demande depuis longtemps.

Quel immense aveu de faiblesse est-ce, pour un gouvernement, que d'emprisonner celui qui a os脙漏 seulement le critiquer! Quel immense aveu de faiblesse! Et que dire de ceux qui, depuis Paris, dans le confort des plateaux de t脙漏l脙漏vision o脙鹿 ils ne risquent rien, ont choisi de relayer les charges contre un 脙漏crivain qui ne peut m脙陋me pas se d脙漏fendre, qui ne peut m脙陋me pas leur r脙漏pondre?

Chers coll脙篓gues, ce cas nous concerne tous. Boualem脗聽Sansal est un 脙漏crivain de France. C'est un 脙漏crivain d'Europe, non pas seulement parce qu'il a demand脙漏 et obtenu la nationalit脙漏 fran脙搂aise, mais parce qu'il d脙漏fend cette libert脙漏 脙聽 laquelle l'Europe doit tenir si elle veut pouvoir survivre. Dans l'un de ses romans, il 脙漏crivait: 脗芦Si l'on ne croit plus 脙聽 la vie et 脙聽 la libert脙漏, il n'y a simplement plus de raison de vivre.脗禄

Il est temps de montrer, en lib脙漏rant Boualem脗聽Sansal, par tous les moyens que nous avons dans nos mains, que nous tenons encore 脙聽 cette id脙漏e de l'Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rapha脙芦l Glucksmann, au nom du groupe S&D. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, Madame la Commissaire, Boualem脗聽Sansal est en prison. Cette phrase absurde condamne 脙聽 elle seule le pouvoir qui l'enferme.

Quel est le crime de Boualem脗聽Sansal? Un crime terrible, un crime impardonnable: son crime est d'脙漏crire, de penser, de parler, de r脙陋ver en homme libre. Et c'est assez pour constituer, je cite, une 脗芦atteinte 脙聽 l'int脙漏grit脙漏 territoriale脗禄. Une trahison qui vous fait encourir, peut-脙陋tre, la perp脙漏tuit脙漏. 脙聙 quel point faut-il douter de soi pour avoir si peur des mots d'un 脙漏crivain de 75脗聽ans?

Le r脙漏gime alg脙漏rien qui l'embastille ressemble ce soir 脙聽 tous ces r脙漏gimes, 脙聽 travers l'histoire, qui ont pers脙漏cut脙漏, arr脙陋t脙漏, d脙漏port脙漏 les po脙篓tes dont les plumes et les 脙垄mes refusaient de se soumettre. Ceux qui font aujourd'hui, en France, le proc脙篓s intellectuel de l'脙漏crivain plut脙麓t que de porter le fer contre ses ge脙麓liers sont les dignes h脙漏ritiers de la cohorte de bureaucrates de la pens脙漏e, qui instruisaient jadis le proc脙篓s en r脙漏action des dissidents sovi脙漏tiques au lieu d'脙漏lever la voix contre leurs bourreaux.

Chers coll脙篓gues, ce soir, depuis le c脜聯ur de la d脙漏mocratie europ脙漏enne, nous condamnons sans r脙漏serve l'arrestation inique de Boualem脗聽Sansal. Nous exigeons sa lib脙漏ration imm脙漏diate et nous demandons instamment aux autorit脙漏s europ脙漏ennes qu'elles mettent tout en 脜聯uvre pour l'obtenir au plus vite.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Malika Sorel, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, Boualem脗聽Sansal est un fr脙篓re, un ami. Il n'a jamais ressenti une once de haine envers quiconque et encore moins envers l'Alg脙漏rie et les Alg脙漏riens qu'il aime profond脙漏ment. Ce qui lui arrive repose sur un terrible malentendu que nous devons r脙漏ussir 脙聽 lever.

Boualem est citoyen alg脙漏rien et citoyen fran脙搂ais. Nous lui devons assistance et protection et c'est pourquoi, d脙篓s le premier instant, nos plus hautes autorit脙漏s politiques et diplomatiques se sont pleinement mobilis脙漏es.

Son brillant parcours et son engagement intellectuel forcent le respect. Ing脙漏nieur de l'脙聣cole polytechnique d'Alger, il est laur脙漏at du Grand Prix du Roman de l'Acad脙漏mie fran脙搂aise ainsi que du prestigieux Prix des libraires allemands. Il est habit脙漏 d'une foi profonde en la capacit脙漏 de l'homme 脙聽 se d脙漏passer pour tendre la main 脙聽 tous les autres. C'est un esprit 脙漏clair脙漏, un v脙漏ritable humaniste.

Chers coll脙篓gues, ceux qui aujourd'hui tentent d'utiliser ce drame pour exprimer leur hostilit脙漏 envers l'Alg脙漏rie et les Alg脙漏riens desservent la cause de Boualem脗聽Sansal. Aussi, j'appelle chacun 脙聽 la retenue afin de ne pas entraver et compromettre les efforts qui sont actuellement d脙漏ploy脙漏s pour lui venir en aide.

Boualem, ici, au Parlement europ脙漏en, nous pensons tr脙篓s fort 脙聽 toi. Nous formons le v脜聯u que les autorit脙漏s politiques de l'Alg脙漏rie, cette si belle terre que j'ai connue, sauront pr脙麓ner la r脙漏conciliation et saisir la main qui leur est tendue pour b脙垄tir ensemble un avenir meilleur pour tous.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marion Mar脙漏chal, au nom du groupe ECR. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, l'脙漏crivain franco-alg脙漏rien Boualem脗聽Sansal est prisonnier parce qu'il a eu le courage de d脙漏noncer 脙聽 la fois le r脙漏gime d'Alger et l'islamisme en France et en Europe. Guid脙漏 par sa haine de la France, le r脙漏gime d'Alger se comporte comme un 脙聣tat voyou, pr脙陋t 脙聽 tout pour exercer une pression diplomatique. C'est en somme une prise d'otage qui ne dit pas son nom.

Nous devons donc avoir un message clair: lib脙漏rez imm脙漏diatement Boualem脗聽Sansal! Pendant qu'en Alg脙漏rie un 脙漏crivain est emprisonn脙漏, les hi脙漏rarques du r脙漏gime continuent d'envoyer leurs enfants faire leurs 脙漏tudes en France et de venir se soigner dans nos h脙麓pitaux. Jusqu'脙聽 quand cet affront?

Revenons donc 脙聽 de vieux usages diplomatiques et proposons 脙聽 l'Alg脙漏rie un 脙漏change de prisonniers. Il y a 3脗聽500脗聽vrais d脙漏linquants et criminels alg脙漏riens dans les prisons fran脙搂aises. Nous sommes pr脙陋ts 脙聽 les 脙漏changer contre l'innocent Boualem脗聽Sansal.

Celui-ci 脙漏crivait r脙漏cemment: 脗芦Le nom m脙陋me de notre pays, Alg脙漏rie, est devenu synonyme de terreur.脗禄 Qu'il ne soit pas tent脙漏 d'脙漏crire, depuis sa prison, que le nom m脙陋me de notre continent, Europe, est devenu synonyme de l脙垄chet脙漏. Il est donc temps que l'Union europ脙漏enne et tous ses partenaires utilisent tous les moyens diplomatiques et 脙漏conomiques pour obtenir la lib脙漏ration de Boualem脗聽Sansal.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Toussaint, au nom du groupe Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, c'est avec une indignation immense que je prends la parole aujourd'hui, au nom des 脙漏cologistes, pour d脙漏noncer l'arrestation de Boualem脗聽Sansal, cet homme de lettres d脙漏tenu pour avoir os脙漏 penser librement, pour avoir choisi la parole plut脙麓t que le silence de plomb exig脙漏 par un r脙漏gime aux abois. Nous demandons sa lib脙漏ration imm脙漏diate.

Demander la lib脙漏ration de Boualem脗聽Sansal, c'est d脙漏fendre une libert脙漏 essentielle: celle des 脙漏crivains, celle des penseurs, celle des cr脙漏ateurs. Cette libert脙漏 n'est pas conditionnelle. Elle ne d脙漏pend pas de savoir si nous partageons leurs id脙漏es ou non. La libert脙漏 d'un 脙漏crivain n'est jamais seulement une question individuelle; elle est le barom脙篓tre de la libert脙漏 d'un peuple. Quand on emprisonne un 脙漏crivain, on emprisonne toute une nation, car on la prive de sa pens脙漏e, de sa capacit脙漏 脙聽 r脙陋ver, 脙聽 questionner, 脙聽 脙漏voluer.

Au-del脙聽, donc, de la personne de Boualem脗聽Sansal, ce cri que nous lan脙搂ons aujourd'hui est un appel 脙聽 la libert脙漏 pour un peuple tout entier, le peuple alg脙漏rien, qui depuis des d脙漏cennies 脙漏touffe. Il 脙漏touffe sous le poids d'une g脙漏rontocratie qui s'accroche au pouvoir, sous un syst脙篓me verrouill脙漏 qui refuse d'entendre des aspirations l脙漏gitimes 脙聽 la d脙漏mocratie, 脙聽 la transparence et 脙聽 une vie meilleure. Alors, d'un seul et m脙陋me 脙漏lan, nous demandons libert脙漏 pour Boualem脗聽Sansal et libert脙漏 pour l'Alg脙漏rie tout enti脙篓re.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sarah Knafo, au nom du groupe ESN. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, il est des heures o脙鹿 il faut laisser la politique de c脙麓t脙漏 et nous ranger, sans arri脙篓re-pens脙漏e, du c脙麓t脙漏 de la libert脙漏, du talent et du courage.

Nous avons rarement l'occasion d'脙陋tre unanimes 脙聽 d脙漏fendre la m脙陋me cause, une cause qui ne m脙漏rite en r脙漏alit脙漏 aucun d脙漏bat, mais un vote 脙聽 l'unanimit脙漏. Cette occasion nous tend les bras aujourd'hui, car un grand 脙漏crivain vient d'脙陋tre arr脙陋t脙漏 en Alg脙漏rie. Ce grand 脙漏crivain, c'est Boualem脗聽Sansal. Il a re脙搂u le Grand Prix de la Francophonie et le Grand Prix du Roman de l'Acad脙漏mie fran脙搂aise. Il incarne la d脙漏nonciation du totalitarisme, la puissance de la litt脙漏rature et la foi en la v脙漏rit脙漏. C'est un intellectuel engag脙漏 qui combat ce que toute l'Europe devrait combattre. Et c'est cet homme que vient d'arr脙陋ter l'脙聣tat alg脙漏rien de mani脙篓re scandaleusement arbitraire.

Alors si l'Europe ne d脙漏fend pas Boualem脗聽Sansal, qui le fera? Ind脙漏pendamment de toute id脙漏ologie, en abandonnant nos divisions le temps d'un seul vote, nous aurons peut 脙陋tre le pouvoir de sauver la libert脙漏 et sans doute la vie d'un homme de 75脗聽ans qui a besoin de nous et dont nous avons cruellement besoin. Chers coll脙篓gues, je vous appelle de tout mon c脜聯ur 脙聽 exiger d'urgence la lib脙漏ration de Boualem脗聽Sansal.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nikos Papandreou (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, we have here a country that has all the possibilities of being a European country: a very high education/literacy rate, a powerful industry based on energy, very close to France 芒聙聯 but I would say a schizophrenic relationship with France. In the daytime it's the revolution, at night it's quoting Anatole脗聽France. So that relationship we have to exploit, we have to make sure that, given their power, given the ancientness of this government, they follow the rule of law that Europe proposes.

And I would like to remind those people there that in 1953, the original document of revolution was written by a writer, and today they put Sansal in jail, a writer. So go back to your values of 1953 and apply them today in 2024, and we will be with you.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mathilde Androu脙芦t (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, apr脙篓s des jours de rumeurs, le 22脗聽novembre dernier l'agence Alg脙漏rie Presse Service confirmait l'incarc脙漏ration de Boualem脗聽Sansal, 脙漏crivain franco-alg脙漏rien plusieurs fois r脙漏compens脙漏 par l'Acad脙漏mie fran脙搂aise, pour atteinte 脙聽 la s脙禄ret脙漏 de l'脙聣tat. 脙聙 ce titre, Boualem脗聽Sansal pourrait 脙陋tre condamn脙漏 脙聽 la r脙漏clusion criminelle 脙聽 perp脙漏tuit脙漏.

脙聙 ce jour, le flou le plus total r脙篓gne sur les conditions de son emprisonnement et sur ses droits, en rupture totale avec les conventions internationales sign脙漏es par l'Alg脙漏rie. La gauche morale se r脙漏v脙篓le 脙漏trangement molle face 脙聽 la d脙漏tention d'un homme dont le seul crime est de d脙漏noncer le r脙漏gime alg脙漏rien et l'islamisation des deux c脙麓t脙漏s de la M脙漏diterran脙漏e. Pire, 脙聽 la t脙漏l脙漏vision publique fran脙搂aise a 脙漏t脙漏 justifi脙漏e, ou du moins comprise, cette incarc脙漏ration, signe de la blessure du sentiment national alg脙漏rien par une France que le r脙漏gime qualifie sans honte de macronisto-sioniste.

Cette capitulation de la gauche, qui ne d脙漏fend plus les intellectuels par 脙漏lectoralisme banlieusard, serait presque le deuxi脙篓me tome du Serment des barbares de Sansal. Car oui, il s'agit de pratiques barbares: on ne jette pas au cachot un homme en raison de ses id脙漏es, les plus d脙漏plaisantes vous semblent-elles. Aussi, la France doit exiger de l'Alg脙漏rie la lib脙漏ration de Boualem脗聽Sansal, emprisonn脙漏 par haine de la libert脙漏 d'expression. Mais il est aussi du devoir de l'Union europ脙漏enne de soutenir les initiatives diplomatiques fran脙搂aises, y compris en proposant de suspendre le versement des millions d'euros pr脙漏vus dans le cadre du programme d'association UE-Alg脙漏rie.

Le chantage et la victimisation d'un pays qui n'a de cesse de fouler la dignit脙漏 fran脙搂aise ont assez dur脙漏. Le temps des colonies est fini, reprenons chacun notre ind脙漏pendance. Comme le dit si bien cette une de Charlie Hebdo: 脗芦Alg脙漏riens! Reprenez vos imams, rendez-nous vos 脙漏crivains!脗禄

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽El presidente. 芒聙聯 Les recuerdo a los diputados que no pueden mostrar elementos desde el atril.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Emma Rafowicz (S&D). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, en脗聽2006, dans une lettre 脙聽 ses compatriotes alg脙漏riens, Boualem脗聽Sansal d脙漏non脙搂ait d脙漏j脙聽 la violence des censeurs: 脗芦Tu n'as pas le droit de penser cela. Tu n'es pas un Alg脙漏rien, tu ne m脙漏rites pas d'exister. Dans une autre vie, ils ont d脙禄 脙陋tre la Sainte Inquisition.脗禄

Cette m脙陋me violence des censeurs, il en est aujourd'hui la victime. Cela fait maintenant sept jours, sept jours que l'脙漏crivain franco-alg脙漏rien est d脙漏tenu apr脙篓s son arrestation par les autorit脙漏s alg脙漏riennes. Mais quel crime motive cette arrestation? Quels d脙漏lits justifient sa d脙漏tention? Rien de plus que sa libert脙漏 de cr脙漏ation, son impertinence, sa plume acide, f脙漏roce, cinglante.

Nous ne pouvons pas rester sourds face 脙聽 l'arbitraire. Non, la place de Boualem脗聽Sansal n'est pas dans les ge脙麓les d'une dictature. C'est l'honneur de la France et de l'Europe de s'脙陋tre construites en opposition aux lettres de cachet et 脙聽 l'embastillement. L'esprit fran脙搂ais et europ脙漏en, ce sont les Lumi脙篓res et la libert脙漏 d'expression, c'est Voltaire qui attaque la monarchie absolue, c'est Alexandre脗聽Soljenitsyne qui d脙漏nonce l'archipel du goulag dans son ouvrage publi脙漏 脙聽 Paris, c'est Charlie Hebdo qui porte la caricature dans la plaie.

L'Union europ脙漏enne doit affirmer d'une seule et m脙陋me voix le caract脙篓re arbitraire et intol脙漏rable d'une telle arrestation. Nous, eurod脙漏put脙漏s, devons exiger la lib脙漏ration imm脙漏diate de Boualem脗聽Sansal.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

Solicitudes incidentales de uso de la palabra (脗芦catch the eye脗禄)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, tunnettu ranskalais-algerialainen kirjailija Boualem Sansal on siirretty tutkintavankeuteen Algeriassa. Mik脙陇 on h脙陇nen v脙陇itetty rikoksensa? Hy脙露kk脙陇ys kansallista turvallisuutta vastaan, sanovat viranomaiset.

Sansal tunnetaan vahvoista asenteistaan sek脙陇 autoritaarisuutta ett脙陇 islamismia vastaan. H脙陇nen islamkritiikkins脙陇 ei koske ainoastaan Algeriaa, vaan h脙陇n on my脙露s varoittanut islamisaation uhasta Euroopassa. Euroopassa t脙陇st脙陇 voi saada sakkoja. Algeriassa seuraukset voivat olla kovemmat.

Toivon syd脙陇mest脙陇ni, ett脙陇 Boualem Sansal p脙陇脙陇see pian vapauteen. Minun viestini sinulle Boualem on: Voit olla varma, ett脙陇 脙陇脙陇ni sananvapauden puolesta ja Euroopan islamisaatiota vastaan ei vaikene. Se kaikuu nyt t脙陇脙陇ll脙陇 Euroopan eduskunnassa.

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, this debate confirms the importance that the European Union gives to promoting human rights and the rule of law in its relations with partner countries. Rest assured that this will continue to be the case, in particular with close partners such as Algeria, and we will, of course, continue to closely follow the case of Boualem Sansal in full coordination with the French authorities. I recall that just a few weeks ago, journalist Ihsane El Kadi, in support of whom this assembly mobilised in May last year, was finally released. This is certainly a positive signal, and we hope that the handling of the case of Sansal will confirm this encouraging trajectory.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽El presidente. 芒聙聯 Se cierra el debate.

脗聽

17. Recommendation on smoke- and aerosol-free environments (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽El presidente. 芒聙聯 El punto siguiente en el orden del d脙颅a es el debate a partir de la declaraci脙鲁n de la Comisi脙鲁n sobre la Recomendaci脙鲁n del Consejo sobre los entornos libres de humo y aerosoles (2024/2911(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, it is now almost four years since we launched Europe's Beating Cancer Plan, one of the most ambitious and well-funded cancer plans in the world. As part of the key priorities of this plan and to protect the health of our citizens, in particular the young ones, we set the goal to create a tobacco-free generation in Europe by 2040. We still have a long way to go in reaching that target, of course.

Today, nearly a quarter of Europeans are smokers, and we lose nearly 700脗聽000 lives in the EU every year due to tobacco consumption. More than half of past and current smokers started smoking before the age of 19. This is why, in September this year, the Commission adopted the proposal for a Council recommendation on smoke- and aerosol-free environments. It broadens its scope to emerging products like electronic cigarettes and to key indoor and outdoor areas. The proposed recommendation aims to ensure that people, especially children, are protected from second-hand smoke and aerosols in key public places, like public transport and playgrounds. The World Health Organization clearly states that there is no safe or acceptable level of exposure to second-hand smoke. Second-hand emissions from electronic cigarettes also expose people nearby to particulate matter, toxicants and contaminants, whether they contain contain nicotine or not. In addition, second-hand emissions from heated tobacco products are associated with serious respiratory and cardiovascular problems.

The tobacco market has evolved significantly in recent years. On average, 30脗聽% of the EU population now uses electronic cigarettes and 2脗聽% use heated tobacco products. These products are increasing their market share, especially among young people, which constitutes a growing health threat. Our proposal responds to these developments.

We ran a Eurobarometer survey related to tobacco last year. Fourty-two per脗聽cent of respondents said they experienced people smoking in outdoor places intended for use by children and adolescents, and fourty-nine per脗聽cent say that they experienced people using emerging products in the same places. That is why we have proposed to reinforce the protection from second-hand smoke and aerosols in outdoor areas, including outdoor areas of hospitals and schools and children's playgrounds. Putting the recommended measures in place in practice will be essential to reinforce the protection of our citizens. We are fully committed to supporting Member States in their work to do so, with EU4Health funding, more research and evidence-gathering, training and best practice-sharing.

I am very happy that this House is also supporting our work by adopting a resolution on smoke- and aerosol-free environments with a high level of ambition. Together, we can make our European health union healthier and more resilient for the benefit of present and future generations.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Laurent Castillo, au nom du groupe PPE. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Madame la Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, huit脗聽millions de morts dans le monde chaque ann脙漏e, 75脗聽000 en France, c'est l'脙漏quivalent de villes comme La Rochelle ou Cannes, ray脙漏es de la carte chaque ann脙漏e. Voil脙聽 le bilan tragique du tabagisme, premi脙篓re cause de cancers 脙漏vitables.

Oui, il est de notre devoir de lutter contre le tabac en aidant ceux qui fument 脙聽 en sortir et en 脙漏vitant que de nouveaux consommateurs y entrent. Cr脙漏er des environnements sans tabac est une 脙漏tape d脙漏cisive dans ce combat, mais soyons clairs: les 脙聣tats membres sont libres dans leurs politiques anti-tabac. Il ne faudrait pas donner le sentiment que l'Union europ脙漏enne interdit de fumer dans des lieux comme les terrasses de caf脙漏s et de restaurants.

L'Union europ脙漏enne s'est fix脙漏 un objectif, celui d'atteindre une g脙漏n脙漏ration sans tabac d'ici脗聽2040. Pour y parvenir, nous devons soutenir la cr脙漏ation d'environnements sans tabac autour des 脙漏coles et des h脙麓pitaux pour prot脙漏ger notre jeunesse et nos malades. Une question se pose, celle de l'approche que nous devons adopter face aux nouveaux produits li脙漏s au tabac, comme la cigarette 脙漏lectronique. En tant que chirurgien et professeur de m脙漏decine, les 脙漏tudes scientifiques sont au c脜聯ur de ma r脙漏flexion. C'est pourquoi je souhaite que nous nous basions sur des preuves r脙漏elles, plut脙麓t que suppos脙漏es, avant toute r脙漏glementation. Les cigarettes 脙漏lectroniques doivent pouvoir aider ceux qui fument 脙聽 sortir du tabac sans devenir pour autant un gadget attrayant pour les plus jeunes et les non-fumeurs.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alessandra Moretti, a nome del gruppo S&D. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signora Commissaria, le zone protette dal fumo e dagli aerosol passivi sono essenziali per la lotta alle dipendenze, ai tumori, alle malattie non trasmissibili. Sono strumenti fondamentali per raggiungere gli obiettivi del Cancer Plan e per proteggere la salute pubblica, in particolare dei pi脙鹿 deboli, come i bambini e le donne in stato di gravidanza.

脙聢 fondamentale che il divieto di fumo sia esteso a tutti gli spazi aperti in cui si aggregano le persone, ma anche prevedere l'inclusione di nuovi prodotti da fumo, come sigarette elettroniche e tabacco riscaldato. Questi nuovi prodotti sono pubblicizzati come innocui dalle aziende che li producono, ovviamente, ma sono in realt脙聽 molto pericolosi per chi li usa ma anche per chi 脙篓 vicino. L'Organizzazione mondiale della sanit脙聽 ha chiarito la pericolosit脙聽 degli aerosol passivi con evidenze scientifiche.

Purtroppo molti colleghi di destra, e non solo, faticano ad accettare le evidenze scientifiche: propongono emendamenti pericolosi che negano le posizioni della scienza e presentano falsamente i nuovi prodotti come meno pericolosi. E questo 脙篓 inaccettabile.

Trovo molto preoccupante che ci siano parlamentari pronti a negare la scienza per sostenere interessi privati, di parte, interessi economici. Possiamo fare molti compromessi su molte cose: ma non sulla pelle e sulla salute delle persone.

Se volete essere presi sul serio nella lotta contro i tumori, allora non voltate le spalle alla scienza, ai medici. Assicurate l'approvazione di un testo che protegga la salute degli europei. Difendete la scienza e rifiutate le ingerenze delle lobby del fumo e del tabacco.

Il gruppo S&D sar脙聽 sempre dalla parte della scienza e dei nostri medici, dei pazienti, dei cittadini. Tutti coloro che ci chiedono di difendere la salute e proteggere i pi脙鹿 deboli, mi auguro che ci troverete sempre dalla parte ragionevole e che anche questo prevalga negli altri gruppi politici.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ond脜聶ej Knotek, on behalf of the PfE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, Madam Commissioner, this is indeed an interesting topic consisting of different elements that need to be put in balance.

First, we want to protect citizens from the effect of second芒聙聭hand smoke and aerosols and achieve the 2040 goal for a tobacco芒聙聭free generation. Second, we do not want to excessively interfere in the lives of Europeans with any ambitions to regulate their lives. Third, European institutions shall not go beyond the treaties and shall respect national competences and sovereignty. Fourth, we shall take science into consideration.

These are four basic elements concerning this topic, and I would like to invite you, dear colleagues, to consider them during tomorrow's vote on the resolution and related amendments.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pietro Fiocchi, a nome del gruppo ECR. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, allora, punto uno: come relatore ombra del Becker ci sono due concetti fondamentali: riduzione del rischio e supporto scientifico. Allora, se noi vogliamo vietare tutte queste cose qua, per脙虏, devono darmi il supporto scientifico perch脙漏 altrimenti non esiste.

Ricordiamoci molto bene che il vietare alcuni di questi comportamenti 芒聙聯 e ricordiamoci che l'uomo 脙篓 comunque legato alla caffeina e all'alcol, alla nicotina, a tutte queste robe qua 芒聙聯 non funziona. Per cui dobbiamo di nuovo tornare alla riduzione del rischio.

E il fatto di equiparare le alternative al fumo tradizionale al fumo tradizionale non va bene, perch脙漏 c'脙篓 stato un grosso studio scientifico sulle sostanze che vengono fuori dalla combustione, che sono cancerogene, che non ci sono negli altri metodi.

Per cui, ragazzi, pensiamoci bene, perch脙漏 脙篓 importante portare a casa anche una sola vita: ma 脙篓 riduzione del rischio.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ignazio Roberto Marino, a nome del gruppo Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, immaginiamo un mondo in cui l'aria che respiriamo non sia contaminata dal fumo o da aerosol nocivi. Non 脙篓 un sogno ma 脙篓 una urgente necessit脙聽.

Le persone esposte regolarmente al fumo passivo, sia esso da combustione di sigarette oppure da dispositivi elettronici 芒聙聯 e lo voglio ripetere davanti all'onorevole Fiocchi 芒聙聯 sono esposte, contro la loro volont脙聽, agli stessi rischi per la salute dei fumatori attivi. E parlo di cancro e malattie cardiovascolari, perch脙漏 la nicotina nell'aerosol determina malattie cardiovascolari.

Bambini e donne in gravidanza sono particolarmente vulnerabili e dobbiamo proteggerli con le nostre decisioni: altrimenti qual 脙篓 il nostro ruolo qui? Abbiamo il sostegno della societ脙聽 civile che, come spesso accade, 脙篓 pi脙鹿 avanti dei parlamenti.

I cittadini dell'Unione europea vogliono ambienti senza fumo. La stragrande maggioranza degli europei vuole uffici e ristoranti senza fumo e la maggioranza vuole anche i bar senza fumo.

Dobbiamo agire senza esitazione per creare un futuro pi脙鹿 sano per tutti. La salute pubblica, come ha accennato Alessandra Moretti prima di me, la salute pubblica di tutti deve prevalere sugli interessi economici dell'industria.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Catarina Martins, em nome do Grupo The Left. 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, o poder da ind脙潞stria do tabaco 脙漏 enorme. As suas mentiras s脙拢o respons脙隆veis por incont脙隆veis mortes prematuras e grav脙颅ssimos problemas de sa脙潞de. A sua interfer脙陋ncia no poder pol脙颅tico 脙漏 lend脙隆ria e, se deixarmos, tem mais poder que qualquer parlamento.

Como no passado, a ind脙潞stria do tabaco esconde hoje os perigos dos seus novos produtos. Tabaco aquecido, cigarros eletr脙鲁nicos e outros aeross脙鲁is t脙陋m composi脙搂脙碌es complexas que incluem subst脙垄ncias qu脙颅micas associadas a um vasto leque de doen脙搂as: cancro, doen脙搂as cardiovasculares e doen脙搂as respirat脙鲁rias cr脙鲁nicas. Escondem ainda que estes novos produtos tamb脙漏m prejudicam os fumadores secund脙隆rios. Situa脙搂脙拢o particularmente grave para as pessoas mais vulner脙隆veis: crian脙搂as, mulheres gr脙隆vidas, quem tem doen脙搂as cr脙鲁nicas.

A resolu脙搂脙拢o do Parlamento tem um objetivo: dizer 脙聽 Comiss脙拢o que este 脙漏 o momento de atualizar a legisla脙搂脙拢o, considerando os perigos que j脙隆 conhecemos. N脙拢o se trata de perseguir fumadores, mas de impor regras a uma ind脙潞stria muito interessada no lucro e que despreza a sa脙潞de p脙潞blica. Uma ind脙潞stria interessada em manter um manto de opacidade sobre tudo isto e que, como o debate no Parlamento mostrou, tem aqui, infelizmente, muitos aliados. 脙聙 esquerda, exigimos transpar脙陋ncia e a脙搂脙拢o para defender a popula脙搂脙拢o.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Christine Anderson, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Es wird immer besser: Jetzt will die EU sogar noch das Rauchen im Freien verbieten, etwa in Schwimmb脙陇dern und Freizeitparks, aber auch an Bushaltestellen oder in Bierg脙陇rten脗聽芒聙聯 und zwar 脙录berall in Europa. Sie hat daf脙录r zwar keine Kompetenz, an wissenschaftlicher Begr脙录ndung fehlt es ebenfalls. Aber der Regulierungswahn der EU kennt schlicht keine Grenzen mehr. Ihr paternalistischer Ansatz ist eine Entm脙录ndigung der B脙录rger und zeigt einmal mehr, welches Bild Sie vom B脙录rger haben. Sie betrachten die B脙录rger als Untertanen, als ungezogene Kinder, deren Leben Sie sich anma脙聼en, bis ins kleinste Detail zu bestimmen. Die B脙录rger sagen daher zu Recht: zum Teufel mit dieser EU. Ihre Denkweise ist nicht freiheitlich, sie ist autorit脙陇r und hat inzwischen deutlich totalit脙陇re Z脙录ge. Lassen Sie doch endlich die Leute in Ruhe!
